users-management: Users Management Overview The User Management section provides a clear overview of everyone who has access to your DHS account. From here, owners can review user details such as contact information, assigned roles, and crew memberships, as well as make updates when necessary. It’s the central place to keep user information current and ensure that each team member has the right level of access. From this section you can: Add new users with a role, contact information, and a password. Edit existing users to update their profile, change their role, or reassign them. Disable users who should no longer have access to the platform (without deleting their data). Enable previously disabled users to restore their access. Send password reset emails so users can set a new password. Search and filter users by name, email, or role. Manage profile pictures by uploading or removing avatar images. Important: Disabling a user prevents them from logging in but preserves all their historical data (past jobs, appointments, timesheets, etc.). You can re-enable a disabled user at any time to restore their access. Users List View The Users List View is the main view for managing all your users. To access it, navigate to Menu > Users . Tabs At the top of the list, a segmented control lets you switch between two views: Tab What It Shows Active Users All users whose accounts are currently enabled. These users can log in and access the platform. Disabled Users All users whose accounts have been disabled. These users cannot log in but their data is preserved. Controls and Filters Above the table you will find: A Search bar — type a keyword to filter users by name or email. A Role filter — a multi-select dropdown that lets you filter the list to show only users with specific roles (Admin, Support, Crew Member, Owner). You can select multiple roles at once. A New User button — click to open the add user form. Table Columns Column What It Shows User The user's avatar followed by their full name. This column is sortable. Click the user's name to open their profile in edit mode. Email Address The user's email address. This column is sortable. Role The user's assigned role is displayed as a colored tag:Owner (gray), Admin (green), Support (yellow), Crew Member (blue), this column is sortable. Crew The name of the first crew the user belongs to. Actions Action buttons for the user (see Row Actions below). Row Actions (Active Users Tab) Action Icon What It Does Edit Pencil icon Opens the user's profile in edit mode where you can change all their details. Disable User with X icon Opens a confirmation dialog to disable the user. If the user has active job assignments, this button is grayed out and disabled with the tooltip: "Cannot Disable User With Active Job Assignments." Row Actions (Disabled Users Tab) Action Icon What It Does Edit Pencil icon Opens the user's profile in edit mode. Enable Undo/restore icon Opens a confirmation dialog to re-enable the user so they can log in again. Sorting The data grid supports sorting by the following columns: Sort Option Description First Name Sort users alphabetically by first name (A-Z or Z-A). Email Sort users alphabetically by email address. Role Sort users by their assigned role. Created At Sort by when the user account was created. Updated At Sort by when the user profile was last modified. User Roles Every user in the system is assigned a role that determines what they can see and do. The four roles are: Role Tag Color Description Owner Gray The account owner. This role is automatically assigned to the person who created the account. There is typically one owner per account. Admin Green Full administrative access to the platform. Admins can manage users, crews, settings, and all business operations. This is the highest role that can be assigned to other users. Support Yellow Support staff role. Intended for office and dispatch personnel who manage scheduling, client communication, and day-to-day operations but may not need full admin access. Crew Member Blue Standard field worker role. Intended for technicians, installers, and other crew members who primarily work on jobs in the field. This is the default role when creating a user via Quick Add. 1. Adding a New User To add a new user to your account: Step 1: Click the Add User button at the top of the Users List View. A panel will slide open from the right side with the title Add New User . Step 2: Fill in the user's profile information. Add User Form Fields Field Required Description Profile Picture No Upload a profile image for the user. Click Upload Image to select a file. Accepted formats: any image file. Maximum size: 2 MB. results. If an image is uploaded, a Remove button appears to clear it. First Name Yes The user's first name. Must be between 2 and 50 characters and can only contain letters and spaces. Last Name Yes The user's last name. Same validation rules as First Name: 2-50 characters, letters and spaces only. Email Address Yes The user's email address. Must be a valid email format (maximum 254 characters). The system checks for duplicates when you leave the field — if the email is already registered, you will see: "This email is already registered. Please use a different email address." Phone Number Yes The user's phone number with a country selector. Defaults to the US country code. The system validates the phone format based on the selected country. Role Yes The user's role in the platform. Select from: Admin , Support , or Crew Member . (The Owner role cannot be assigned here.) Timezone Yes The user's timezone. Select from a full list of timezones. This affects how dates and times are displayed for this user. Description No An optional text description. Maximum 500 characters. The placeholder reads "Description (optional)." Use this for notes about the user's specialty, certifications, or responsibilities. Set Password Yes The initial password for the user's account. This password will be sent to the user's email. See password requirements below. Confirm Password Yes Retype the password to confirm. Must match the password entered above. Only appears after you start typing a password. Password Requirements The password must meet all of the following criteria: Minimum 10 characters long Maximum 128 characters long At least one uppercase letter (A-Z) At least one lowercase letter (a-z) At least one number (0-9) At least one special character (!@#$%^&*()_+-=[]{}|;:'",./<>?/~`) If the password does not meet these requirements, you will see the error: "Password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character." Tip: You can use the Generate button (star icon next to the password field) to automatically create a strong password that meets all requirements. Use the Copy button (clipboard icon) to copy the generated password so you can share it with the user. You will see a confirmation: "Password copied." A note below the password field reads: "The password will be sent to the recipient email." Step 3: Click Add User at the bottom of the panel to create the user account. On success, you will see the toast message: "User successfully created." The panel closes and the new user appears in the list. If creation fails, you will see: "Failed to create user." 2. Editing a User To edit an existing user, clic k the Edit (pencil icon) button on the user's row, or click the user's name in the table. A panel will slide open from the right side with the title Edit User. The edit form shows the same fields as the create form (Profile Picture, First Name, Last Name, Email, Phone, Role, Timezone, Description), pre-filled with the user's current information. The Set Password and Confirm Password fields are not shown in edit mode — instead, a password reset section appears. Password Reset Below the main form fields, a section titled Reset Password appears with the description: "Send a password reset link to the user's email address." Note: The password reset section is not shown when editing your own profile — it only appears when editing another user's profile. 2.1. Profile Picture Management Each user can have a profile picture (avatar) that appears throughout the platform — in the users list, crew member lists, appointment assignments, and more. 2.2. Uploading a Profile Picture Open the user's profile in add or edit mode. Click Upload Image next to the avatar placeholder. Select an image file from your computer. The image is displayed as a preview in the form. 2.3. Removing a Profile Picture The remove button appears next to the image. Click it to clear the avatar. The user's display will revert to showing the default avatar. 3. Disabling a User Disabling a user prevents them from logging into the platform while preserving all their historical data. This is the recommended approach when someone leaves your team or should temporarily lose access. There are two ways to disable a user: 3.1. From the Users List View Make sure you are on the Active Users tab. Click the Disable (user with X icon) button on the user's row. A confirmation dialog appears with the title Disable User and the message: "Are you sure you want to disable this user? This will prevent them from logging in and accessing the app." Click Disable User (red button) to confirm, or Cancel to go back. 3.2. From the Edit Panel Open the user's profile in edit mode. Click the Disable User button in the top-right corner of the panel. A black confirmation popover appears with the same message: "Are you sure you want to disable this user? This will prevent them from logging in and accessing the app." Click Disable User to confirm, or Cancel to dismiss. The user moves from the Active Users tab to the Disabled User s tab. Warning: You cannot disable a user who has active job assignments . If a user is currently assigned to an active job, the disable button will be grayed out and disabled. The tooltip reads: "Cannot Disable User With Active Job Assignments." 4. Enabling a Disabled User To restore access for a disabled user: 4.1. From the Users List View Switch to the Disabled Users tab. Click the Enable (undo icon) button on the user's row. A confirmation dialog appears with the title Enable User and the message: "Do you want to enable this user? They will restore app access." Click Enable User to confirm, or Cancel to go back. 4.2. From the Edit Panel Open the disabled user's profile in edit mode. Click the Enable User button in the top-right corner. A black confirmation popover appears with the message: "Are you sure you want to enable this user?" Click Enable User to confirm, or Cancel to dismiss. The button shows "Enabling User" while processing. On success: "User successfully enabled." The user moves from the Disabled Users tab back to the Active Users tab and can immediately log in again. Quick Add User You can create a user quickly from other sections of the platform (such as when adding members to a crew) using the Quick Add feature. The Quick Add modal is titled Quick Add New User and shows a simplified form with an informational note: "Quickly add a new user without leaving this page. You can complete all the details later." Quick Add Form Fields Field Description First Name The user's first name. Last Name The user's last name. Email Must be unique. The system checks for duplicates. Phone Phone number with country selector (defaults to US). Role Defaults to Crew Member. When creating from a Crew context, only Admin and Crew Member are available. Timezone The user's timezone. The Quick Add form does not include the description field, profile picture, or password — a password is automatically generated, and the user can set their own via email. The locale defaults to English. On success, you will see the toast message: "User created using Quick Add" with a View User button. Clicking View User navigates to the Users List View with the new user's edit panel already open, so you can add more details if needed. Searching and Filtering Search The search bar at the top of the Users List View lets you filter users by keyword. The search applies to user names and email addresses. As you type, the list updates automatically. Filter by Role The Role filter is a multi-select dropdown with four options: Owner Admin Support Crew Member Select one or more roles to see only users with those roles. This is useful for quickly finding all your admins or all your field crew members. When filters or search terms change, the pagination resets to page 1. Cross-Platform Usage Users appear throughout the entire DHS platform: Crews Users can be assigned as members of one or more crews. Within a crew, one user is designated as the Crew Lead . When viewing the Users List View, the Crew column shows which crews each user belongs to. Disabled users still appear in crew member lists but are marked as "(Disabled)" and cannot be set as crew lead. Appointments When scheduling appointments, crews (and their member users) are assigned to handle the work. The system uses crew availability settings to determine when team members are available for scheduling. Jobs A user's crew determines their job assignments. The system uses a flag to stop you from disabling users who are still working Timesheets Each user's work hours are tracked through timesheets. The timesheet system records when users clock in and out of jobs. Reports and Dashboard User data feeds into various reports including performance metrics, feedback ratings, and activity summaries. Users can be filtered in reports to see individual or team performance. Chat and Messages Users can communicate through the platform's messaging system. Each user's name and avatar appear in conversations and notification lists. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It View all active users Navigate to Menu > Users > Active Users tab View all disabled users Navigate to Menu > Users > Disabled Users tab Search for a user Type in the search bar at the top of the Users List View Filter by role Use the Role dropdown filter and select one or more roles Add a new user Click New User > fill in all required fields > set a password > click Add User Edit a user's profile Click the pencil icon or user name > make changes > click Apply Changes Change a user's role Open the user in edit mode > change the Role dropdown > click Apply Changes Upload a profile picture Open the user in add or edit mode > click Upload Image > select a file Remove a profile picture Open the user in edit mode > click Remove next to the current image Disable a user Click the disable icon on the user row (or in edit panel) > confirm in the dialog Enable a disabled user Switch to Disabled Users tab > click the enable icon > confirm in the dialog Send a password reset Open the user in edit mode > scroll to Reset Password > click Send Reset Email Generate a strong password When adding a user, click the star icon next to the password field Copy a generated password Click the clipboard icon next to the password field Quick-add a user from another section Use the Quick Add option in user selection dropdowns (for example, in Crews) See a user's crew assignments Look at the Crew column; hover the "+N" badge to see all crews
crews-management: Crews Management Overview Crews represent teams of users that work together on jobs and appointments. Instead of assigning individual technicians to every appointment or estimate, you can group users into a crew and assign the whole crew at once. Each crew has a name, a color for visual identification, and a designated Crew Lead who is the primary point of contact for that team. From this section you can: Create new crews with a name, color, description, and members. Edit existing crews to change their details, manage members, or reassign the crew lead. Delete crews that are no longer needed (only if not currently assigned to jobs or estimates). View availability for each crew, and jump directly to the Availability page to configure their schedule. View customer feedback and ratings for each crew. Search and filter crews by name or by assigned users. Important: A crew can only be deleted if it is not currently assigned to any job or estimate. If a crew is in use, the delete button will be disabled and a tooltip will read: "Cannot delete crew assigned to jobs or estimates." Crews List View The Crews List View is the main view for managing all your crews. To access it, navigate to Menu > Crews . At the top of the page you will see: A Search bar with the placeholder "Search crews" — type a keyword to filter crews by name. A Users filter — a multi-select dropdown that lets you filter the list to show only crews that include specific users as members. A New Crew button — click to open the create crew form. Table Columns Column What It Shows Crew The crew's name, preceded by a small colored dot that matches the crew's assigned color. This column is sortable — click the header to sort alphabetically (A-Z or Z-A). Description A truncated preview of the crew's description text. If the description is longer than the column width, it is cut off with an ellipsis. Rating The crew's average customer rating displayed as star icons (1 to 5 stars) with the numeric value. If the crew has no feedback yet, it shows "Not Rated." Members An avatar stack showing the crew's members. The crew lead appears first with a special border. Up to 2 additional active members are shown as avatars, and if there are more, a "+N" badge indicates how many additional members exist. Disabled users are excluded from this display. Actions Three action icons per row (see Row Actions below). Row Actions Each crew row has three action buttons on the right side: Action Icon What It Does Edit Pencil icon Opens the crew in edit mode where you can modify all details, manage members, view availability, and see customer feedback. Availability Calendar/clock icon Navigates to the Availability page with this crew pre-selected in Per Crew mode, so you can configure the crew's schedule. Delete Trash icon Opens a confirmation dialog to delete the crew. If the crew is currently assigned to jobs or estimates, this button is grayed out and disabled with a tooltip: "Cannot delete crew assigned to jobs or estimates." Sorting The data grid supports sorting by the following columns: Sort Option Description Name Sort crews alphabetically by name (A-Z or Z-A). Created At Sort by when the crew was created (oldest first or newest first). Updated At Sort by when the crew was last modified (oldest first or newest first). Pagination The list uses server-side pagination. By default, 10 crews are shown per page. You can change the page size to 10, 25, or 50 per page. The pagination controls at the bottom of the table show the current page, total pages, and total number of records. Changing any filter or search term resets the page back to page 1. Empty State If no crews match your search or filters, the table shows: "No crews found." 1. Creating a New Crew To create a new crew: Step 1: Click the New Crew button at the top of the Crews List View. A panel will slide open from the right side with the title Add New Crew . Step 2: Fill in the crew details. Create Form Fields Field Required Description Crew Name Yes The name of the crew (for example, "Team Alpha" or "Installation Crew A"). Color Yes A color that visually identifies this crew throughout the platform. Click the color swatch to open a color picker. This color appears as a dot next to the crew name in lists and in the scheduler. Description No An optional text description for the crew. This is a multi-line text area (3 rows). Use it to describe the crew's specialty, coverage area, or any other relevant notes. The label shows "Description (Optional)." Members Yes Select one or more users to add as members of this crew. Uses a multi-select dropdown labeled "Select Members." Only active (non-disabled) users appear in the dropdown. Selected members appear as tags below the dropdown. At least one member is required. Important: The first member you add to a new crew is automatically designated as the Crew Lead. A helper message below the Members field reads: "The first member added will be designated as the Crew Lead." The crew lead appears with a special icon on their tag. Step 3 : You can also create new users on the fly using the Quick Add feature within the Members dropdown. If a user doesn't exist yet, you can create them inline without leaving the crew form. Step 4: Click Add Crew at the bottom of the panel to create the crew. Editing a Crew To edit an existing crew, click the Edit (pencil icon) button on the crew's row, or click the crew name in the table. A panel will slide open from the right side with the title Edit Crew . The edit panel has the same fields as the create form (Crew Name, Color, Description) plus additional sections: Members Section (Edit Mode) In edit mode, the members section changes from a simple multi-select to a members table that gives you more control: Column What It Shows User The member's avatar and full name. If the user's account has been disabled, a "(Disabled)" label appears next to their name. Crew Lead A radio button. Only one member can be the crew lead at a time. Click the radio button next to a different member to reassign the lead role. Actions A delete button (trash icon) to remove this member from the crew. If there is only one member left, the delete button is disabled with the tooltip: "Cannot delete last crew member." To add a new member, use the Add Crew Member dropdown that appears to the right of the members table. Select a user from the dropdown to add them to the crew. Note: Disabled users are shown in the members table so you can see who was previously on the crew, but they cannot be set as crew lead and are excluded from the active member count shown in the Crews List View. Availability Section (Edit Mode) Below the members table, the edit panel shows the crew's current availability schedule as a read-only summary: General Availability — Lists the crew's enabled work days with their time ranges, each marked with a green dot (for example, "Monday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM"). Exceptions and Special Conditions — Lists any active exceptions (date ranges, months, or seasons), each marked with an orange dot. If no availability has been configured, it shows: "No availability data." Click View Availability Settings to navigate to the full Availability page with this crew pre-selected in Per Crew mode. Customer Feedback Section (Edit Mode) Below the availability section, the edit panel shows the crew's customer feedback: Displays the crew's average star rating (1 to 5 stars). Shows feedback grouped by crew member. Displays job-specific feedback with the latest description and average rating. If the crew has no feedback, this section is hidden. Saving Changes After making your changes, click Apply Changes at the bottom of the panel. This button is only enabled if you have actually modified something — if no changes have been detected, the button stays disabled. The button shows "Updating Crew..." while saving. On success, you will see: "Crew successfully updated." On failure: "Failed To Update Crew." Click Cancel to close the panel without saving any changes. Deleting a Crew To delete a crew: Step 1: Click the Delete (trash icon) button on the crew's row in the list. Step 2: A confirmation dialog appears with the title Delete and the message: "Are you sure you want to delete this crew?" Step 3: Click Delete to confirm, or Cancel to go back. Warning: Deletion is permanent. There is no archive or restore for crews. Once a crew is deleted, it is gone. However, historical records that referenced this crew (such as past jobs or appointments) will retain the crew information. Note: If the crew is currently assigned to active jobs or estimates, the delete button is disabled and grayed out. The tooltip reads: "Cannot delete crew assigned to jobs or estimates." You must first remove the crew from those jobs or estimates before you can delete it. Quick Add Crew You can create a crew quickly from other sections of the platform (such as when assigning a crew to an appointment) using the Quick Add feature. The Quick Add modal has a simplified form: Quick Add New Crew -> Field Required Description Crew Name Yes The name of the crew. Crew Members Yes Select members for the crew. On success, you will see the toast message: "Crew created using Quick Add" with a View Crew button that navigates to the crew's edit panel if you want to add more details. Searching and Filtering Search The search bar at the top of the Crews List View filters crews by name. Type any keyword and the list updates automatically (with a slight delay for performance). The search is not case-sensitive. Filter by Users The Users filter is a multi-select dropdown that shows all users in your account. Select one or more users to see only crews that include those users as members. This is useful when you need to find which crews a specific person belongs to. Cross-Platform Usage Crews are used throughout the DHS platform in several important ways: Appointments When creating or editing an appointment, you can assign a crew to handle the work. The appointment form includes a crew selection dropdown where you can: Select an existing crew from the dropdown (crews are shown with their color indicator). Quick-add a new crew if the one you need doesn't exist yet. The system checks the assigned crew's availability when scheduling the appointment. If the appointment falls outside the crew's available hours or during an exception period, the system will flag it. Jobs Crews can be assigned to jobs. The system tracks which crews are assigned to active jobs, which is what determines whether a crew can be deleted. The platform can also detect schedule conflicts — if a crew is already assigned to a job during a given time range, the system can warn you before double-booking. Estimates When creating an estimate, you can assign a crew. If the estimate is linked to an existing appointment or job, the crew may be auto-filled from that related record. Availability Each crew can have its own custom availability schedule (work days, time slots, and exceptions) separate from the global business availability. See the Availability Management section of this manual for full details on configuring per-crew schedules. Dashboard and Reports Crew data feeds into several reporting areas: Crew ratings and performance — Average customer ratings per crew. Crew satisfaction metrics — Trends in customer feedback. Crew-wise complaint distribution — Which crews receive the most complaints. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It View all crews Navigate to Menu > Crews Search for a crew Type in the search bar at the top of the Crews List View Filter by team member Use the Users dropdown filter and select one or more users Create a new crew Click New Crew > fill in name, color, description, members > click Add Crew Edit a crew Click the pencil icon or crew name > make changes > click Apply Changes Change the crew lead Open the crew in edit mode > in the Members table, click the radio button next to the new lead Add a member to a crew Open the crew in edit mode > use the Add Crew Member dropdown Remove a member from a crew Open the crew in edit mode > click the trash icon next to the member (not available for the last member) View a crew's availability Click the Availability icon in the row, or open the crew in edit mode and look at the Availability section Configure a crew's schedule Click the Availability icon or View Availability Settings in the edit panel to go to the Availability page in Per Crew mode View customer feedback Open the crew in edit mode and scroll to the Customer Feedback section Delete a crew Click the trash icon on the crew row > confirm in the dialog (only if the crew is not in use) Quick-add a crew from another section Use the Quick Add option in crew selection dropdowns (for example, in Appointments) Sort crews Click the Crew column header to sort by name, or use the sort controls for Created At / Updated At
clients-management: Clients Management Overview The Clients Management section is where you manage all your client accounts, their properties, contact information, and billing details. A client represents a customer you do business with — they can be residential homeowners or commercial businesses. Each client can have multiple properties, and all appointments, estimates, jobs, and invoices are linked to client accounts. From this section you can: Add new clients with a multi-step wizard covering personal details, property, billing, and attachments. Edit existing clients to update their contact information, properties, or billing addresses. Archive clients you no longer work with (without losing historical data). Restore archived clients if you need them again. Search and filter by keyword, city, state, tags, and client type. Import clients in bulk via CSV files. Export your client list to CSV for external use. Manage properties — add, edit, or delete properties for each client. View related records — see all appointments, estimates, jobs, and invoices linked to a client. Communicate with clients through the built-in messaging system. Manage attachments for both clients and their properties. Important: Clients cannot be permanently deleted — they can only be archived . Archiving preserves all historical data (appointments, estimates, jobs, invoices) while hiding the client from active views. You can restore an archived client at any time. Clients List View The Clients List View is the main view for managing all your clients. To access it, navigate to Menu > Clients . When you open the Clients List View, you will see a table listing your clients. Each row represents one client account and displays the following information: Column What It Means User The client's avatar, primary user name and company name as a subtitle if available. If the client was created via Quick Add and has incomplete information, a red warning icon appears with a tooltip: "Created via Quick Add - details incomplete" . Type The client's primary property type, shown with a colored dot: blue dot + "Residential" for residential clients, or orange dot + "Business" for commercial clients. Phone The primary user's phone number, formatted for display. Email The primary user's email address or the client account email. Truncated with an ellipsis if too long, with the full email visible on hover. Address The primary property's address (street, city, state, ZIP). Falls back to the billing address if no property exists. If the client has multiple properties, a tag showing +N appears (e.g., "+2") with a tooltip showing the breakdown by type (e.g., "1 Residential, 2 Business"). Tags Color-coded tag chips assigned to the client. Shows "—" if no tags are assigned. Actions An edit button (pencil icon) to open the client detail page, and a three-dot menu with additional actions. Tabs At the top of the list you will see four tabs that filter clients by type: All — Shows all active clients, regardless of type. This is the default view. Residential — Shows only active clients whose primary property type is Residential. Business — Shows only active clients whose primary property type is Commercial/Business. Archived — Shows clients that have been archived. You can switch between these tabs at any time. The table updates to reflect the selected filter. Sorting the List You can sort the clients list by clicking on the column headers for User (sorts by company name) or Type (sorts by account type). Click once to sort in ascending order and click again to sort in descending order. Searching and Filtering The Clients List View provides several tools to find clients quickly: Search bar — Type a keyword to search across client names, email addresses, company names, and more. Results update in real time. Cities filter — A multi-select dropdown populated with all cities from your client properties. Select one or more cities to show only clients in those locations. States filter — A multi-select dropdown populated with all states. Select one or more states to narrow the results. Tags filter — A multi-select dropdown with all tags used across clients. Select tags to show only clients with those tags. The filters can be combined — selecting "New York" in States AND "HVAC" in Tags will show only clients in New York who also have the "HVAC" tag. Row Actions Menu Clicking the three-dot menu (⋮) on any row reveals the following actions: Start a Conversation — Opens the Inbox with this client pre-selected. If a direct chat already exists, it opens the existing conversation. If not, a new chat is created. Attachments — Opens the Client Attachments panel to manage files associated with this client. Archive (active clients only) — Opens a confirmation dialog: "Are you sure you want to archive [client name]?" . Archiving removes the client from active tabs but preserves all data. Restore (archived clients only) — Opens a confirmation dialog: "Are you sure you want to restore [client name]?" . Restoring moves the client back to the active tabs. You can also click anywhere on a row to navigate to that client's Detail Page. 1. Adding a New Client To add a new client to your account, follow these steps: Step 1: Open the New Client Form Click the "+ New Client" button (blue) located in the top-right corner of the Clients List View. A drawer panel will slide open from the right side with a five-step wizard. Tip: If the list is empty, you will also see a "New Client" button in the center of the page. Step 2: General Information (Step 1 of 5) First, select the client type by clicking one of the two buttons at the top: Residential (blue, person icon) — For individual homeowners. Business (blue, briefcase icon) — For companies and commercial clients. Then fill in the following fields: Field Required? Description First Name Yes The client's first name. Last Name Yes The client's last name. Same validation as first name. Phone Number Yes The client's phone number with a country selector (defaults to US). Validated using country-specific phone format rules. Email Address Yes The client's email. Must be a valid email format. The system checks if the email is already in use — if so, an error appears. Company Name No (Business only) The company or business name. Only visible when "Business" type is selected. Website No (Business only) The company website. Only visible when "Business" type is selected. Tags No Type a tag name and press Enter to add it. You can add as many tags as needed. Tags help with filtering and organizing clients. Click "Next" to proceed. All required fields are validated before moving forward. Step 3: Property Details (Step 2 of 5) This step collects the client's primary property information. Property Image (Optional) At the top of the form, you can upload a photo of the property: Drag and drop an image into the upload area, or click to browse. Accepted formats: JPEG, JPG, PNG, WebP. Maximum file size: 4 MB. Once uploaded, a preview appears with a remove button. Property Fields Field Required? Description Property Type Yes Select "Residential" or "Business" from the dropdown. Address Line 1 Yes The street address. Address Line 2 No Apartment, suite, unit, etc. City Yes The city name. Timezone No Select the property's timezone from the dropdown. State Yes Select the state from a dropdown of all US states. Zip Code Yes Must be a valid 5-digit ZIP code or ZIP+4 format (e.g., 10001 or 10001-1234 ). Address Notes No Any additional notes about the property location (e.g., "Gate code: 1234"). Click "Next" to proceed after filling in the required address fields. Step 4: Billing Details (Step 3 of 5) This step collects billing address information for invoices. At the top of the form, a checkbox option reads: "Billing Address Same as Property Address" . When checked, all billing fields are automatically filled with the property address from the previous step. When unchecked, you must enter the billing address manually. Field Required? Description Billing Address Line 1 Yes The billing street address. Billing Address Line 2 No Apartment, suite, unit, etc. Billing City Yes The billing city name. Billing State Yes Select from a dropdown of all US states. Billing Zip Code Yes Must be a valid 5-digit or ZIP+4 format. Click "Next" to proceed. Step 5: Attachments (Step 4 of 5) This step allows you to upload files for the client and/or the property. Two tabs are available: Client Attachments — Files associated with the client account (contracts, agreements, IDs, etc.). Property Attachments — Files associated with the property (photos, floor plans, permits, etc.). Each tab has an independent upload area where you can: Drag and drop files into the dashed border area, or click to browse. Each file shows a progress bar during upload. Uploaded files display the file name, size, and a delete button. If an upload fails, a "Retry" button appears. Note: Attachments are optional. You can skip this step and add attachments later from the Client Detail Page. Click "Next" to proceed. Step 6: Review (Step 5 of 5) The review step shows a summary of all the information you entered: Property Image — A large preview of the uploaded property photo. Client Information — Name, type (with colored dot), phone, email, company name, website, and full property address. State codes are displayed as full names (e.g., "NY" shows as "New York"). Review the information carefully, then click "Create Client" to submit. Tip: You can click any step indicator at the top of the wizard to jump back and make changes before submitting. In create mode, you can only go back to previously completed steps. 2. Client Detail Page The Client Detail Page shows all information about a single client and their related records. To access it, click on any client's row or edit icon in the Clients List View. Header Area The header displays: Back button — Returns to the Clients List View. Client name — The company name (or "Unknown Client" if none). Status badge — "Active" (green) if the client is not archived, or "Archived" (gray) if archived. Start a Conversation button — Opens the Inbox with this client pre-selected for messaging. Three-dot menu — Contains: Attachments, Archive (or Restore if archived). Sidebar The right sidebar displays: Company name and property type tags (Residential/Business colored dots). Phone — Primary user's phone number. Email Address — Client account email. Billing Address — Full formatted billing address (or "—" if none). Tags — All assigned tags as colored chips. Edit button — Opens the Edit Client modal with all data pre-filled. Notes section — Internal notes with the ability to create, edit, and attach files. 2.1 Properties Tab The Properties tab shows all properties associated with this client. A count badge in the tab header shows the number of active properties. At the top, a "+ New Property" button allows adding additional properties. Each property is displayed as a card showing: Property type badge — "Residential" or "Commercial". Property image — The uploaded photo or a placeholder. Full address — Street, city, state, and ZIP. Quick Add indicator — A warning icon if the property was created via Quick Add and has incomplete data. "View on Map" link — Opens the property location in the Map view. Action buttons — Attachments (paperclip icon), Edit (pencil icon), Delete (trash icon). If no properties exist, the message "No Properties" is displayed. 2.2 Appointments Tab Shows all appointments associated with this client. A count badge in the tab header shows the number of appointments (with a loading skeleton while fetching). Each appointment is listed and clickable — clicking navigates to the Appointment Detail Page. If the client has only one active property, it is automatically pre-selected when creating a new appointment. 2.3 Estimates Tab Shows all estimates associated with this client. A count badge shows the estimate count. A "+ New Estimate" button at the top allows creating a new estimate with the client pre-selected. Clicking any estimate navigates to the Estimate Detail View. 2.4 Jobs & Invoices Tab Shows all jobs and invoices associated with this client. A count badge shows the job count. Two buttons at the top: "+ New Job" and "+ New Invoice" — both pre-fill the client. Clicking any job or invoice navigates to the respective Detail View. 3. Editing a Client To update information for an existing client: Step 1: Open the Client for Editing You can edit a client from two places: From the Client Detail Page — Click the "Edit" button in the sidebar. From the Clients List View — Click the edit icon (pencil) in the Actions column. The same five-step drawer opens, pre-filled with the client's current information. The title changes to "Edit Client" . Step 2: Make Your Changes In edit mode, all five steps are accessible — you can click any step indicator to jump directly to that section without needing to complete previous steps sequentially. You can modify: General — Name, phone, email, company name, website, tags. Note: the client type toggle is not shown in edit mode. Property — Property type, address, image, timezone, notes. Billing — Billing address fields, "same as property" checkbox. Attachments — Upload new or remove existing files. Review — Verify all changes before saving. Note: In edit mode, the email uniqueness check is not performed (since the client already owns that email). The client field in the edit panel displays the client's current information, which is read-only for the client account association but editable for the user details. Step 3: Save Your Changes Navigate to the Review step and click "Save Changes" . The system updates the client account, user information, and property in separate operations. On success, a toast appears: "Client successfully updated" . Click "Cancel" at any step to close the drawer without saving. 4. Archiving Clients Archiving is a safe way to deactivate a client without losing any historical data. Archived clients: Do not appear in the All, Residential, or Business tabs — they only appear in the Archived tab. Are preserved in historical records — existing appointments, estimates, jobs, and invoices remain intact. Can be restored at any time. How to Archive a Client On the Clients List View, find the client you want to archive. Click the three-dot menu (⋮) in the Actions column. Select "Archive" . A confirmation dialog appears: "Are you sure you want to archive [client name]?" Click "Archive" (red) to confirm, or "Cancel" to go back. On success, a toast appears: "Client archived successfully" . You can also archive a client from the Client Detail Page using the three-dot menu in the header. 4.1 Restoring an Archived Client To bring an archived client back into active use: Switch to the Archived tab on the Clients List View. Find the client you want to restore. Click the three-dot menu (⋮) and select "Restore" . A confirmation dialog appears: "Are you sure you want to restore [client name]?" Click "Restore" to confirm. On success, a toast appears: "Client restored successfully" . The client will be moved back to the appropriate active tab (All, Residential, or Business). 5. Managing Properties Each client can have multiple properties. Properties are managed from the Properties tab on the Client Detail Page. 5.1 Adding a New Property On the Client Detail Page, go to the Properties tab. Click the "+ New Property" button. A modal opens with the property form fields (same fields as Step 2 of the client wizard): property type, property image, address, city, state, ZIP, timezone, and address notes. Fill in the required fields and click "Save" to create the property. 5.2 Editing a Property On the Properties tab, click the edit icon (pencil) on the property card you want to modify. The same property form opens, pre-filled with the property's current data. Make your changes and click "Save" . 5.3 Deleting a Property On the Properties tab, click the delete icon (trash) on the property card. A confirmation dialog appears: "Are you sure you want to delete this property?" Click "Delete" (red) to confirm Warning: Deleting a property is permanent and cannot be undone. Consider whether any active appointments, estimates, or jobs reference this property before deleting. 5.4 Property Attachments Each property has its own set of attachments, managed separately from client-level attachments. Click the paperclip icon on any property card to open its Attachments panel. 5.5 Viewing a Property on the Map Click the "View on Map" link on any property card to open the Map view centered on that property's location. 6. Importing Clients via CSV [a] [b] You can import clients in bulk using a CSV file. To access the import feature, click the "Import CSV" button on the Clients List View. Note: The Import CSV button may not be visible if it has been disabled by your system administrator. Step 1: Upload Your CSV File Drag and drop a CSV file into the upload area, or click to browse your computer. The system validates the file format before proceeding. If you don't have a CSV file ready, click "Download Sample CSV" to get a template with example data and the expected column headers. Step 2: Map Columns The system shows your CSV headers and asks you to map each column to the corresponding client field. The following fields are available for mapping: Required: Company Name Email Optional: Website Property 1 and Property 2: Name, Contact Name, Contact Email, Contact Phone, Type, Address Line 1, Address Line 2, City, State, ZIP, Country Billing: Company Name, Contact Name, Address Line 1, Address Line 2, City, State, ZIP, Country Tags (comma-separated) Timezone Is Archived (Yes/No) Step 3: Preview and Validate A data grid shows the mapped data so you can review before importing. The system validates: Required fields have values. Email addresses are in valid format. Property types are either "RESIDENTIAL" or "COMMERCIAL". State codes are valid. Step 4: Import Click "Import" to begin the import process. The system shows progress and, on completion, displays the number of successfully imported clients and any errors. A toast message summarizes the result. 7. Exporting Clients to CSV [c] To download your client list as a spreadsheet file: Click the "Export CSV" button on the Clients List View. The system generates a CSV file containing clients matching your current filters (tab, search, city, state, tags). The file downloads automatically with a name like clients_2026-02-19_14-30.csv . On success, a toast appears: "Clients exported successfully" . Tip: Use the search and filter tools to narrow down the export. For example, switch to the "Business" tab before exporting to get only commercial clients. Note: The Export CSV button may not be visible if it has been disabled by your system administrator. 8. Client Attachments Attachments allow you to associate files with a client account. These are separate from property-level attachments. Opening the Attachments Panel There are three ways to manage client attachments: From the Clients List View — Click the three-dot menu and select "Attachments" . From the Client Detail Page — Click the three-dot menu in the header and select "Attachments" . During client creation — Attachments are part of Step 4 of the five-step wizard. Uploading Files Drag and drop files into the upload area, or click to browse. A progress bar appears for each file during upload. Uploaded files display the file name, size, and type icon. Deleting Files Click the trash icon next to any file to delete it. Deletion is permanent. 9. Quick Add Feature Quick Add allows creating clients with minimal information for rapid data entry. When a client is created via Quick Add (from another section such as Estimates or Jobs), the record is flagged as incomplete. Identifying Quick Add Clients Quick Add clients are identified by a red warning triangle icon next to their name in the Clients List View. A tooltip reads: "Created via Quick Add - details incomplete" . [d] [e] [f] Completing Quick Add Clients Click the warning icon or the "Complete Info" button in the tooltip. The Edit Client modal opens with whatever data was entered during Quick Add. Fill in the remaining required fields. Click "Save Changes" to complete the client. Once saved, the Quick Add flag is removed and the warning icon disappears. 10. Searching and Filtering Clients 10.1 Quick Search The search bar at the top of the Clients List View searches across client names, company names, email addresses, and other fields. Type your search term and results update automatically. 10.2 Advanced Filtering Three multi-select filter dropdowns are available: Filter What It Does Cities Shows only clients with properties in the selected cities. States Shows only clients with properties in the selected states. Tags Shows only clients that have the selected tags assigned. 10.3 Tab Filtering The four tabs (All, Residential, Business, Archived) provide quick filtering by client type and status. Tab filtering is combined with search and advanced filters. Tip: You can combine all filters for maximum precision. For example, search for "Smith" on the "Residential" tab while filtering by "Texas" in States and "Premium" in Tags to find all residential clients named Smith in Texas with the Premium tag. 11. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It Create a new client Click "+ New Client" , complete the five-step wizard, and click "Create Client" . Edit a client Open the Client Detail Page and click "Edit" in the sidebar, or click the edit icon on the List View. Archive a client Click the three-dot menu > "Archive" and confirm. Restore an archived client On the Archived tab, click the three-dot menu > "Restore" and confirm. Add a property On the Client Detail Page, go to the Properties tab and click "+ New Property" . Delete a property On the Properties tab, click the trash icon on the property card and confirm. Start a conversation Click "Start a Conversation" in the header or three-dot menu to open the Inbox. Manage attachments Click the three-dot menu > "Attachments" to open the Attachments panel. Import clients via CSV Click "Import CSV" , upload your file, map columns, preview, and import. Export clients to CSV Click "Export CSV" to download a CSV of the current filtered view. Create an estimate for a client On the Client Detail Page, go to Estimates tab and click "+ New Estimate" . Create a job for a client On the Client Detail Page, go to Jobs & Invoices tab and click "+ New Job" . Create an invoice for a client On the Client Detail Page, go to Jobs & Invoices tab and click "+ New Invoice" . Filter by client type Click the All , Residential , Business , or Archived tab. Complete a Quick Add client Click the warning icon next to the client name and fill in the missing fields.
services-management: Services Management Overview The Services Management section is where you manage all the service items your business offers. These are labor-based items — such as inspections, installations, repairs, or consultations — that can be added to appointments, estimates, invoices, and jobs. From this section you can: Add new services with pricing, descriptions, and supporting documents. Edit existing services to keep information current. Archive services you no longer offer (without losing historical data). Restore archived services if you need them again. Permanently delete archived services that are no longer needed. Search and filter to quickly find what you're looking for. Import and export services via CSV files for bulk operations. Manage attachments such as specifications, manuals, and safety documents. Important: A service can only be permanently deleted if it is not currently linked to any appointment, estimate, invoice, or job. If a service is in use, you must first remove it from all associated records before deletion is possible. Services List View The Services List View is the main view for managing all your services. To access it, navigate to Menu > Inventory > Services . When you open the Services List View, you will see a table listing all your active services. Each row represents one service and displays the following information: Column What It Means SKU A unique code that identifies the service (e.g., SVC-001 ). This cannot be changed after the service is created. Service The name of the service, along with its description (if one was provided). Cost Per Hour The hourly rate charged for this service, displayed in dollars (e.g., $75.00 ). Taxable A checkmark indicates this service is subject to tax. In Use A checkmark indicates this service is currently being used in at least one appointment, estimate, invoice, or job. Client Portal Visible A checkmark indicates that customers can see and request this service through your online client portal. Tags Color-coded labels you have assigned to help categorize the service (e.g., "HVAC", "Plumbing", "Electrical"). Groups The groups this service belongs to, if any. Groups are managed separately and help organize services into logical categories. Actions Quick-action buttons to edit the service, manage its attachments, or archive/delete it. Active vs. Archived Services At the top of the list you will see two tabs: Active — Shows all services that are currently available for use. This is the default view. Archived — Shows services that have been archived (temporarily disabled). Archived services do not appear in dropdowns when creating appointments, estimates, or invoices, but their historical data is preserved. You can switch between these tabs at any time to manage both active and archived services. Sorting the List You can sort the services list by clicking on the column headers for SKU , Service (name), or Cost Per Hour . Click once to sort in ascending order (A-Z or lowest to highest), and click again to sort in descending order (Z-A or highest to lowest). This helps you quickly organize services alphabetically or by price. 1. Adding a New Service To add a new service to your account, follow these steps: Step 1: Open the New Service Form Click the "+ New Service" button (blue) located in the top-right corner of the Services List View. A panel will slide open from the right side of the screen with a form to fill out. Tip: If the list is empty (no services have been added yet), you will also see a "New Service" button in the center of the page. Step 2: Fill In the General Information The form contains the following fields: Field Required? Description SKU Yes Enter a unique code to identify this service. This will be used as a reference throughout the system and cannot be changed after the service is created. Choose something meaningful, like INSP-001 for an inspection service. Service Name Yes Enter a clear, descriptive name for the service (e.g., "Annual HVAC Inspection" or "Emergency Plumbing Repair"). Cost Per Hour Yes Enter the hourly rate for this service in dollars (e.g., 75.00 ). The system accepts up to two decimal places. The maximum allowed value is $21,474,836.47. If you enter a value over $1,000,000, a warning will appear to confirm the amount is intentional. Description No Optionally, provide additional details about what this service includes, how long it typically takes, or any other relevant information. This description is visible to your team and can help clarify the scope of the service. Below the fields, you will find two configuration options: Taxable — Check this box if the service is subject to sales tax. When enabled, tax will be automatically calculated when this service is added to estimates and invoices. Show on Client Portal for Service Request — Check this box to make this service visible on your customer-facing online portal. Clients will be able to see and request this service when booking online. If left unchecked, only your internal team will see it. Step 3: Add Tags (Optional) Tags help you organize and categorize your services for easy searching and filtering later. To add a tag: Click on the Tags field. Type the name of the tag you want to add (e.g., "HVAC", "Premium", "Seasonal"). Press Enter to create the tag. Repeat to add as many tags as needed. Tags are flexible — you can create any tag name that makes sense for your business. Once created, tags become available for filtering on the Services List View. Step 4: Click "Next" to Continue After filling in the required fields and configuring your options, click the "Next" button at the bottom of the form. The system will validate your entries before moving forward: If any required field is missing, you will see an error message next to that field. If the SKU you entered already exists, a warning banner will appear at the top of the form letting you know. Step 5: Add Attachments (Optional) After clicking "Next", you will move to the Attachments tab. Here you can upload supporting documents for the service. Attachments are organized into five categories: Category Typical Use Specifications Technical specifications, datasheets, or detailed service descriptions. Manual Operating procedures, step-by-step guides, or reference manuals. Installation Installation instructions or setup guides related to the service. Safety Safety guidelines, compliance documents, or hazard information. Other Any other supporting documentation that doesn't fit the above categories. To upload a file: Select the appropriate category tab (Specifications, Manual, etc.). Either drag and drop a file into the upload area, or click the upload area to browse your computer. The file will begin uploading and show a progress bar. Wait for the upload to complete. Repeat to add more files in any category. Supported file formats: PDF, TXT, JPEG, JPG, and GIF. Note: You can skip this step if you don't have attachments to add right now. Attachments can always be added later through the Attachment Manager (see Section 2 below). Step 6: Save the Service Once you are satisfied with the information and attachments, click the "Add Service" button at the bottom of the form. You will see a brief "Creating Service..." overlay, and once the service is created successfully, a confirmation message will appear: "Service Successfully Created" . The new service will now appear in your Services List View and be available for use in appointments, estimates, invoices, and jobs. Tip: If you need to go back and modify the general information before saving, click the "Back" button to return to the previous tab. 2. Managing Service Attachments Attachments allow you to associate supporting documents with your services. This is useful for providing technicians with specifications, safety guidelines, installation instructions, or any other reference material they might need in the field. Opening the Attachment Manager There are two ways to manage attachments: From the Services List View: Click the paperclip icon in the Actions column for any service. This opens the Attachment Manager in a side panel. During service creation: Attachments are part of the second step when adding a new service (see Section 1, Step 5). Uploading Attachments In the Attachment Manager, select the category tab where you want to upload the file (Specifications, Manual, Installation, Safety, or Other). Drag and drop your file into the upload area, or click to browse your files. A progress bar will appear showing the upload status. Wait for it to complete. Once uploaded, the file will appear in the list below the upload area with its name and file size. If an upload fails, you will see a message: "The file uploading failed. Please retry." along with a Retry button to try again. You can also cancel an in-progress upload if needed. Searching Attachments If a service has many attachments, use the search bar at the top of the Attachment Manager to filter files by name. Simply type part of the file name and the list will update in real time. Downloading Attachments Single file: Click the three-dot menu next to any file and select "Download" to download that individual file. All files in a category: Click the "Download All" button to download every file in the currently selected category tab. The files will be packaged into a single ZIP file named [ServiceName]-attachments.zip . Deleting Attachments To remove an attachment: Click the three-dot menu next to the file you want to delete. Select "Delete" . The file will be permanently removed from the service. Note: Deleting an attachment is permanent and cannot be undone. Make sure you have a backup of the file if you might need it later. Closing the Attachment Manager When you are done managing attachments, simply close the side panel to return to the Services List View. All changes are saved automatically as you upload or delete files. 3. Editing a Service To update the information for an existing service: Step 1: Open the Service for Editing You can open a service for editing in two ways: Click the SKU of the service in the first column of the Services List View. This will open the edit panel. Click the edit icon (pencil icon) in the Actions column for the service you want to modify. The edit panel will slide open from the right side of the screen, pre-filled with the service's current information. Step 2: Make Your Changes You can modify any of the following fields: Service Name — Update the name if needed. Description — Add, edit, or remove the description. Cost Per Hour — Update the hourly rate. The same validation rules apply as when creating a service. Tags — Add new tags or remove existing ones. Taxable — Toggle the tax status on or off. Show on Client Portal for Service Request — Toggle whether this service is visible to customers on the online portal. Note: The SKU field is displayed but cannot be changed after the service has been created. This ensures consistency across all records that reference this service. Step 3: Save Your Changes Once you have made your edits, click the "Apply Changes" button at the bottom of the panel. You will see a brief "Updating Service..." overlay, and once the update is saved, a confirmation message will appear: "Service Successfully Updated" . If you haven't made any changes, the "Apply Changes" button will be grayed out (disabled) to indicate there is nothing to save. Tip: If you change your mind and don't want to save your edits, click "Cancel" to close the panel without saving. Archive or Delete from the Edit Panel While editing a service, you will also see an Archive button (or Delete button if the service is already archived) in the top area of the edit panel. This provides a convenient way to archive or delete a service without going back to the Services List View. 4. Archiving Services Archiving is a safe way to disable a service without permanently deleting it. Archived services: Do not appear in dropdowns when creating new appointments, estimates, or invoices. Are preserved in historical records — any existing appointments, estimates, invoices, or jobs that used this service will continue to show it. Can be restored at any time if you need the service again. How to Archive a Service On the Services List View (Active tab), locate the service you want to archive. Click the archive icon (trash icon) in the Actions column for that service. A confirmation dialog will appear: "Are you sure you want to archive [Service Name]?" Click "Archive" to confirm, or "Cancel" to go back. The service will be removed from the Active tab and moved to the Archived tab. 4.1 Restoring an Archived Service If you need to bring an archived service back into active use: Switch to the Archived tab on the Services List View. Find the service you want to restore. Click the restore icon (undo icon) in the Actions column. A confirmation popover will appear: "Do you want to restore this service item?" Click "Restore" to confirm. The service will be moved back to the Active tab and will once again be available for use in appointments, estimates, invoices, and jobs. 4.2 Permanently Deleting a Service Permanent deletion is only available for archived services and only when the service is not in use (not linked to any appointment, estimate, invoice, or job). Switch to the Archived tab on the Services List View. Find the service you want to permanently delete. Click the delete icon (trash icon) in the Actions column. If the delete icon is grayed out (disabled), it means the service is still in use somewhere. You will see a tooltip: "Delete In Use Service Item" . You must first remove the service from all associated records before you can delete it. A confirmation dialog will appear: "Delete [Service Name]?" Click "Delete" to confirm the permanent deletion, or "Cancel" to go back. A success message will appear: "Service Successfully Deleted" . Warning: Permanent deletion cannot be undone. Once a service is deleted, all of its information and attachments are removed from the system permanently. If you are unsure, keep the service archived instead. 5. Searching and Filtering Services The Services List View provides several tools to help you quickly find the services you need. 5.1 Quick Search The search bar at the top of the Services List View allows you to search for services by keyword. The search looks across the following fields: SKU Service Name Description Simply type your search term and the results will update automatically. For example, typing "plumb" would show all services that contain "plumb" in their SKU, name, or description (such as "Plumbing Repair" or "Plumbing Inspection"). To clear the search, delete the text from the search bar or click the clear button. 5.2 Advanced Filtering For more precise results, click the Filter button to open the advanced filter panel. The following filter options are available: Filter What It Does Tags Select one or more tags to show only services that have those tags. For example, selecting "HVAC" will show only services tagged with "HVAC". Groups Select one or more groups to show only services that belong to those groups. Taxable Check this box to show only services that are marked as taxable. Online Booking Check this box to show only services that are visible on the client portal for online booking. When filters are active, a badge with a number will appear on the Filter button, indicating how many filters are currently applied. This helps you remember that your view is filtered. To remove all filters at once, open the filter panel and click the "Reset Filter" link. Tip: You can combine quick search and advanced filters for maximum precision. For example, search for "inspection" while filtering by the "HVAC" tag to find all HVAC inspection services. 6. Importing and Exporting Services Note: The Import and Export features may not be visible if they have been disabled by your system administrator. 6.1 Exporting Services to CSV To download all your services as a spreadsheet file: Click the "Export CSV" button on the Services List View. The system will generate a CSV file containing all your services. The file will automatically download to your computer with a name like services_2026-02-18_14-30.csv (with the current date and time). The exported file can be opened in Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, or any spreadsheet application. This is useful for: Keeping an offline backup of your services. Reviewing and analyzing your service catalog in a spreadsheet. Preparing data for bulk updates via the Import feature. 6.2 Importing Services from CSV To add multiple services at once from a spreadsheet file: Click the "Import CSV" button on the Services List View. This will take you to the Import page. Follow the step-by-step import wizard: Upload your CSV file — Drag and drop your file or click to browse. Map your columns — Match the columns in your file to the required fields in the system. Review the data and click to import. Required columns in your CSV file: Column Description sku Unique identifier for each service. name The name of the service. unitRetailPrice The cost per hour in dollars (e.g., 75.00 ). Optional columns: Column Description description A description of the service. tags Tags to assign to the service. isTaxable Whether the service is taxable. isShowNewService Whether to show on the client portal. isShowOnlineBooking Whether to show for online booking. isArchived Whether the service should be created as archived ( true or false ). Once the import is complete, you will be returned to the Services List View where the newly imported services will appear. Tip: You can use the Export feature to download a sample CSV with the correct format, then use it as a template for your import file. 7. Quick Add (Keyboard Shortcut) For power users who need to create services quickly from anywhere in the application, there is a Quick Add feature: Press Ctrl + K (Windows) or Cmd + K (Mac) from any page in the application. A Quick Add modal will appear. Select "Service" from the options. Enter the SKU , Service Name , and Cost Per Hour . Click "Add" to create the service. The Quick Add modal creates the service with minimal information. After creating a service this way, you will see a confirmation with a "View Services" link that takes you to the full edit form where you can add a description, tags, attachments, and configure other settings. Note: Quick Add is designed for speed. For a complete setup including attachments and all configuration options, use the full "New Service" form from the Services List View. 8. How Services Are Used Across the Platform Once a service has been created, it becomes available throughout the platform in several key areas. Understanding where services are used helps explain why certain restrictions exist — for example, why you cannot permanently delete a service that is "In Use", and why the SKU cannot be changed after creation. Appointments When creating or editing an appointment, you can add services in the Services step . Each service added to an appointment includes: The service name and description. Hours and minutes for the expected duration. The hourly rate (pulled automatically from the service's Cost Per Hour). A calculated total based on time and rate. Services can also be edited directly from an appointment's Overview > Services tab , where you can adjust hours, minutes, and rates inline. Services cannot be modified on appointments that are marked as Completed , Canceled , or No-Show . Estimates In the Services tab of an estimate, you can add services and adjust their hours, minutes, and rates. The system calculates totals automatically and applies tax if the service is marked as taxable. Invoices Services work similarly in invoices. When viewing an invoice, the Services section displays all labor-based items with the following editable fields: Hours — The number of hours for this service. Minutes — The number of minutes (0-59). Rate — The hourly rate (pre-filled from the service's Cost Per Hour, but can be adjusted per invoice). Total — Automatically calculated based on time and rate. You can edit multiple services at once. When changes are made, a summary popover will show what was modified (original value vs. new value), and a "Save Changes" button will appear. Click it to apply all your edits at once. To add services to an invoice, click the "Add Services" button, select the services you need from the list, and they will be added with a default of 1 hour at the service's standard rate. To remove a service from an invoice, click the delete icon next to that service. Important: Once an invoice is marked as Paid , all services on that invoice become read-only . You will not be able to edit hours, rates, or remove services from a paid invoice. Jobs Jobs have a Services tab in their Overview where you can add, edit, and remove services with inline editing of hours, minutes, and rates — the same way as in appointments. Summary of Common Actions What You Want to Do How to Do It Add a new service Click "+ New Service" on the Services List View Edit a service Click the SKU or the edit icon on the Services List View Archive a service Click the archive icon on the Active tab Restore a service Click the restore icon on the Archived tab Permanently delete a service Click the delete icon on the Archived tab (only if not in use) Add attachments to a service Click the paperclip icon on the Services List View Search for a service Type in the search bar at the top Filter services Click the Filter button and select criteria Export all services Click "Export CSV" Import services from a file Click "Import CSV" Quickly add a service from anywhere Press Ctrl/Cmd + K
products-management: Products Management Overview The Products Management section is where you manage all the physical items and materials your business sells or uses in jobs. These are material-based items — such as parts, supplies, equipment, or consumables — that can be added to estimates, invoices, and jobs. Unlike services (which are billed by time), products are billed by quantity and include inventory tracking features so you always know what you have in stock. From this section you can: Add new products with cost, pricing, markup, and inventory details. Edit existing products to update pricing, stock levels, or descriptions. Archive products you no longer sell or use (without losing historical data). Restore archived products if you need them again. Permanently delete archived products that are no longer needed. Track inventory with quantity on hand, reorder levels, and stock availability. Search and filter to quickly find what you're looking for. Import and export products via CSV files for bulk operations. Bulk edit inventory quantities across multiple products at once. View stock availability including quantities reserved for estimates and jobs. Important: A product can only be permanently deleted if it is not currently linked to any estimate, invoice, or job. If a product is in use, you must first remove it from all associated records before deletion is possible. Products List View The Products List View is the main view for managing all your products. To access it, navigate to Menu > Inventory > Products . When you open the Products List View, you will see a table listing all your active products. Each row represents one product and displays the following information: Column What It Means SKU A unique code that identifies the product (e.g., PART-001 ). This cannot be changed after the product is created. Product The name of the product with a thumbnail image (if one was uploaded), along with its description. Hovering over the thumbnail shows a larger preview. Cost Your internal cost for this product — what you pay to acquire it. Displayed in dollars (e.g., $10.00 ). Markup % The percentage added on top of your cost to determine the selling price. This is automatically calculated from the difference between the Item Price and the Cost. Item Price The selling price your customers pay. Displayed in dollars (e.g., $15.00 ). Taxable A checkmark indicates this product is subject to tax. In Use A checkmark indicates this product is currently being used in at least one estimate, invoice, or job. Tags Color-coded labels you have assigned to help categorize the product (e.g., "Plumbing", "Electrical", "HVAC"). Groups The groups this product belongs to, if any. Groups are managed separately and help organize products into logical categories. Actions Quick-action buttons to edit the product, view its details, check inventory status, or archive/delete it. Inventory Status Indicator Each product row includes a small colored dot in the Actions column that shows the inventory status at a glance: Green dot — More than 5 units in stock. Yellow dot — Between 1 and 5 units in stock (running low). Red dot — Out of stock (0 units available). Clicking the inventory dot opens the Stock Availability window, which shows a breakdown of how your stock is allocated (see Section 3). Active vs. Archived Products At the top of the list you will see two tabs: Active — Shows all products that are currently available for use. This is the default view. Archived — Shows products that have been archived (temporarily disabled). Archived products do not appear in dropdowns when creating estimates, invoices, or jobs, but their historical data is preserved. You can switch between these tabs at any time to manage both active and archived products. Sorting the List You can sort the products list by clicking on the column headers for SKU , Product (name), Cost , or Item Price . Click once to sort in ascending order (A-Z or lowest to highest), and click again to sort in descending order (Z-A or highest to lowest). 1. Adding a New Product Adding a new product is a two-step process. The first step captures the essential pricing information, and the second step captures additional details like vendor, manufacturer, inventory, and images. Step 1: Open the New Product Form Click the "+ New Product" button (blue) located in the top-right corner of the Products List View. A panel will slide open from the right side of the screen. Tip: If the list is empty (no products have been added yet), you will also see a "New Product" button in the center of the page. Step 2: Fill In the General Information The first tab, General Info , contains the core pricing fields: Field Required? Description SKU Yes Enter a unique code to identify this product. This will be used as a reference throughout the system and cannot be changed after the product is created. Choose something meaningful, like FILTER-001 for an air filter. Name Yes Enter a clear, descriptive name for the product (e.g., "4-inch PVC Elbow" or "Honeywell T6 Thermostat"). Description No Optionally, provide additional details about the product such as specifications, model variations, or usage notes. Cost Yes Enter your cost to acquire this product (what you pay your supplier). The system accepts up to two decimal places. The maximum allowed value is $21,474,836.47. If you enter a value over $1,000,000, a warning will appear to confirm the amount is intentional. Markup (%) No Enter a markup percentage to automatically calculate the Item Price from the Cost. For example, entering 50 on a $10.00 cost will set the Item Price to $15.00. The maximum allowed markup is 1000%. This field auto-recalculates when you change the Cost or Item Price. Item Price Yes The price your customers will pay for this product. This field auto-recalculates when you change the Cost or Markup. The Item Price cannot be less than the Cost . If you enter a price lower than the cost, you will see an error message. Below the pricing fields, you will find one configuration option: Taxable — Check this box if the product is subject to sales tax. When enabled, tax will be automatically calculated when this product is added to estimates and invoices. How Cost, Markup, and Item Price Work Together These three fields are interconnected and automatically calculate each other to save you time: If you enter a Cost and a Markup % , the Item Price is calculated automatically. If you enter a Cost and an Item Price , the Markup % is calculated automatically. If you change any one of the three, the others adjust accordingly. Note: If the Item Price is set lower than the Cost, the Markup field will be cleared since a negative markup is not allowed. Step 3: Click "Next" to Continue After filling in the required fields, click the "Next" button. The system will validate your entries before moving forward. If any required field is missing or has an invalid value, you will see an error message next to that field. If the SKU already exists, a warning banner will appear at the top of the form.
