Step 4: Fill In the Additional Details The second tab, Additional Details , contains optional fields for more comprehensive product information: Field Required? Description Product Image No Upload a photo of the product. For best results, use a 500px x 500px image, maximum 2MB. The image will appear as a thumbnail in the Products List View. Vendor No Select the supplier you purchase this product from. You can also create a new vendor on the fly by using the quick-add option in the dropdown. Vendor SKU No Enter the vendor's own SKU or part number for this product. Useful for reordering from the supplier. Manufacturer No Select the manufacturer of this product. You can also create a new manufacturer on the fly using the quick-add option. Manufacturer Number No Enter the manufacturer's model or part number for this product. Group No Assign this product to a group for organizational purposes. You can create a new group on the fly using the quick-add option. Unit of Measure No Select how this product is measured or sold. Options include: Each, Box, Case, Gallon, Dozen, Cup, Pint, Quart, Milliliter, Liter, Ounce, Pound, Inch, Feet, Yard, Cubic Meter, Cubic Centimeter, and Cubic Millimeter. Color No Enter the color of the product, if applicable. Tags No Type a tag name and press Enter to create it. Add as many tags as needed to help categorize and filter your products. Disable Inventory Tracking No Check this box if you do not want to track stock levels for this product. When checked, the Quantity On Hand and Reorder Quantity fields will be disabled and set to zero. Quantity On Hand No Enter the current number of units you have in stock. This field is disabled if inventory tracking is turned off. Reorder Quantity No Enter the minimum stock level that triggers a reorder alert. When the Quantity On Hand falls below this number, you will see a warning. Location No Enter the physical storage location of this product (e.g., "Warehouse A, Shelf 3" or "Service Van #2"). Average Delivery Time No Enter the typical number of days it takes to receive this product from the supplier after ordering. Warning: If inventory tracking is enabled and the Quantity On Hand is below the Reorder Quantity, the quantity field will show a red warning and you will not be able to save until you adjust the values. This is to prevent creating products that are immediately flagged as below reorder level. Step 5: Save the Product Once you are satisfied with all the information, click the "Add Product" button at the bottom of the form. You will see a brief "Creating Product..." overlay, and once the product is created successfully, a confirmation message will appear: "Product Successfully Created" . The new product will now appear in your Products List View and be available for use in estimates, invoices, and jobs. Tip: If you need to go back and modify the general information before saving, click the "Back" button to return to the previous tab. 2. Viewing Product Details To see a complete read-only summary of a product's information without entering edit mode: Click the eye icon (view icon) in the Actions column for the product you want to view. A details panel will slide open showing all product information organized in a clean layout: Product image and basic info (name, description) Vendor and manufacturer details Group and unit of measure Inventory details (quantity on hand, reorder level) Location and average delivery time Tags and color Pricing (cost, markup, item price) Tax and inventory tracking status From this panel, you can click "Edit Info" at the bottom to switch directly into edit mode. 3. Checking Stock Availability To see how your product stock is allocated: Click the colored inventory dot (green, yellow, or red) in the Actions column for any product. A window will appear showing three key numbers: Field What It Means Stocks Available The number of units currently available (not reserved for any estimate or job). Stock Reserved for Estimates The number of units that have been allocated to open estimates but not yet confirmed. Stock Reserved for Jobs The number of units that have been allocated to active jobs. This breakdown helps you understand why your available stock might be lower than your total quantity on hand — some units may be reserved for pending estimates or active jobs. 4. Editing a Product To update the information for an existing product: Step 1: Open the Product for Editing You can open a product for editing in three ways: Click the SKU of the product in the first column of the Products List View. Click the edit icon (pencil icon) in the Actions column. Click "Edit Info" from the Product Details panel (see Section 2). The edit panel will slide open from the right side of the screen, pre-filled with the product's current information. Step 2: Make Your Changes You can modify any field except the SKU , which is displayed but cannot be changed after the product has been created. All fields from both the General Info and Additional Details tabs are editable, including pricing, inventory levels, vendor, manufacturer, image, and tags. Note: The SKU field is read-only in edit mode. This ensures consistency across all records that reference this product. Step 3: Save Your Changes Once you have made your edits, click the "Apply Changes" button at the bottom of the panel. You will see a brief "Updating Product..." overlay, and once the update is saved, a confirmation message will appear: "Product Successfully Updated" . If you haven't made any changes, the "Apply Changes" button will be grayed out (disabled) to indicate there is nothing to save. Tip: If you change your mind and don't want to save your edits, click "Cancel" to close the panel without saving. Archive or Delete from the Edit Panel While editing a product, you will also see an Archive button (or Delete button if the product is already archived) in the top area of the edit panel. This provides a convenient way to archive or delete a product without going back to the Products List View. 5. Archiving Products Archiving is a safe way to disable a product without permanently deleting it. Archived products: Do not appear in dropdowns when creating new estimates, invoices, or jobs. Are preserved in historical records — any existing estimates, invoices, or jobs that used this product will continue to show it. Can be restored at any time if you need the product again. How to Archive a Single Product On the Products List View (Active tab), locate the product you want to archive. Click the archive icon in the Actions column for that product. A confirmation dialog will appear: "Are you sure you want to archive [Product Name]?" Click "Archive" to confirm, or "Cancel" to go back. You will see an "Archiving Product..." overlay, followed by a success message: "Product Successfully Archived" . How to Archive Multiple Products at Once On the Products List View (Active tab), select the checkboxes next to the products you want to archive. Click the "Archive" button that appears in the bulk actions bar. A confirmation dialog will appear asking you to confirm the bulk archive. Click "Archive" to confirm. A success message will appear: "Products Successfully Archived" . 5.1 Restoring an Archived Product If you need to bring an archived product back into active use: Switch to the Archived tab on the Products List View. Find the product you want to restore. Click the restore icon (undo icon) in the Actions column. A confirmation popover will appear: "Do you want to restore this product?" Click "Restore" to confirm. A success message will appear: "Product Successfully Restored" . The product will be moved back to the Active tab and will once again be available for use in estimates, invoices, and jobs. 5.2 Permanently Deleting a Product Permanent deletion is only available for archived products and only when the product is not in use (not linked to any estimate, invoice, or job). Switch to the Archived tab on the Products List View. Find the product you want to permanently delete. Click the delete icon (trash icon) in the Actions column. If the delete icon is grayed out (disabled), it means the product is still in use somewhere. You must first remove the product from all associated records before you can delete it. A confirmation dialog will appear: "Delete [Product Name]?" Click "Delete" to confirm the permanent deletion, or "Cancel" to go back. A success message will appear: "Product Successfully Deleted" . Bulk Deleting Archived Products You can delete multiple archived products at once by selecting their checkboxes and clicking the "Delete" button in the bulk actions bar. If some of the selected products are in use: The confirmation dialog will tell you how many can be deleted and how many are in use. Only the products that are not in use will be deleted. Products that are in use will remain in the Archived tab. If all selected products are in use, the delete button will be disabled and you will see a message explaining that none of the selected products can be deleted. Warning: Permanent deletion cannot be undone. Once a product is deleted, all of its information is removed from the system permanently. If you are unsure, keep the product archived instead. 6. Bulk Editing Inventory If you need to update inventory quantities for multiple products at the same time: On the Products List View, select the checkboxes next to the products you want to update. Click the "Bulk Edit" button that appears in the bulk actions bar. A panel will slide open showing: A count of how many products are selected. A Quantity On Hand field — enter the new quantity to apply to all selected products. A Reorder Quantity field — enter the new reorder level to apply to all selected products. A list of the selected products with their current values for reference. Fill in the new values and click "Bulk Edit" to apply the changes. A success message will appear: "Products Successfully Updated" . Note: Bulk edit applies the same quantity and reorder level to all selected products. If you need different values for each product, edit them individually. 7. Searching and Filtering Products The Products List View provides several tools to help you quickly find the products you need. 7.1 Quick Search The search bar at the top of the Products List View allows you to search for products by keyword. Simply type your search term and the results will update automatically. To clear the search, delete the text from the search bar or click the clear button. 7.2 Advanced Filtering For more precise results, click the Filter button to open the advanced filter panel. The following filter options are available: Filter What It Does Tags Select one or more tags to show only products that have those tags. Groups Select one or more groups to show only products that belong to those groups. Taxable Check this box to show only products that are marked as taxable. When filters are active, a badge with a number will appear on the Filter button, indicating how many filters are currently applied. To remove all filters at once, open the filter panel and click the "Reset Filter" link. Tip: You can combine quick search and advanced filters for maximum precision. For example, search for "thermostat" while filtering by the "HVAC" tag to find all HVAC thermostats. 8. Importing and Exporting Products Note: The Import and Export features may not be visible if they have been disabled by your system administrator. 8.1 Exporting Products to CSV To download all your products as a spreadsheet file: Click the "Export CSV" button on the Products List View. The system will generate a CSV file containing all your products. The file will automatically download to your computer with a name like products_2026-02-18_14-30.csv (with the current date and time). The exported file can be opened in Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, or any spreadsheet application. 8.2 Importing Products from CSV To add multiple products at once from a spreadsheet file: Click the "Import CSV" button on the Products List View. This will take you to the Import page. Follow the step-by-step import wizard: Upload your CSV file — Drag and drop your file or click to browse. Map your columns — Match the columns in your file to the required fields in the system. Review the data and click to import. Required columns in your CSV file: Column Description sku Unique identifier for each product. name The name of the product. unitCost Your cost to acquire the product in dollars (e.g., 10.00 ). unitRetailPrice The selling price in dollars (e.g., 15.00 ). Optional columns: Column Description description A description of the product. tags Tags to assign (comma-separated, e.g., "lumber, construction"). isTaxable Whether the product is taxable ( Yes or No ). isArchived Whether the product should be created as archived ( Yes or No ). vendorSku The vendor's SKU for this product. manufacturerModelNumber The manufacturer's model or part number. color The product color. unitOfMeasure How the product is measured (e.g., EACH , GALLON , BOX ). quantityOnHand Current stock level (number). reorderLevel Minimum stock before reorder alert (number). isInventoryDisabled Disable inventory tracking ( Yes or No ). location Physical storage location. adt Average delivery time in days. Once the import is complete, you will see a success message: "Products Successfully Imported" , and you will be returned to the Products List View. Tip: You can use the Export feature to download a CSV with the correct format, then use it as a template for your import file. 9. Quick Add (Keyboard Shortcut) For users who need to create products quickly from anywhere in the application, there is a Quick Add feature: Press Ctrl + K (Windows) or Cmd + K (Mac) from any page in the application. A Quick Add modal will appear. Select "Product" from the toggle at the top. Enter the SKU , Name , Price , and optionally Quantity On Hand . Click "Add" to create the product. The Quick Add modal creates the product with minimal information and marks it as taxable by default. After creating a product this way, you will see a confirmation with a "View Products" link that takes you to the product details where you can add all the additional information (vendor, manufacturer, image, etc.). Note: Quick Add does not set a Cost or Markup — only the selling Price. To complete the full product setup, open the product for editing after creating it. 10. How Products Are Used Across the Platform Once a product has been created, it becomes available throughout the platform. Unlike services (which use hours and minutes), products use a quantity — the number of units being sold or used. Estimates In the Products tab of an estimate, you can add products and adjust their quantity and price per unit. Each product added shows: The product name, description, and SKU. An editable Quantity field (number of units). An editable Price field (pre-filled from the product's Item Price, but adjustable per estimate). An inventory status indicator showing current stock levels. A calculated total based on quantity and price. When you add a product to an estimate, the quantity is reserved — it appears in the "Stock Reserved for Estimates" count in the Stock Availability window. Note: Products that are out of stock (0 available quantity with inventory tracking enabled) cannot be added to estimates. They will appear grayed out in the product picker. Invoices Products work similarly in invoices. When viewing an invoice, you can add products with a quantity and price per unit. The price is pre-filled from the product's Item Price but can be adjusted for each invoice. Important: Once an invoice is marked as Paid , all products on that invoice become read-only . You will not be able to edit quantities, prices, or remove products from a paid invoice. Jobs Jobs have a Products section in their Overview where you can add, edit, and remove products. Key behaviors in jobs: Quantity can be adjusted using +/- buttons or by typing directly. Quantity is capped at available stock — you cannot add more units than are available (unless inventory tracking is disabled for that product). If you reach the maximum, a message will appear: "Maximum stock reached. [X] available." When you add a product to a job, the quantity is reserved — it appears in the "Stock Reserved for Jobs" count. A change summary popover shows before/after values when you modify quantity or price. If a job is locked , all product fields become read-only. Summary of Common Actions What You Want to Do How to Do It Add a new product Click "+ New Product" on the Products List View Edit a product Click the SKU or the edit icon on the Products List View View product details Click the eye icon on the Products List View Check stock availability Click the colored inventory dot on the Products List View Archive a product Click the archive icon on the Active tab Restore a product Click the restore icon on the Archived tab Permanently delete a product Click the delete icon on the Archived tab (only if not in use) Bulk edit inventory quantities Select products, then click "Bulk Edit" Bulk archive products Select products on Active tab, then click "Archive" Bulk delete archived products Select products on Archived tab, then click "Delete" Search for a product Type in the search bar at the top Filter products Click the Filter button and select criteria Export all products Click "Export CSV" Import products from a file Click "Import CSV" Quickly add a product from anywhere Press Ctrl/Cmd + K and select "Product"
availability-management: Availability Management Overview The Availability section is where you configure when your business is open for service and when it is not. Think of it as your business calendar — it tells the platform which days and hours your team is available to work, and which dates, months, or seasons should be blocked off entirely. Availability affects how appointments are scheduled across the platform. When someone creates an appointment, the system uses these settings to determine which time slots are available. There are two levels of availability: Global Availability — Sets the overall work schedule for your entire business. This is the default schedule that applies to all crews unless overridden. Per Crew Availability — Allows you to set a custom schedule for individual crews. A crew's availability must fall within the global hours (a crew cannot be scheduled outside the business's overall hours). From this section you can: Enable or disable work days — Toggle individual days of the week on or off. Set time slots — Define one or more working time ranges per day (for example, morning and afternoon shifts). Add exceptions — Block off specific date ranges, entire months, or full seasons when your business is unavailable. Configure per-crew schedules — Give individual crews their own availability within the global hours. Important: Availability is not a standalone list of records like Services or Products. It is a configuration page — you set it up once and adjust it as your business needs change. There are no items to archive or delete. Changes are saved automatically as you make them. Accessing Availability There are two ways to access Availability settings: Main Availability Page — Navigate to Menu > Availability . This is the full-page view with the title "Availability" at the top, the schedule type toggle, and all configuration sections. Settings > Availability — Navigate to Menu > Settings > Availability . This shows the exact same form embedded within the Settings page. Any changes you make in either location are reflected in both. Tip: If you see an Availability summary on a crew's detail view, you can click the View Availability Settings link to jump directly to the Availability page with that crew pre-selected in Per Crew mode. Schedule Types: Global vs. Per Crew At the top of the Availability page, you will see a segmented control (toggle) with two options: Option What It Does Global Sets the overall service availability for your entire business. This is the default schedule. The description reads: "Set your overall service availability." Below the toggle, the section is titled General Schedule . Per Crew Lets you set a custom schedule for a specific crew. The description reads: "Set crew service availability." After selecting this option, a Select Crew dropdown appears where you choose which crew to configure. Note: You cannot switch between Global and Per Crew while any changes are being saved. The toggle is disabled during save operations to prevent data conflicts. Selecting a Crew (Per Crew Mode) When you switch to Per Crew , a dropdown labeled Select Crew appears. This dropdown lists all the crews in your account (up to 100 crews are loaded). Select the crew you want to configure. Once selected, the page loads that crew's existing availability settings so you can review and modify them. Important: In Per Crew mode, a crew's time slots must fall within the global hours . If you try to set a crew's time slot outside the global availability window, you will see a warning message: "Crew slot is outside global hours." The system will show you what the global hours are for that day so you can adjust accordingly. Work Days Section The Work Days section is where you configure which days of the week your business (or a specific crew) is available, and during what hours. It is displayed inside a bordered card with the heading Work Days . You will see all seven days of the week listed vertically: Day Index Monday 1 Tuesday 2 Wednesday 3 Thursday 4 Friday 5 Saturday 6 Sunday 7 Enabling and Disabling Days Each day has a toggle switch next to it. Here's how it works: Turning a day ON — Flipping the toggle to the "on" position enables that day and automatically creates an empty time slot for you to fill in. Turning a day OFF — Flipping the toggle to the "off" position removes all time slots for that day and immediately saves the change to the backend. You will see a success message: "Availability updated successfully." Warning: Turning off a day is immediate and removes all time slots for that day. If a day had multiple time slots (such as a morning and afternoon shift), all of them will be removed at once. Setting Time Slots For each enabled day, you can define one or more time slots. Each time slot represents a continuous block of working hours. Each time slot row has: Element Description From time selector The start time of the work period. Click the field to open a time picker modal where you select hours and minutes, then AM or PM. To time selector The end time of the work period. Same time picker interface as the From field. Apply button (checkmark icon) Saves this time slot to the backend. Only enabled when both From and To times are set and valid. Disabled if any other time slot on any day has incomplete times. Discard button (X icon) Reverts the time slot to its previously saved state. If this is a brand-new slot that has never been saved, discarding it removes it entirely. Delete button (trash icon) Removes a previously saved time slot. Only shown when a day has more than one time slot. Immediately saves to the backend. Adding Multiple Time Slots Per Day To add a second (or third, etc.) time slot on the same day, click the + Add Time Slot button that appears below the existing slots for that day. Note: You can only add one new time slot at a time across all days. While you have an unsaved (pending) time slot, the + Add Time Slot buttons on all other days are disabled. You must either Apply or Discard the pending slot before you can add another one. Time Slot Validation Rules The system enforces several rules when you set time slots: Rule Error Message The From time must be before the To time (same day) "Time slot cannot span to the next day" Time slots on the same day cannot overlap each other "Time slots overlap" All time slots across all days must have complete times before you can apply any slot "Please complete all time slots before applying" Both From and To times must be set to a valid time "Please select valid start and end times" (Per Crew only) Crew time slots must fall within the global availability window "Crew slot is outside global hours" Tip: Times are displayed in 12-hour format (for example, "9:00 AM" or "5:30 PM"). Internally, the system stores times in HHMM format (such as "0900" for 9:00 AM or "1730" for 5:30 PM). Exceptions Section The Exceptions section lets you define periods when your business (or a specific crew) is completely unavailable, regardless of the regular work days schedule. This is useful for holidays, vacation periods, seasonal shutdowns, or any other time you need to block off the calendar. The Exceptions area appears below the Work Days section with the heading Exceptions and an Add button to the right. There are three types of exceptions: Exception Type Tab Label What It Does Date Range By Day Range Blocks a specific range of dates (for example, December 24 through January 2). Can optionally repeat every year. Month By Month Blocks one or more entire months (for example, all of January and February). Only one set of month exceptions is allowed at a time. Season By Season Blocks one or more seasons: Winter, Spring, Summer, or Fall. Only one set of season exceptions is allowed at a time. Adding an Exception Step 1: Click the Add button in the Exceptions header. A form will appear with tabs at the top for the available exception types. Step 2: Select the type of exception you want to create by clicking one of the tabs: By Day Range , By Month , or By Season . Note: If you already have a month exception configured, the By Month tab will not appear (you can only have one set of month exceptions). The same applies to season exceptions — once configured, the By Season tab disappears. Date range exceptions have no limit; you can add as many as you need. When both month and season exceptions are already configured, the message "Month and season exceptions configured" appears. Date Range Exceptions (By Day Range) Date range exceptions block a specific window of dates. Field Required Description From Yes The start date of the exception period. Click the field to open a date picker. To Yes The end date of the exception period. Must be the same date or later than the From date. Annual Exception No A checkbox. When checked, this exception repeats every year on the same dates. Useful for recurring holidays or annual shutdowns. Unchecked by default. After filling in the dates, a description appears below the form: "All services will be unavailable: [start date] - [end date]" (and if annual is checked, it adds "annually"). Click “ Add Exception ” to save. The button shows "Creating..." while saving. Note: If the end date is before the start date, the “ Add Exception” button remains disabled. Editing a Date Range Exception After a date range exception is created, it appears as a card showing the date range and whether it recurs annually. Each card has: An Edit button (pencil icon) — Click to open the exception in edit mode, where you can change the From date, To date, and the Annual checkbox. A Delete button (trash icon) — Click to delete the exception (a confirmation dialog appears first). When editing: Modify the fields as needed. Click Save to apply changes (the button shows "Saving..." while processing). Click Cancel to discard your changes and return to the view mode. Month Exceptions (By Month) Month exceptions let you block one or more entire calendar months. When you select the By Month tab, you see: 12 circular month buttons arranged in a row — Jan, Feb, Mar, Apr, May, Jun, Jul, Aug, Sep, Oct, Nov, Dec. Each button is a small circle showing the three-letter month abbreviation. Click a month to select it (the button turns blue with white text). Click again to deselect (returns to gray). A description below updates in real time: "All services will be unavailable: [selected month names]." For a new month exception (created from the Add form): Select the months you want to block. Click Apply Changes to save (the button shows "Applying..." while processing). Click Cancel to discard and close the form. For an existing month exception (already saved): The exception appears as a card titled Exception with a number (for example, "Exception #1"). You can toggle months on or off. When changes are detected, Apply Changes and Cancel buttons appear. A Delete button (trash icon) in the card header lets you remove all month exceptions. Season Exceptions (By Season) Season exceptions let you block one or more entire seasons. When you select the By Season tab, you see: 4 checkboxes : Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall. Check the seasons you want to block. A description below updates in real time: "All services will be unavailable: [selected season names]." The season definitions used by the system are: Season Months Covered Winter November, December, January Spring February, March, April Summer May, June, July Fall August, September, October For a new season exception: Check the seasons you want to block. Click Apply Changes to save (the button shows "Applying..." while processing). Click Cancel to discard and close the form. For an existing season exception: The exception appears as a card titled Exception with a number. You can toggle seasons on or off. When changes are detected, Apply Changes and Cancel buttons appear. A Delete button (trash icon) in the card header lets you remove all season exceptions. Deleting Exceptions All exception types can be deleted. Here's what happens for each: Exception Type How to Delete Confirmation Dialog Date Range Click the trash icon on the exception card. Title: "Delete Exception" — Message: "Are you sure you want to delete this exception?" Month Click the trash icon in the month exception card header. Confirmation message: "Are you sure you want to delete this month exception?" Season Click the trash icon in the season exception card header. Confirmation message: "Are you sure you want to delete this season exception?" Each confirmation dialog has a Cancel button (to go back without deleting) and a Delete button (to confirm the deletion). Empty State When no exceptions of any type have been configured, the Exceptions section shows the message: "No exceptions." How Changes Are Saved Availability uses an auto-save approach — most changes are saved to the backend as soon as you confirm them. Here is how saving works for each action: Action When It Saves Success Feedback Turn off a work day Immediately when you flip the toggle off "Availability updated successfully" (Global) or "Crew successfully updated" (Per Crew) Turn on a work day Does not save yet — waits for you to set times and click Apply No feedback until you Apply Apply a time slot When you click the Apply (checkmark) button "Availability updated successfully" or "Crew successfully updated" Delete a time slot Immediately when you click the trash icon "Availability updated successfully" or "Crew successfully updated" Create a date exception When you click Create Exception "Availability updated successfully" or "Crew successfully updated" Save a month/season exception When you click Apply Changes "Availability updated successfully" or "Crew successfully updated" Delete an exception When you confirm in the dialog "Availability updated successfully" or "Crew successfully updated" Note: If a save fails, the system automatically reverts your change to the previous state so your data stays consistent. You will see an error toast message: "Failed to update availability." or "Failed to update crew." Cross-Platform Usage Availability is not just a settings page — it directly affects how other parts of the platform work. Appointments and Scheduler When creating or editing an appointment, the system checks the crew's availability to determine which time slots can be offered. The appointment form uses the availability data to: Show available time slots based on the assigned crew's schedule. Check for date exceptions (date ranges, months, seasons) and block those dates. Display an Availability Info note so users understand why certain times may not be available. If a user needs to schedule an appointment outside the normal availability window, the system supports an Override Availability option. When this is enabled, the appointment can be created even if it falls outside the configured hours or during an exception period. Tip: The Override Availability option is useful for emergency calls, special client requests, or one-off situations where the regular schedule doesn't apply. Crews Each crew has an Availability summary displayed on its detail view. This summary shows: General Availability — A list of enabled days with their time ranges, each marked with a green dot. For example: "Monday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM." Exceptions and Special Conditions — A list of all active exceptions, each marked with an orange dot. This includes date range exceptions (for example, "Not Available Dec 24 - Jan 2"), month exceptions (for example, "Not Available during January"), and season exceptions (for example, "Not Available during Winter"). If no availability data has been configured for a crew, the summary shows: "No availability data." Clicking View Availability Settings on a crew's summary navigates you to the Availability page with that crew automatically pre-selected in Per Crew mode. Jobs and Estimates When scheduling work through jobs, the system uses crew availability data to calculate available time slots for the job's duration. Exception dates, monthly exceptions, and seasonal exceptions are all factored into the calculation. Step-by-Step: Setting Up Availability from Scratch If this is your first time configuring availability, follow these steps: Step 1: Set Global Work Days Navigate to Menu > Availability (or Settings > Availability ). Make sure Global is selected in the toggle at the top. For each day your business operates, flip the toggle switch to the "on" position. Set the From and To times for each enabled day. Click the time fields to open the time picker. Click the Apply (checkmark) button next to each time slot to save it. If a day has split shifts (for example, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM and 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM), click + Add Time Slot and repeat steps 4-5 for the additional slot. Step 2: Add Any Exceptions Scroll down to the Exceptions section. Click Add . Choose the exception type: By Day Range , By Month , or By Season . Fill in the details (dates, months, or seasons to block). Click Create Exception or Apply Changes to save. Repeat for each exception you need. Remember you can have unlimited date range exceptions but only one set of month exceptions and one set of season exceptions. Step 3: Configure Per-Crew Schedules (Optional) Switch to Per Crew using the toggle at the top. Select a crew from the Select Crew dropdown. Set the crew's work days and time slots, following the same process as for Global availability. Add any crew-specific exceptions. Repeat for each crew that needs a custom schedule. Important: Remember that a crew's time slots cannot extend beyond the global hours. If the global schedule says Monday is 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, a crew cannot be set to 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM on Monday. You'll see the warning "Crew slot is outside global hours" if you try. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It Switch between Global and Per Crew Click the Global or Per Crew toggle at the top of the page Enable a work day Flip the day's toggle switch on, set times, click Apply Disable a work day Flip the day's toggle switch off (saves immediately) Add a second time slot to a day Click + Add Time Slot below the existing slot, set times, click Apply Remove a time slot Click the trash icon next to the slot (only visible when a day has multiple slots) Discard unsaved time changes Click the Discard (X) button next to the slot Block a specific date range Click Add in Exceptions > By Day Range > set dates > Create Exception Make a date exception annual Check the Annual Exception checkbox when creating or editing the exception Block entire months Click Add in Exceptions > By Month > select months > Apply Changes Block entire seasons Click Add in Exceptions > By Season > check seasons > Apply Changes Edit a date range exception Click the pencil icon on the exception card, modify dates, click Save Delete any exception Click the trash icon on the exception card and confirm in the dialog View a crew's availability Go to the crew's detail view and look for the Availability section, or click View Availability Settings Set up a crew-specific schedule Switch to Per Crew , select the crew, configure days and exceptions Override availability for an appointment Enable the Override Availability option in the appointment creation form
appointments-management: Appointments Management Overview The Appointments Management section is where you schedule, track, and manage all field service appointments for your clients. An appointment represents a single scheduled visit to a client's property, assigned to a specific crew at a specific date and time. From this section you can: Create new appointments with a 4-step wizard that walks you through details, scheduling, services, and review. Edit existing appointments to update details, reschedule, or change service items. Track status as appointments progress from Draft through Scheduled, Confirmed, En Route, Started, and finally Completed. Send to client to notify clients about their upcoming appointment with a summary of services and billing. Convert appointments into estimates, jobs, or invoices for seamless workflow continuity. Archive appointments you no longer need in your active view. Restore archived appointments back to active status. Permanently delete archived appointments that have no linked records. Use the Scheduler to see appointments on a visual calendar with day, week, month, and list views. Communicate with clients via Chat, add internal Notes, and manage Attachments from the sidebar. Important: An appointment can only be permanently deleted if it is not linked to any job, estimate, invoice, or timesheet entry. If any linked records exist, you must first remove those links before deletion is possible. Appointments List View The Appointments List View is the main view for managing all your appointments. To access it, navigate to Menu > Appointments . When you open the Appointments List View, you will see a table listing your appointments. Each row represents one appointment and displays the following information: Column What It Means Name The appointment name. If the appointment is marked as High Priority , a priority icon appears next to the name. If it is a Callback appointment (a follow-up visit), a callback icon appears as well. Click the name to open the appointment's Detail Page. This column is sortable. Client The client associated with this appointment. Displays the client's avatar (or initials) and company name. Scheduled For The scheduled date, time range (start – end), and duration of the appointment (e.g., Feb 20, 2026 9:00 AM – 11:00 AM • 2h ). If no date has been set, it shows "Not Scheduled". This column is sortable. Crew The crew assigned to this appointment. Displays a color dot matching the crew's assigned color, followed by the crew name. Status The current status of the appointment, displayed as a color-coded tag. If the appointment has a chat with unread messages, a chat icon with the unread count appears next to the status. Clicking that icon takes you to the Inbox. This column is sortable. Actions Quick-action buttons: an Edit icon (pencil) to open the Detail Page, and a More Actions menu (three dots) for additional options like Send to Client, View on Scheduler, View on Map, Cancel, Archive, Restore, or Delete. Active vs. Archived Appointments At the top of the list, a segmented control lets you switch between two views: Tab What It Shows Active Appointments that are not archived. These are your current, in-progress, or upcoming appointments. Archived Appointments that have been archived. From this tab you can Restore an appointment back to Active, or permanently Delete it (if it has no linked records). Appointment Statuses Each appointment has one of the following statuses, displayed as color-coded tags throughout the system: Status Description Draft The appointment has been created but is not yet fully scheduled. It can still be edited freely. A draft appointment can be saved even without a date/time. Scheduled The appointment has a date, time, and crew assigned and is ready for the crew to work on. This is also the default status when restoring an archived appointment. Confirmed The client has confirmed the appointment. The appointment is still editable. En Route The crew is traveling to the client's location. The appointment is still editable. Started The crew has arrived and work is in progress. The appointment is still editable and the timer can be used to track work time. Completed All work is finished. The appointment becomes read-only and cannot be edited. It moves to the Archived tab. Canceled The appointment was canceled before completion. It becomes read-only and moves to the Archived tab. No-Show The client did not show up for the appointment. It becomes read-only and moves to the Archived tab. Note: Once an appointment is set to Completed, Canceled, or No-Show, it cannot be edited. You can only Restore it (which changes its status back to Scheduled) or Delete it (permanent, if no linked records exist). 1. Creating a New Appointment To create a new appointment, click the "+ Add Appointment" button at the top-right of the Appointments List View. A side-panel drawer will open with a 4-step wizard . Step 1: Details Fill in the basic appointment information: Field Required Description Appointment Name Yes A descriptive name for the appointment (e.g., "Annual HVAC Inspection", "Furnace Repair Follow-up"). Client Account Yes Select the client from a searchable dropdown. You can also use Quick Add to create a new client on the fly. Property Yes Select the property where the work will take place. This dropdown auto-populates based on the selected client's properties. You can also Quick Add a new property. Crew Yes Choose the crew that will perform the work. The crew selection affects which dates/times are available in the next step. You can also Quick Add a new crew. Priority No Check this box to flag the appointment as High Priority. A priority icon will appear next to the appointment name throughout the system. Callback No Check this box if this is a follow-up/callback appointment. When checked, a Job dropdown appears so you can link this callback to the original job. Job Only if Callback is checked Select the job this callback appointment relates to. This field only appears when the Callback checkbox is enabled. After filling in all required fields, click "Next Step" to proceed. Step 2: Schedule Choose when the appointment will take place: Field Required Description Date Yes Select the appointment date. The date picker highlights available dates based on the selected crew's availability. Dates where the crew is unavailable are grayed out. Availability is checked within a 90-day window from today. Time Yes Select the start time from the available time slots. Time slots are shown in 30-minute intervals during the crew's business hours (typically 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM). Duration Yes Select how long the appointment will last. Options range from 15 minutes to 8 hours. The default is 1 hour (60 minutes). The system calculates the end time automatically. Override Availability No Toggle this on to bypass crew availability restrictions. When enabled, all dates and all 24-hour time slots become available, allowing you to schedule outside normal business hours. Schedule Conflicts: If the selected date/time overlaps with another appointment or job already assigned to the same crew, a Schedule Conflict Warning will appear. You can still proceed by clicking "Schedule Anyway" (the button turns amber to alert you), or go back to choose a different time. After scheduling, click "Next Step" . You can also click "Save as Draft" at any point during Steps 2, 3, or 4 to save the appointment in Draft status without completing all steps. Step 3: Services Add the services that will be performed during this appointment: Click the service dropdown to search and select from your available labor-type services. For each service added, you can configure: Hours and Minutes — How long this particular service will take. Unit Retail Price — The hourly rate (auto-populated from the service definition but can be adjusted). Total Cost — Calculated automatically based on hours × rate. Duplicate services are not allowed. If a service is already added, it will not appear in the dropdown. Note: Adding services is optional . You can create an appointment without any services and add them later from the Detail Page. Click "Next Step" to proceed to the review. Step 4: Review Review all the appointment details before finalizing: Client information — Name and contact details. Property address — Where the work will take place. Crew details — The assigned crew and their color. Date and time — The scheduled date, start time, and duration. Services list — All services with hours and pricing. If a schedule conflict exists, a warning banner will appear on this page as well. At the bottom you have three options: Button What It Does Save as Draft Saves the appointment with Draft status. The appointment will appear in your list but will not be considered "scheduled" yet. Cancel Closes the modal without saving. All entered data is lost. Create Appointment Creates the appointment with Scheduled status (requires all details and schedule to be complete). If there is a schedule conflict, this button shows as "Schedule Anyway" in amber. 2. Appointment Detail Page Click any appointment row in the List View (or click the appointment name) to open its Detail Page . The Detail Page is divided into a main content area and a right sidebar. Header The header shows: Title: "Appointment: [name]" with priority and callback icons if applicable. Status Dropdown: A dropdown to quickly change the appointment's status to any of the 8 available statuses. Start Timer: A button to start tracking work time for this appointment (only visible for non-archived appointments). More Actions (⋮): A menu with additional actions (see section 7 below). Tab 1: Overview The Overview tab displays all core appointment information in a card layout: Field Description Title The appointment name. Date Created When the appointment was first created. Property The service property address. A "Show Map" link opens the property on the Map view. Crew The assigned crew with their color indicator. Time The scheduled time range with timezone (e.g., "9:00 AM – 11:00 AM EST • 2h"). Date The scheduled date (e.g., "Thursday, February 20, 2026"). Callback for If the appointment is a callback, a link to the related job appears here. An Edit button at the top-right of the overview card opens the appointment edit modal (same 4-step wizard). This button is disabled for Completed, Canceled, or No-Show appointments. Below the overview card you will find: Visits Section: If the appointment has related follow-up visits, they are listed here. Services Section: A table listing all service items with columns for service name/description, hours, minutes, rate, and total cost. You can add services , edit existing service hours/pricing or delete individual service items. Editing is disabled for archived appointments. Bill Section: Shows the billing summary including subtotal, tax, discount, and total. You can also manage alternate billing addresses and "Bill to" client settings here. Tab 2: Related Records The Related Records tab shows any estimates, jobs, or invoices linked to this appointment, and lets you create new ones: Section Description Estimates Shows linked estimates, if any. Click "Create Estimate" to generate a new estimate from the appointment's data. The system copies the client, property, crew, and service items into the new estimate and navigates you to it. Jobs Shows linked jobs, if any. Click "Create Job" to convert the appointment into a job. A modal appears to set the job's start/end dates and priority. Invoices Shows linked invoices, if any. Click "Create Invoice" to generate an invoice from the appointment's data. A confirmation dialog appears before creating. Note: If the appointment is not yet Completed, an info banner appears: "Additional related records available after completion." You can still create related records before completion, but the banner serves as a reminder. Sidebar The right sidebar provides client information and communication tools: Client Info Section: Client avatar, company name, and type (Residential / Business). Phone number, email address, and billing address. An Edit button to modify the client's billing details (alternate billing address). A Bill to Client button to assi gn a different client for billing purposes (limited to 1 bill-to client per appointment). Bill to Client Section (appears only if a bill-to client has been assigned): Shows the bill-to client's name, phone, email, and billing address. A Change popover to switch to a different bill-to client. A Remove button (trash icon) to remove the bill-to client with a confirmation dialog. Communication Tabs: Tab Description Chat Client-facing messaging. Send and receive messages with the client. Supports file attachments and message threading. Unread messages are indicated with a badge count in the List View. Notes Internal notes visible only to your team (not sent to the client). Add timestamped notes with optional file attachments (Word, PDF, PPT, TXT, images). Individual notes can be deleted. Attachments View and manage all files attached to this appointment. Files are grouped by type with appropriate icons. You can download or preview attached files. 3. Editing an Appointment You can edit an appointment from several places: From the List View: Click the pencil (edit) icon in the Actions column, or select "Edit From the Detail Page: Click the "Edit" button on the Overview card. This opens the same 4-step wizard used during creation, pre-filled with the appointment's current data. In Edit mode , all four steps (Details, Schedule, Services, Review) are accessible and pre-marked as completed. You can jump to any step to make changes. Cannot Edit: Appointments with status Completed , Canceled , or No-Show cannot be edited. The Edit button will be disabled and show a tooltip: "This appointment cannot be edited." Quick-edit: On the Overview tab of the Detail Page, you can also edit service items directly without opening the full edit wizard. Click on a service's hours or minutes fields to modify them inline. Changes are saved in batch. 4. Archiving Appointments Archiving removes an appointment from the Active list without permanently deleting it. This is useful when an appointment is no longer needed in your day-to-day view but you want to keep the record. To archive an appointment: From the List View or Detail Page , click the More Actions menu (⋮). Select "Archive" . A confirmation dialog will ask: "Are you sure you want to archive this appointment?" Click "Archive" to confirm. The appointment will move from the Active tab to the Archived tab. 4.1 Restoring an Archived Appointment To restore an archived appointment back to active status: Switch to the Archived tab in the List View. Find the appointment and click the More Actions menu (⋮). Select "Restore" . A confirmation dialog will ask: "Are you sure you want to restore this appointment?" Click "Restore" to confirm. The appointment's status will be changed to Scheduled and it will reappear in the Active tab. 4.2 Permanently Deleting an Appointment Permanent deletion is only available for archived appointments. To delete: Switch to the Archived tab in the List View. Find the appointment and click the More Actions menu (⋮). Select "Delete" . A confirmation dialog will ask: "Are you sure you want to permanently delete this appointment?" Click "Delete" to confirm. Deletion Restrictions: An appointment cannot be deleted if it has any of the following linked records: A linked Job A linked Estimate A linked Invoice Associated Timesheet Entries If deletion is blocked, the Delete button will be disabled and a tooltip will explain the reason (e.g., "This appointment cannot be deleted. It has a linked job."). 5. Sending an Appointment to a Client You can send an appointment notification to the client via the "Send to Client" feature. This sends an email to the client with the appointment details, services, and billing summary. To send an appointment to a client: From the List View or Detail Page , click the More Actions menu (⋮). Select "Send to Client" . A side-panel drawer opens showing a preview of what the client will receive: Appointment Details — Client info, scheduled date/time. Services — All service line items with descriptions and pricing. Billing Summary — Subtotal, tax, discount, required deposit, and total. Click "Send to Client" to send, or "Cancel" to close without sending. Requirements: To send an appointment to a client, the appointment must have a scheduled date/time and a crew assigned . Draft appointments without these details will have the "Send to Client" option disabled. 6. Converting Appointments to Other Records From the Related Records tab on the Appointment Detail Page, you can convert an appointment into other record types. This streamlines your workflow by carrying over the client, property, crew, and service information. 6.1 Creating an Estimate from an Appointment Open the appointment's Detail Page. Go to the Related Records tab. In the Estimates section, click “Convert to Estimate" . The system creates a new estimate with the appointment's client, property, crew, and services pre-populated. You are automatically navigated to the new estimate's Detail Page. 6.2 Creating a Job from an Appointment Open the appointment's Detail Page. Go to the Related Records tab. In the Jobs section, click "Convert to Job" . A modal appears where you can set the job's start date, end date, and mark it as priority. Click "Create Job" to finalize. The new job will be linked to the appointment. 6.3 Creating an Invoice from an Appointment Open the appointment's Detail Page. Go to the Related Records tab. In the Invoices section, click "Convert to Invoice" . A confirmation dialog asks: "Are you sure you want to convert this appointment to invoice?" Click "Convert" to confirm. The system creates a new invoice with the appointment's data and navigates you to it. Note: Each appointment can have its own set of linked records. Once an estimate, job, or invoice is created from an appointment, it appears in the Related Records tab with a link to navigate directly to it. 7. More Actions Menu The More Actions menu (⋮) is available both in the List View (per row) and on the Detail Page header. The available options depend on the appointment's current state: Action When Available What It Does Edit Appointment Active (not Completed, Canceled, or No-Show) Opens the 4-step edit wizard with current data pre-filled. Send to Client Always visible; disabled for drafts without crew/date Opens the Send to Client preview drawer to notify the client by email. View on Scheduler Always visible; disabled if no date is scheduled Opens the Scheduler view in a new tab with this appointment highlighted. View on Map Always Navigates to the Map view centered on this appointment's property location. Complete Appointment Active appointments (not already Completed) Marks the appointment as Completed. A confirmation dialog appears first. Cancel Appointment Non-archived appointments Cancels the appointment. A confirmation dialog appears: "Are you sure you want to cancel this appointment?" Archive Non-archived appointments Archives the appointment (moves it to the Archived tab). Restore Archived appointments only Restores the appointment to Scheduled status (moves it back to the Active tab). Delete Archived appointments only; disabled if linked records exist Permanently deletes the appointment. Cannot be undone. 8. Time Tracking Each active appointment has a "Start Timer" button in the Detail Page header. Use this to track the time spent on an appointment: Click "Start Timer" to begin tracking work time. Clicking 'Start Time' will automatically change the status to 'Started' . The timer runs in the background as the crew works. Timer data is linked to timesheets for payroll and reporting purposes. The timer is not available for archived appointments (Completed, Canceled, No-Show). 9. The Scheduler (Calendar View) In addition to the List View, appointments can be viewed on a visual calendar . To access it, navigate to Menu > Scheduler . The Scheduler is powered by Syncfusion and offers the following views: View Description Day View Shows all appointments for a single day with time slots. You can click on a time slot to create a new appointment with that date/time pre-filled. Week View A custom week grid showing appointments across a 7-day period. Supports drag-and-drop to reschedule appointments. Month View A traditional month calendar showing appointment counts per day. List / Agenda View A list-style view showing appointments in chronological order, similar to an agenda. Scheduler Features: Crew Filtering: Toggle specific crews on/off to filter which appointments appear. Crew Colors: Each crew has an assigned color. Appointments are displayed in their crew's color for quick visual identification. Unavailable Dates: Dates with no crew availability are visually grayed out. Drag-and-Drop: In the custom Week View, you can drag appointments to a new time slot to reschedule them. Legend: A legend shows crew colors and event types for reference. Date Navigation: Use forward/back arrows to navigate between days, weeks, or months. Override Availability: Toggle to show all dates regardless of crew availability. 10. Searching and Filtering Quick Search Use the search bar at the top of the List View to search appointments by keyword. The search filters appointments by name, client name, and other text fields. Results update in real time as you type. Status Filter Click the "Status" filter button to open a dropdown with all 8 appointment statuses as checkboxes. You can select one or more statuses to show only appointments matching those statuses. Each status is displayed with its color-coded tag for easy identification. A badge on the filter button shows how many statuses are currently selected. Click "Reset Filter" to clear all status filters and show all appointments. Deselecting all checkboxes has the same effect as resetting — it shows all appointments. Sorting The following columns can be sorted in ascending or descending order by clicking the column header: Name Scheduled For (date/time) Status Pagination The list uses server-side pagination . You can configure the number of appointments shown per page (10, 25, or 50) using the page size selector at the bottom. Navigate between pages using the pagination controls. 11. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It Create a new appointment Click "+ New Appointment" and complete the 4-step wizard (Details → Schedule → Services → Review). Save as draft During creation or editing, click "Save as Draft" (available from Steps 2, 3, and 4). Edit an appointment Click the pencil icon in the List View, or click "Edit" on the Overview card in the Detail Page. Change appointment status Use the Status Dropdown in the Detail Page header to select a new status. Send to client More Actions menu → "Send to Client" → review the preview → click "Send to Client" . View on scheduler More Actions menu → "View on Scheduler" (opens in new tab). View on map More Actions menu → "View on Map" . Complete an appointment More Actions menu → "Complete Appointment" → confirm. Cancel an appointment More Actions menu → "Cancel Appointment" → confirm. Archive an appointment More Actions menu → "Archive" → confirm. Restore an archived appointment Archived tab → More Actions menu → "Restore" → confirm. Status changes to Scheduled. Permanently delete Archived tab → More Actions menu → "Delete" → confirm. Only possible if no linked records. Create estimate from appointment Detail Page → Related Records tab → "Create Estimate" . Create job from appointment Detail Page → Related Records tab → "Create Job" → set dates → confirm. Create invoice from appointment Detail Page → Related Records tab → "Create Invoice" → confirm. Add a service to an appointment Detail Page → Overview tab → Services section → "Add Service" . Track work time Detail Page → click "Start Timer" in the header. Send a chat message to client Detail Page → Sidebar → Chat tab → type and send. Add an internal note Detail Page → Sidebar → Notes tab → write and save. Change billing details Detail Page → Sidebar → click the Edit (pencil) icon next to client info to set an alternate billing address. Assign a bill-to client Detail Page → Sidebar → click the Invoice icon next to client info to assign a different billing client. Filter by status Click the "Status" filter button → check the statuses you want to see. Search for an appointment Type in the search bar at the top of the List View.
estimates-management: Estimates Management Overview The Estimates Management section is where you create, manage, and track service estimates for your clients. An estimate is a proposed scope of work — including services, products, and pricing — that you prepare for a client before starting a job. Once the client reviews and approves the estimate, you can convert it directly into a job. From this section you can: Create new estimates with client, property, crew, and service/product line items. Edit existing estimates to update details or line items. Submit estimates to clients via email and/or SMS for review. Track status through the estimate lifecycle (Draft, Ready for Review, Changes Requested, Approved). Convert to Job approved estimates into scheduled jobs with a single action. Duplicate estimates to quickly create similar proposals. Delete estimates that are no longer needed. Download PDF copies of estimates for offline sharing or records. Manage billing with alternate billing addresses and "Bill To" client assignments. Communicate with clients through built-in chat, notes, and attachments. Important: Estimates in Ready for Review or Approved status are read-only — you cannot edit service items, products, or billing details. To make changes, return the estimate to Draft or Changes Requested status first. The recurring schedule tab remains editable regardless of status. Estimates List View The Estimates List View is the main view for managing all your estimates. To access it, navigate to Menu > Estimates . When you open the Estimates List View, you will see a table listing all your estimates. Each row represents one estimate and displays the following information: Column What It Means Name The title of the estimate. Click the name to navigate to the Estimate Detail View. If the estimate was created via Quick Add (incomplete), a warning icon will appear next to the name indicating that additional information is needed. Client The client associated with the estimate, shown with their avatar (or initials) and company name. If no client is assigned, it displays "No Client". Total The total estimated amount in dollars (e.g., $1,250.00 ). Amounts are stored in cents and automatically formatted for display. Status The current status of the estimate, shown as a color-coded tag. If the estimate has a chat conversation, a chat icon will also appear. A blue chat icon with a red badge indicates unread messages. Date Created The date the estimate was created, formatted as Mon DD, YYYY (e.g., "Feb 10, 2026"). Actions Quick-action buttons: edit (pencil icon to open detail view), duplicate (copy icon), and delete (trash icon). Estimate Statuses Every estimate has one of four statuses. These statuses help you track where each estimate is in the approval process: Status Color Meaning Draft Purple The estimate is being prepared and has not been sent to the client. You can freely edit all details. Ready for Review Gray The estimate has been submitted to the client for review. The estimate becomes read-only (services, products, and billing cannot be edited). Changes Requested Orange The client has requested modifications. The estimate returns to an editable state so you can make the requested changes. Approved Green The client has approved the estimate. The estimate is read-only and can now be converted into a job. Sorting the List You can sort the estimates list by clicking on the column headers for Name , Total , Status , or Date Created . Click once to sort in ascending order and click again to sort in descending order. Searching Use the search bar at the top of the Estimates List View to search for estimates by keyword. The search looks across estimate names and client names. Simply type your search term and the results will update automatically. If no estimates match your keyword, you will see the message: "No estimates found matching your keyword" . Filtering by Status Click the "Status" filter button to open a popover with status checkboxes. You can select one or more statuses to show only estimates matching those statuses: Draft Approved Ready for Review Changes Requested When filters are active, a badge with a number appears on the filter button indicating how many status filters are applied. To remove all filters, open the popover and click "Reset filter" . Pagination The Estimates List View supports server-side pagination. You can change the number of estimates displayed per page and navigate between pages using the pagination controls at the bottom of the table. Row Actions Each row in the estimates table has three quick-action buttons in the Actions column: Edit (pencil icon) — Opens the Estimate Detail View for the selected estimate. Duplicate (copy icon) — Creates an exact copy of the estimate. A loading overlay displays "Duplicating Estimate..." while the operation completes. Delete (trash icon) — Opens a confirmation dialog to permanently delete the estimate (see Section 4). You can also click anywhere on a row to navigate to that estimate's Detail View. Chat Icon in Status Column If an estimate has an associated chat conversation, a chat icon appears next to the status tag. The icon behavior is: Gray background — A chat exists but there are no unread messages. Clicking navigates to the Inbox with the conversation open. Blue background with red badge — There are unread messages. The badge shows the unread count (up to "99+"). Clicking navigates to the Inbox. 1. Creating a New Estimate To create a new estimate, follow these steps: Step 1: Open the New Estimate Form Click the "+ New Estimate" button (blue) located in the top-right corner of the Estimates List View. A drawer panel will slide open from the right side of the screen with a two-step wizard. Tip: If the list is empty (no estimates have been created yet), you will also see a "New Estimate" button in the center of the page. Step 2: Fill In the Estimate Details (Step 1 of 2) The first step of the wizard is titled "Estimate Details" and contains the following fields: Field Required? Description Title Yes Enter a descriptive name for the estimate (e.g., "Kitchen Renovation Estimate" or "Annual HVAC Maintenance Proposal"). Client Yes Select the client this estimate is for from the dropdown. You can search by company name or email. If the client does not exist yet, click the "Quick Add" option to create a new client without leaving the form. The new client will be automatically selected after creation. Property Yes Select the property where the work will be performed. Properties are filtered based on the selected client. If no client is selected, this field displays "Select client first" and is disabled. You can also Quick Add a new property. Crew Yes Select the crew that will perform the work. You can also Quick Add a new crew from this dropdown. Checklist No Optionally select a checklist to associate with this estimate. Checklists help ensure all required steps are completed when the estimate is converted to a job. After filling in all required fields, click "Next Step" to proceed. The system will validate your entries — if any required field is missing, you will see an error message next to that field. Note: You can click on the step indicators at the top of the wizard to navigate between steps. Step 2 is only accessible once Step 1 passes validation. Step 3: Add Service Items (Step 2 of 2) The second step is titled "Service Items" and allows you to add services and products to the estimate. This step is optional — you can add items now or later from the Estimate Detail View. From this step you can: Add Services — Select from your existing service catalog. For each service, specify the hours and hourly rate. The total is calculated automatically. Add Products — Select from your product catalog. For each product, specify the quantity and unit price. The total is calculated automatically. Step 4: Save the Estimate You have two options for saving: "Add Estimate" (blue button) — Saves the estimate with all selected service items and products. A loading overlay displays "Creating Estimate..." while the operation completes. "Save as Draft" (text button) — Saves the estimate without adding any service items from Step 2. This is useful if you want to fill in the line items later. Both options create the estimate in Draft status. The new estimate will appear in your Estimates List View. Tip: To go back and modify the estimate details before saving, click the step indicator for "Estimate Details" or navigate back to the first step. 2. Estimate Detail View The Estimate Detail View is where you manage all aspects of a single estimate. To access it, click on any estimate's name or edit icon in the Estimates List View. The Detail View has two main areas: Main content area (left) — Contains tabs for Overview and Recurring Schedule. Client sidebar (right) — Shows client information, billing details, and communication tools (Chat, Notes, Attachments). Header Area The header displays: Estimate name — The title of the estimate. If the estimate is part of a recurring series, a recurring badge icon appears next to the name. Status dropdown — A color-coded tag showing the current status. Click it to change the status (see Section 5). Action buttons — Vary depending on the current status (see below). More actions menu (three-dot icon) — Contains additional actions: Duplicate, Download PDF, and Delete. Back button — Returns to the Estimates List View. If you arrived from an Appointment Detail View, the back button returns you to that appointment's Related Records tab. Action Buttons by Status The primary action buttons in the header change depending on the estimate's current status: Current Status Available Buttons What They Do Draft "Submit Estimate" Opens the Submit Estimate modal to send the estimate to the client via email and/or SMS (see Section 6). Changes Requested "Submit Estimate" Same as Draft — allows resubmitting after making requested changes. Ready for Review "Submit Estimate" Same as Draft — allows resubmitting if needed. Approved "Edit Estimate" + "Convert to Job" Edit Estimate changes the status back to Changes Requested (does not open an edit panel). Convert to Job opens the Convert to Job wizard (see Section 7). Requires at least one service item. Converted (has job_id) "View Job" Navigates to the Job Detail View for the created job. Note: The "Submit Estimate" and "Convert to Job" buttons are disabled if the estimate has no service items. A tooltip appears: "At least one service item required to submit estimate" or "At least one service item required to convert to job" . More Actions Menu The three-dot menu in the header contains three actions available in any status: Duplicate — Creates an exact copy of the estimate. Download PDF — Downloads the estimate as a PDF file named [EstimateName].pdf . Delete — Opens a confirmation dialog to permanently delete the estimate. 2.1 Overview Tab The Overview tab is the default tab and displays all the estimate's details organized into sections: Recurring Instance Banner If the estimate is a recurring instance (part of a recurring series), a blue information banner appears at the top with two buttons: "Edit This Estimate Instance" — Detaches this estimate from the series so changes apply only to this instance. "Edit Series" — Navigates to the parent (template) estimate where you can modify the recurring schedule for all estimates in the series. Overview Card An "Edit" button (with pencil icon) appears in the top-right corner. Clicking it opens the Edit Estimate drawer (see Section 3). This button is disabled when the estimate is in a read-only status (Ready for Review or Approved). The overview card displays a grid with the following fields: Field Description Title The name of the estimate. Date Created The date the estimate was created. Property The property name or address, with a Residential or Business tag if applicable. Crew The assigned crew name. Checklist The associated checklist name (if one was selected), shown as a gray tag. Only appears if a checklist is assigned. Services Section Lists all labor/service line items on the estimate. Each service shows the service name, description, hours, rate, and total. You can add, edit, or remove services when the estimate is not in a read-only status. Products Section Lists all material/product line items on the estimate. Each product shows the product name, description, quantity, unit price, and total. You can add, edit, or remove products when the estimate is not in a read-only status. Bill Section Displays the billing summary including subtotals for services and products, applicable taxes, discounts, and the total amount. Tax management and discount adjustments can be made from this section when the estimate is not in a read-only status. 2.2 Recurring Schedule Tab The Recurring Schedule tab allows you to set up automatic recurring generation of estimates. This tab remains editable regardless of the estimate's status (even when the estimate is Approved or Ready for Review). From this tab you can configure: Recurrence frequency and pattern Start and end dates for the series Exceptions and modifications to individual instances 2.3 Client Sidebar The right sidebar on the Estimate Detail View shows client information and communication tools. Client Information The top section displays: Client avatar (or initials) Company name with Residential/Business property type tags Phone number Email address Billing address (uses alternate billing info if configured, otherwise uses the property address) Two action buttons appear next to the client name: Edit icon (pencil) — Opens the Billing Details modal to change the billing address. Invoice icon — Opens the Bill To Client modal to assign a different client for billing. This button is disabled if a Bill To Client is already assigned (tooltip: "You can't add more than 1 bill to client" ). Bill To Client Section If a "Bill To" client has been assigned (different from the main client), a separate section appears showing that client's name, phone, email, and billing address. This section includes: Change (swap icon) — Opens a popover to select a different Bill To client. Remove (trash icon) — Opens a confirmation dialog: "Are you sure you want to remove bill to client?" to remove the Bill To assignment. Communication Tabs Below the client information, three tabs provide communication tools: Tab Description Chat Real-time messaging with the client. Messages sent here appear in the client's portal and your Inbox. Notes Internal notes visible only to your team. Use these for reminders, observations, or context about the estimate. Attachments Upload and manage files associated with this estimate (photos, documents, specifications, etc.). 3. Editing an Estimate To update the details of an existing estimate: Step 1: Open the Estimate for Editing From the Estimate Detail View, click the "Edit" button (with pencil icon) in the Overview tab header. A drawer panel will slide open from the right side of the screen, pre-filled with the estimate's current information. Note: The Edit button is disabled when the estimate is in Ready for Review or Approved status. To edit, first change the status back to Draft or Changes Requested using the status dropdown. Step 2: Make Your Changes The edit panel shows the following fields: Field Editable? Description Client No (read-only) Displays the client name with avatar. The client cannot be changed after the estimate is created. Title Yes (required) Update the estimate name. Property Yes (required) Change the property. You can also Quick Add a new property from the dropdown. Crew Yes (required) Change the assigned crew. You can also Quick Add a new crew from the dropdown. Checklist Yes (optional) Change or remove the associated checklist. You can clear this field to remove the checklist assignment. Step 3: Save Your Changes Click the "Save Changes" button at the bottom of the panel. While saving, the button text changes to "Saving..." and a loading indicator appears. Once saved successfully, the panel closes and the estimate is refreshed with the updated information. Click "Cancel" to close the panel without saving. Your changes will be discarded and the form will revert to the original values. 4. Deleting an Estimate Estimates can be permanently deleted from two places: From the Estimates List View Locate the estimate you want to delete in the list. Click the delete icon (trash icon) in the Actions column. A confirmation dialog will appear with the title "Delete Estimate" and the message: "Are you sure you want to delete this estimate?" Click "Delete" (red button) to confirm, or "Cancel" to go back. A loading overlay will display "Deleting Estimate..." while the operation completes. From the Estimate Detail View Open the Estimate Detail View for the estimate you want to delete. Click the three-dot menu in the header. Select "Delete" . The same confirmation dialog will appear. Click "Delete" to confirm. After deletion, you will be redirected to the Estimates List View. If you arrived from an Appointment Detail View, you will be redirected back to that appointment. Warning: Permanent deletion cannot be undone. Once an estimate is deleted, all of its information, service items, products, notes, and attachments are removed from the system permanently. 5. Changing Estimate Status You can change an estimate's status at any time using the status dropdown in the Estimate Detail View header. Click the color-coded status tag to open the dropdown, then select the new status. Status Transitions Most status changes happen immediately without confirmation. However, certain transitions require a confirmation dialog: From Status To Status Confirmation Required? Dialog Title Ready for Review Draft Yes "Return to Draft" Approved Draft Yes "Return to Draft" Approved Ready for Review Yes "Request New Review" Any other transition — No — While the status is being updated, a spinner with the message "Updating status to [new status]..." replaces the status dropdown. Once the update completes, a success toast appears: "Estimate Successfully Updated" .
