Read-Only Statuses When an estimate is in Ready for Review or Approved status, the following parts of the estimate become read-only: Overview card (Edit button disabled) Services section (cannot add, edit, or remove services) Products section (cannot add, edit, or remove products) Bill section (cannot modify taxes or discounts) The Recurring Schedule tab remains fully editable regardless of status. 6. Submitting an Estimate to a Client When your estimate is ready for the client to review, you can submit it via email and/or SMS. Step 1: Open the Submit Estimate Modal From the Estimate Detail View, click the "Submit Estimate" button in the header. A full-screen drawer opens with the submission form. Note: The Submit Estimate button is disabled if the estimate has no service items. You must add at least one service before submitting. Step 2: Configure Delivery Channels Choose how the estimate will be delivered: Email — Check this box to send the estimate via email. Enabled by default. SMS — Check this box to send the estimate via text message. Enabled by default. At least one delivery channel must be selected. Step 3: Customize the Message The form includes the following editable fields: Field Default Value Description Subject "Your estimate prepared!" The email subject line. Required if sending via email. Email Message Pre-filled with a standard message including a link to view the estimate online. The body of the email. You can customize freely. A "Reset Text" link restores the default message. Send me the copy Checked When enabled, a copy of the email is sent to your own email address. SMS Message Same default as email message. The text message content. Can be customized independently from the email message. Using Template Variables While typing in the Subject, Email Message, or SMS Message fields, press the / key to open a variable insertion menu. Available variables include: Category Variable Inserts General {{currentDate}} Today's date Client {{name}} Client's full name Client {{firstname}} Client's first name Client {{lastname}} Client's last name Client {{email}} Client's email address Client {{mobile}} Client's mobile number Step 4: Preview and Send Below the form, a Message Preview section shows how the message will look to the client with all variables replaced by actual values. You can toggle between Email and SMS previews using the segmented control. You can also click the "Preview Estimate" button (arrow icon) in the drawer header to open a full preview of the estimate document as the client will see it. Click "Submit Estimate" to send, or "Cancel" to close without sending. 7. Converting an Estimate to a Job Once a client approves an estimate, you can convert it directly into a scheduled job. Prerequisites The estimate must be in Approved status. The estimate must have at least one service item. Step 1: Open the Convert to Job Wizard From the Estimate Detail View (with Approved status), click the "Convert to Job" button. A drawer opens with a two-step wizard showing the job title and estimated duration at the top. Step 2: Select a Start Date (Step 1 of 2) A calendar is displayed showing available dates for the next 90 days. Dates are color-coded based on crew and client availability: Available dates — Selectable and shown normally. Unavailable dates — Grayed out and cannot be selected. These dates conflict with the assigned crew's schedule or the client's availability. Select a start date and time, then click "Next Step" . Step 3: Select an End Date (Step 2 of 2) Select the job's end date and time. The system validates that: The end date/time is after the start date/time. Both date and time fields are filled in. Priority Flag On both steps, a "Priority" checkbox is available at the bottom. Check this to mark the resulting job as high priority. Step 4: Convert Click "Convert to Job" to create the job. After conversion: The system creates a new job with the estimate's client, property, crew, services, products, and billing details. You are automatically navigated to the newly created Job Detail View. The estimate now has a linked job_id . Returning to the estimate, the header shows a "View Job" button instead of "Convert to Job". Note: If the estimate has already been converted to a job, opening the Convert to Job modal shows a success message: "This estimate has already been converted to job" with a "View Job" button to navigate to the existing job. 8. Duplicating an Estimate To create a copy of an existing estimate: From the Estimates List View Click the copy icon in the Actions column for the estimate you want to duplicate. A loading overlay displays "Duplicating Estimate..." while the copy is created. The new estimate appears in the list with the same details as the original. From the Estimate Detail View Click the three-dot menu in the header. Select "Duplicate" . The estimate is duplicated and the current view refreshes. The duplicated estimate includes all the original's details (title, client, property, crew, services, products, billing) but is created as a new, independent estimate in Draft status. 9. Downloading an Estimate as PDF To download a PDF copy of an estimate: Open the Estimate Detail View. Click the three-dot menu in the header. Select "Download PDF" . The system generates and downloads a PDF file named [EstimateName].pdf . The PDF can be shared with clients via other channels (print, email attachments, etc.) or kept for your records. 10. Searching and Filtering Estimates 10.1 Quick Search The search bar at the top of the Estimates List View allows you to search for estimates by keyword. The search looks across estimate names and associated client names. Simply type your search term and the results will update automatically. To clear the search, delete the text from the search bar or click the clear button. 10.2 Status Filter Click the "Status" button to open the filter popover. Select one or more statuses to filter the list: Filter Option What It Shows Draft Estimates being prepared, not yet sent to client. Approved Estimates approved by the client, ready for conversion. Ready for Review Estimates sent to the client, awaiting their review. Changes Requested Estimates where the client has asked for modifications. When filters are active, a badge with the number of active filters appears on the button. Click "Reset filter" in the popover to clear all filters. Tip: You can combine the quick search and status filter for precise results. For example, search for "renovation" while filtering by "Approved" to find all approved renovation estimates. 11. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It Create a new estimate Click "+ New Estimate" on the List View, fill in the two-step wizard, and click "Add Estimate" . Edit estimate details Open the Estimate Detail View, click "Edit" in the Overview tab, make changes, and click "Save Changes" . Add services or products On the Detail View Overview tab, click "Add Service" or "Add Product" in the respective sections. Change estimate status Click the status tag in the Detail View header and select the new status from the dropdown. Submit to client Click "Submit Estimate" , configure email/SMS delivery, customize the message, and click "Submit Estimate" . Convert to job With an Approved estimate, click "Convert to Job" , select start/end dates, and click "Convert to Job" . Duplicate an estimate Click the copy icon in the List View, or use the three-dot menu > "Duplicate" in the Detail View. Download as PDF Use the three-dot menu > "Download PDF" in the Detail View. Delete an estimate Click the trash icon in the List View, or use the three-dot menu > "Delete" in the Detail View. Confirm in the dialog. Change billing address Click the edit (pencil) icon next to the client name in the sidebar to open the Billing Details modal. Assign a Bill To client Click the invoice icon next to the client name in the sidebar to open the Bill To Client modal. Chat with client Use the Chat tab in the client sidebar on the Detail View. Add internal notes Use the Notes tab in the client sidebar on the Detail View. Upload attachments Use the Attachments tab in the client sidebar on the Detail View. Filter by status Click the "Status" filter button on the List View and select one or more statuses.
jobs-management: Jobs Management Overview Jobs are the core work units of the DHS platform. A job represents a specific piece of work to be performed for a client at a property, assigned to a crew, and scheduled for a particular date and time. Jobs bring together clients, properties, crews, services, products, checklists, and invoices into a single, trackable record. Jobs follow a status workflow that tracks their progress from initial creation through completion or cancellation. Each job can include services (labor items), products (material items), a checklist of tasks to complete, and related invoices. From this section you can: Create new jobs with a client, property, crew, and scheduled date/time. Track status through Draft, Scheduled, En Route, Started, Paused, Completed, and Canceled. Manage services and products — add, edit, and remove labor and material line items. Assign checklists — attach a checklist that must be completed before the job can be marked done. Complete jobs with a checklist review process. Reschedule jobs with crew availability checking. Generate invoices from completed jobs. Download PDFs of job details. Manage recurring jobs with repeating schedules. Search and filter by keyword, date range, and status. Important: A job can only be deleted if it has no linked invoices and no linked timesheet entries . If a job has invoices or timesheet entries attached, the delete button will be disabled and a tooltip will explain why. Jobs List View The Jobs List View is the main view for managing all your jobs. To access it, navigate to Menu > Jobs . At the top of the page you will see: A Search bar — type a keyword to filter jobs by name or details. A Date Range filter — select a predefined range (All Time, Last 7 Days, Last 30 Days, etc.) or a custom date range. A Status filter button — opens a popover with checkboxes for each status. Shows a badge with the count of selected filters. Includes a "Reset filter" button to clear selections. A New Job button — click to open the create job wizard. Table Columns Column What It Shows Name The job title. If the job has no title, it shows "Untitled Job." Recurring jobs display a recurring badge icon next to the name. Priority jobs show a priority flag icon. This column is sortable. Client The client's company name with their avatar. Scheduled The start and end date/time formatted naturally. If the job spans a single day, only one date is shown with the time range. This column is sortable. Crew The assigned crew's name with a colored dot matching the crew's color. Status The job's current status as a colored tag (see Status Tags below). If the job has unread chat messages, a chat icon with a count badge appears next to the status. This column is sortable. Total The job's total value displayed as a formatted currency amount. Actions View and Delete buttons (see Row Actions below). Status Tags Status Tag Color Meaning Draft Blue/Primary The job has been created but not yet scheduled or assigned. Scheduled Gray/Default The job has a confirmed date, time, and crew assignment. En Route Blue/Primary The crew is traveling to the job site. Started Yellow/Warning The crew has arrived and work is in progress. Paused Gray/Default Work has been temporarily paused (for example, due to a break or waiting for materials). Completed Green/Success The job has been finished and all required tasks are done. Canceled Red/Error The job has been canceled and will not be performed. Row Actions Action Icon What It Does View Eye/Edit icon Navigates to the job's detail page. You can also click the job name to open it. Delete Trash icon Opens a confirmation dialog to delete the job. Disabled if the job has linked invoices or timesheet entries. The tooltip explains the restriction (for example, "There are 2 invoices linked to this job."). Pagination The list uses server-side pagination with a default page size of 10 jobs per page. Pagination controls at the bottom show the current page, total pages, and total records. Empty State If no jobs exist, the page shows: "No jobs found" with a button to create a new job. If a search returns no results: "No jobs found matching keyword." Creating a New Job To create a new job, click the New Job button. A multi-step wizard opens with three steps: Step 1: Job Details Field Required Description Client Yes Select the client this job is for. Choose from a dropdown of all active clients. When you select a client, the Property dropdown filters to show only that client's properties. Title Yes A descriptive name for the job (for example, "AC Unit Installation" or "Quarterly Maintenance"). Property Yes The property where the work will be performed. The dropdown is filtered based on the selected client. Checklist No Optionally assign a checklist of tasks that must be completed before the job can be marked done. Select from a dropdown of active checklists. Crew Yes The crew assigned to perform the work. When you select a crew, the system checks their availability for the next 90 days and displays a message showing how many dates have available time slots. Priority No A toggle switch to mark this job as high priority. Priority jobs display a flag icon in the Jobs List View. Click Next to proceed to Step 2. Step 2: Start Date A calendar picker appears where you select the start date for the job. The calendar shows up to one year in advance. Dates where the crew is available are shown normally. Dates where the crew is unavailable (due to availability settings or exceptions) are grayed out. After selecting a date, choose a Start Time from a dropdown of available time slots based on the crew's schedule. Click Next to proceed to Step 3. Step 3: End Date Similar to Step 2, select the end date and time. The end date/time must be after the start date/time. If the end date/time is before the start, you will see the error: "End date and time must be after start date and time." Tip: If you need to schedule a job outside the crew's normal availability, you can use the Override Availability option. The system will show a banner indicating whether the selected time is available, tentative, or unavailable for the crew. Click Create to finish. On success: "Job successfully created." Job Detail Page The detail page shows everything about a single job. Click any job in the list to open it. The page has a header, a set of tabs for different sections, and a sidebar with client information and communication tools. Page Header A Back button — returns to the Jobs List View (or to the appointment/estimate you came from, if applicable). The job title with a recurring badge if the job is part of a recurring series. A Status dropdown — click to change the job's status. When you select "Completed," the Complete Job modal opens instead of changing the status directly. A Timer dropdown — Start, Stop, or Resume a timer to track active work time. This is hidden for Completed and Canceled jobs. A More Actions menu (three dots icon) — opens a menu with additional actions (see More Actions below). Tabs The detail page has five tabs. The Checklist tab only appears if a checklist is assigned to the job. Tab What It Contains Overview Job details, billing information, services (labor items), products (material items), and customer feedback. Visits A history of visits and activity records related to this job. Checklist The assigned checklist with its items and completion status. Only shown if the job has a checklist. Recurring Schedule Details about the recurring schedule if this job is part of a repeating series. Invoices All invoices linked to this job. You can create new invoices or view existing ones. Overview Tab The Overview tab is the default view when you open a job. It contains several sections: Job Details Section Shows the core information about the job: Title — the job name. Date Created — when the job was created. Property — the property address with a link to open it on a map. Scheduled — the scheduled start and end date/time. Status — the current status. ETA — estimated time of arrival, if available. Notes — any notes or description added to the job. An Edit button allows you to modify these details (availability depends on job status). Billing Section Shows financial information: Bill to Client — optionally set a different client for billing purposes. You can add, change, or remove the billing client. Tax Rate — editable tax rate applied to the job total. Discount — editable discount amount or percentage. Subtotal, Tax, and Total — calculated amounts based on services, products, tax rate, and discount. Services Section (Labor Items) A table listing all services (labor items) added to the job: Column What It Shows Name The service name. Qty (Hours) The number of hours for this service. Unit Price The price per hour. Total Qty x Unit Price. You can Add Service , Edit (inline editing with Save/Cancel), or Delete services from this table. Products Section (Material Items) A table listing all products (material items) added to the job: Column What It Shows Name The product name. Qty (Quantity) How many units of this product are used. Unit Price The price per unit. Total Qty x Unit Price. Products show additional inventory information: available quantity, reorder level, and a stock status indicator. You can Add Product , Edit , or Delete products from this table. Customer Feedback Section If the job has received customer feedback, this section displays: The average rating (star rating). The latest feedback description . Crew-specific feedback ratings. Sidebar The right side of the job detail page shows a sidebar with: Client Information Client avatar and company name. Property type tags (Residential or Commercial). Phone number and email address. Billing address. An Edit billing details button. A Bill to Client button for setting an alternate billing client. Sidebar Tabs Below the client info, three tabs provide communication and file management: Tab What It Contains Chat Messages and conversation threads related to this job. Notes Internal notes about the job that your team can add and view. Attachments Files and documents attached to this job. Job Status Workflow Jobs follow a status workflow that tracks their progress. You can change a job's status using the status dropdown in the detail page header or through the More Actions menu. Status Flow The typical flow is: Draft → Scheduled → En Route → Started → Completed However, you can transition from any status to any other status. For example, a Started job can be Paused and then resumed (Started again), or a Scheduled job can be Canceled directly. Important: When you select Completed from the status dropdown, the system does not change the status immediately. Instead, it opens the Complete Job Modal where you must review and complete the checklist (if one is assigned) before the job can be finalized. See the "Completing a Job" section below for details. While the status is being updated, a message appears: "Updating status to [New Status]." On success: "Job successfully updated." Completing a Job When you change a job's status to Completed (either from the status dropdown or the More Actions menu), the Complete Job Modal opens. Jobs Without a Checklist If the job has no checklist assigned, the modal simply asks: "Are you sure you want to complete this job?" Click Complete Job to confirm. Jobs With a Checklist If the job has a checklist, the modal displays all checklist items and requires you to review them before completing: The modal shows: "Before completing review the checklists." Each checklist item appears with a checkbox . You must check every item to proceed. If a checklist item has Description Required enabled, a text area appears when you check that item. You must fill in the description before the job can be completed. If any items are not checked or required descriptions are empty, you will see the error: "This field is required." The Complete Job button is only enabled when all items are checked and all required descriptions are filled in. Click Complete Job to finalize, or Cancel to go back without completing. Tip: If you need to add notes about specific checklist items, the Description Required feature ensures that important details (like serial numbers, readings, or pass/fail notes) are captured before the job is closed. More Actions Menu The three-dots menu in the job detail header provides additional actions: Action What It Does Set Priority A toggle switch to mark or unmark the job as high priority. Priority jobs show a flag icon in the list. View on Scheduler Opens the job in the Scheduler view in a new browser tab so you can see it in the calendar context. View on Map Navigates to the Map view to show the job's property location. Complete Job Opens the Complete Job Modal (same as selecting Completed from the status dropdown). Schedule / Reschedule Opens a stepper modal to set or change the job's scheduled dates. Shows crew availability and detects scheduling conflicts. The label changes based on whether the job already has dates. Disabled for Completed and Canceled jobs. Cancel Job Opens a confirmation dialog: "Are you sure you want to cancel this job?" with Do Not Cancel and Cancel Job (red) buttons. Download PDF Downloads the job details as a PDF file for printing or sharing. Delete Opens a confirmation dialog: "Are you sure you want to delete this job?" Disabled if invoices or timesheet entries are linked (the tooltip explains the restriction). Detach from Series Only shown for jobs that are instances of a recurring series. Detaches this specific job from the series so it becomes a standalone job. On success: "Job has been detached from the recurring series." Editing a Job To edit a job's core details, click the Edit button on the job detail page or click the edit icon in the list view. A panel opens with the same fields as Step 1 of the create wizard (Client, Title, Property, Checklist, Crew, Priority). Note: In edit mode, you cannot change the scheduled dates directly. To change dates, use the Reschedule action from the More Actions menu, which opens a dedicated rescheduling wizard with availability checking. Edit Permissions by Status Job Status Edit Allowed? Draft, Scheduled Full editing — all fields can be changed. En Route, Started, Paused Limited editing — some fields may be read-only. Completed, Canceled Read-only — the edit button is disabled. On success: "Job successfully updated." On failure: "Failed to update job." Deleting a Job To delete a job, click the Delete button on the row in the list view or in the More Actions menu on the detail page. A confirmation dialog appears with the title Delete and the message: "Are you sure you want to delete this job?" Click Delete (red button) to confirm, or Cancel to go back. On success: "Job successfully deleted." On failure: "Failed to delete job." Warning: Deletion is permanent. You cannot delete a job that has linked invoices or timesheet entries. The system checks for these and disables the delete button if they exist. The tooltip explains the exact counts — for example: "There are 2 invoices linked to this job." or "There is 1 timesheet entry linked to this job." You must remove or delete those linked records first before the job can be deleted. Recurring Jobs Jobs can be set up as recurring , meaning they automatically repeat on a schedule (for example, weekly maintenance visits or monthly inspections). Recurring jobs have special features: A recurring badge icon appears next to the job name in the list view, indicating whether the job is a template or an instance of a recurring series. The Recurring Schedule tab on the detail page shows the repeating pattern (using RRULE scheduling rules). Individual instances of a recurring job can be detached from the series to become standalone jobs. This is useful when a specific occurrence needs different handling. Instances can be modified individually without affecting the rest of the series. The entire series can be canceled , or individual instances can be skipped by adding exceptions. Crew Availability and Scheduling When creating or rescheduling a job, the system checks the assigned crew's availability: The calendar highlights dates where the crew has available time slots. Dates where the crew is unavailable (due to work day settings, exceptions, or seasonal blocks) are grayed out. A message shows how many dates in the next 90 days have available slots. The system detects schedule conflicts — if the crew already has overlapping jobs scheduled, it will warn you. An Override Availability option lets you schedule the job outside normal hours when needed. A banner indicates whether the selected time is Available , Tentative , or Unavailable . Cross-Platform Usage Appointments Jobs can be created from appointments. When viewing an appointment, you can generate a job that inherits the client, property, and crew information from the appointment. Estimates Jobs can also be created from estimates using the Create Job from Estimate feature. This copies the line items, client, and property information from the estimate into the new job. Invoices Invoices can be generated from jobs. The Invoices tab on the job detail page shows all related invoices. Services and products from the job carry over to the invoice. Timesheets Time entries in timesheets can be linked to specific jobs. When a time entry references a job, the job name appears in the timesheet entry table. Jobs with linked timesheet entries cannot be deleted. Checklists When a checklist is assigned to a job, the job gets its own independent copy of the checklist items. Completing or modifying checklist items on the job does not affect the original checklist template. The Checklist tab appears only when a checklist is assigned. Scheduler and Map Jobs appear on the Scheduler (calendar view) and Map view based on their scheduled dates and property location. You can navigate to either view directly from the More Actions menu. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It View all jobs Navigate to Menu > Jobs Create a new job Click New Job > fill in details, start date, end date > click Create View a job's details Click the job name or the view icon in the list Edit a job Open the job > click Edit on the Overview tab > make changes > save Change a job's status Open the job > click the status dropdown in the header > select the new status Complete a job Select Completed from the status dropdown or More Actions > review checklist > click Complete Job Mark a job as priority Open the job > More Actions > toggle Set Priority Reschedule a job Open the job > More Actions > Schedule/Reschedule > select new dates Cancel a job Open the job > More Actions > Cancel Job > confirm Add a service to a job Open the job > Overview tab > click Add Service in the Services section Add a product to a job Open the job > Overview tab > click Add Product in the Products section Download a job as PDF Open the job > More Actions > Download PDF Delete a job Click the trash icon in the list or More Actions > Delete > confirm (only if no invoices/timesheets linked) View a job on the Scheduler Open the job > More Actions > View on Scheduler View a job on the Map Open the job > More Actions > View on Map Detach from a recurring series Open the recurring job > More Actions > Detach from Series > confirm Filter by status Click the Status filter button > check the statuses you want to see Filter by date range Use the Date Range filter to select a time period Set a billing client Open the job > sidebar > click Bill to Client > select a client
invoices-management: Invoices Management Overview The Invoices Management section is where you create, send, and track invoices for your clients. An invoice is a billing document generated from a job, listing the services performed and products used along with pricing, taxes, discounts, and payment terms. The system supports the full invoice lifecycle from draft creation through client delivery, payment recording, and completion. From this section you can: Create new invoices linked to jobs, with service and product line items auto-populated from the job. Edit invoice details, services, products, taxes, discounts, and deposit requirements. Send invoices to clients via email and/or SMS with customizable messages. Track status through the invoice lifecycle (Draft, Sent, Viewed, Paid). Record payments with multiple payment methods and statuses. Download PDF copies of invoices for offline sharing or records. Delete invoices that are no longer needed. Communicate with clients through built-in chat, notes, and attachments. Add comments visible to clients on the invoice document. Important: Once an invoice is marked as Paid , it becomes fully read-only. You cannot edit services, products, billing details, or invoice information. You can still add, edit, or delete payments and use the communication tools (chat, notes, attachments). Invoices List View The Invoices List View is the main view for managing all your invoices. To access it, navigate to Menu > Invoices . When you open the Invoices List View, you will see invoices grouped by job . Each job group shows the job name as a header, a "Job Details" button to navigate to that job, and the total amount for all invoices in that group. Within each group, invoices are displayed in a table with the following columns: Column What It Means Number The auto-generated invoice number. Click the number to navigate to the Invoice Detail View. If the invoice was created via Quick Add (incomplete), a warning icon appears next to the number indicating additional information is needed. Issue Date The date the invoice was issued, formatted as Mon DD, YYYY (e.g., "Feb 10, 2026"). Shows "—" if no issue date was set. Due Date The date payment is due, formatted as Mon DD, YYYY . Shows "—" if no due date was set. Total Balance Two lines: the top line shows the invoice total, and the bottom line shows the remaining balance. If fully paid, the balance shows $0.00 in green. If unpaid, the balance due is shown in red. Status The current status of the invoice, shown as a color-coded tag. If the invoice has a chat conversation, a chat icon also appears. A blue icon with a red badge indicates unread messages. Actions Quick-action buttons: edit (pencil icon to open detail view) and delete (trash icon). Invoice Statuses Every invoice has one of the following statuses. These statuses help you track where each invoice is in the billing process: Status Color Meaning Draft Purple The invoice is being prepared and has not been sent to the client. You can freely edit all details. Sent Orange The invoice has been sent to the client via email and/or SMS. The invoice can still be edited. Viewed Gray The client has opened and viewed the invoice. The invoice can still be edited. Payment in Progress Orange A payment is being processed through a payment gateway. The invoice can still be edited. Paid Green The invoice has been fully paid. The invoice becomes completely read-only (except for payments and communication). Sorting the List You can sort the invoices list by clicking on the column headers for Number , Due Date , Total Balance , or Status . Click once to sort in ascending order and click again to sort in descending order. Searching Use the search bar at the top of the Invoices List View to search for invoices by keyword. Simply type your search term and the results will update automatically. The search resets to page 1 when the search term changes. Filtering by Status Click the "Status" filter button to open a popover with status checkboxes. You can select one or more statuses to show only invoices matching those statuses: Draft Sent Viewed Paid When filters are active, a badge with the number of active filters appears on the button. Click "Reset filter" in the popover to clear all filters. Pagination The Invoices List View supports server-side pagination. You can change the number of invoices displayed per page (default is 10) and navigate between pages using the pagination controls at the bottom of the table. Chat Icon in Status Column If an invoice has an associated chat conversation, a chat icon appears next to the status tag: Gray background — A chat exists but there are no unread messages. Clicking navigates to the Inbox. Blue background with red badge — There are unread messages. The badge shows the count (up to "99+"). Clicking navigates to the Inbox with the conversation pre-selected. 1. Creating a New Invoice To create a new invoice, follow these steps: Step 1: Open the New Invoice Form Click the "+ New Invoice" button (blue) located in the top-right corner of the Invoices List View. A drawer panel will slide open from the right side of the screen with a two-step wizard. Tip: If the list is empty (no invoices have been created yet), you will see a "Create first invoice" button in the center of the page. Step 2: Fill In the Invoice Details (Step 1 of 2) The first step of the wizard is titled "Invoice Details" and contains the following fields: Field Required? Description Details No A text area (3 rows) for entering a description or notes regarding the invoice. Placeholder: "Enter description or details regarding this invoice" . Client Yes Select the client this invoice is for from the dropdown. You can search by company name or email. If the client does not exist yet, use the Quick Add option to create one without leaving the form. Property Yes Select the property where the work was performed. Filtered by the selected client. Disabled until a client is selected (shows "Select client first"). You can Quick Add a new property. Job Yes Select the job this invoice is associated with. Filtered by the selected client and property. Disabled until a property is selected (shows "Select property first"). You can Quick Add a new job. When a job is selected, its existing services and products are automatically loaded into Step 2. Issue Date Yes The date the invoice is issued. Defaults to today's date. Cannot be after the due date. Due Date Yes The date payment is due. Must be on or after the issue date. After filling in all required fields, click "Next Step" to proceed. The system validates your entries — if any required field is missing, an error message appears next to that field. Note: The cascading dropdown pattern means you must select a Client first, then a Property, then a Job. Each dropdown filters based on the previous selection. Step 3: Review Service Items (Step 2 of 2) The second step is titled "Service Items" and shows services and products that were automatically loaded from the selected job. You can: Review the auto-populated services and products from the job. Add additional services or products from your catalog. Edit quantities, hours, rates, and prices for any item. Remove items you don't want to include on this invoice. Step 4: Save the Invoice You have two options for saving: "Create Invoice" (blue button) — Saves the invoice with all service items and products. A loading indicator appears while the operation completes. "Skip" (text button) — Creates the invoice without any service items. You can add items later from the Invoice Detail View. Both options create the invoice in Draft status. On success, a toast message appears: "Invoice created successfully" . The new invoice will appear in your Invoices List View. Click "Back" to return to Step 1 to modify the invoice details before saving. 2. Invoice Detail View The Invoice Detail View is where you manage all aspects of a single invoice. To access it, click on any invoice's number or edit icon in the Invoices List View. The Detail View has two main areas: Main content area (left) — Contains the Job Details and Payments tabs. Client sidebar (right) — Shows client information and communication tools (Chat, Notes, Attachments). Header Area The header displays: Back button — Returns to the Invoices List View. If you arrived from an Appointment or Estimate, it returns you there instead. Invoice number/name — The title of the invoice. Status dropdown — A color-coded tag showing the current status. Click to change the status (see Section 5). Send Invoice button — Opens the Send Invoice modal (see Section 6). Disabled if the invoice is Paid or has no service items. More actions menu (three-dot icon) — Contains: Edit Invoice, Download PDF, and Delete. Edit and Delete are hidden when the invoice is Paid. 2.1 Job Details Tab The Job Details tab is the default tab and displays all the invoice's editable content. For Paid Invoices When an invoice is in Paid status, the Job Details tab shows a read-only PDF-style preview of the invoice. No editing is allowed. All information is displayed in a formatted layout. For Unpaid Invoices (Editable) When the invoice is in Draft, Sent, Viewed, or Payment in Progress status, the following sections are displayed: Overview Section A card showing the invoice's basic information with an "Edit" button (pencil icon) to open the Edit Invoice drawer. The card displays: Field Description Invoice Number The auto-generated invoice number, or "Auto-generated" if not yet assigned. Issue Date The date the invoice was issued. Due Date The payment due date. Job The associated job name. Property The full property address with a Residential or Business tag if applicable. Services Section Lists all labor/service line items. Each service is displayed in a table with inline editing capabilities: Column Description SKU The service's unique code. Service Service name (bold) with description below in gray text. Hours Two inline inputs: hours and minutes (max 59). Editable directly in the table. Rate Dollar amount per hour. Editable inline with a dollar sign prefix. Total Calculated automatically: (hours + minutes/60) x rate. Updates in real time. Actions Update icon (shows change summary popover) and delete icon (removes item from invoice). When you make changes to services, the footer changes from showing an "Add Services" button to showing "Cancel Edit" and "Save Changes" buttons. Click "Save Changes" to apply your edits, or "Cancel Edit" to discard them. The Change Summary Popover (accessible via the update icon) shows a field-by-field comparison of what you changed (old value vs. new value in green), with a "Refresh" icon to reset individual fields and a "Reset All" button to discard all changes. Products Section Lists all material/product line items with inline editing: Column Description SKU The product's unique code. Product Product name (bold) with description below in gray text. Qty Quantity with minus/plus buttons and an editable input. Validates against available inventory (see below). Price Unit price. Editable inline with a dollar sign prefix. Total Calculated automatically: quantity x price. Updates in real time. Actions Update icon, inventory status indicator, and delete icon. Inventory Validation: For products with inventory tracking enabled, the system enforces maximum quantity limits based on available stock. The plus button is disabled when at maximum. If inventory tracking is disabled for a product, unlimited quantities are allowed. An inventory status indicator shows stock level: green (in stock), yellow (low stock), or red (out of stock). Bill Section Displays the billing summary with the following line items: Line Item Description Subtotal The sum of all service and product totals, with the number of items shown (e.g., "3 items"). Tax A dropdown to select a tax rate from your saved rates (format: "Name - Rate%"). The first option is "None" (no tax). A settings icon opens the Tax Management Modal to create, edit, or delete tax rates. Discount An editable dollar amount field for discounts. Required Deposit An editable dollar amount field for required deposits. Total The grand total (Subtotal + Tax - Discount), calculated by the backend and displayed in bold. When you modify the tax rate, discount, or deposit, "Cancel" and "Apply Changes" buttons appear. Click "Apply Changes" to save, or "Cancel" to revert to the original values. Comments to Client Section A text area where you can write a message that will appear on the invoice document when the client views it. Features: 4-row text area with placeholder: "Please take a look at the invoice and don't hesitate to ask questions" Character counter showing current length out of a 400-character maximum A "Save" button appears when you modify the text (only enabled when text is not empty) Disabled when the invoice is Paid Help text: "This text will be displayed in the invoice" 2.2 Payments Tab The Payments tab is accessed by clicking the "Payments" tab in the segmented control at the top of the Detail View. Summary Cards Three cards at the top provide a financial overview: Card Description Total The total invoice amount. Required Deposit The deposit amount required before work begins. Remaining The outstanding balance due (Total - Total Paid). Payments History A table showing all recorded payments with an "Add Payment" button in the header. Each payment shows: Column Description Date The transaction date. If the payment was created via Quick Add, a warning tooltip appears. Amount The payment amount in dollars (bold). Type The payment method (Check, Cash, Credit Card, Bank Transfer, Other, or Payment Processor). Note Any notes associated with the payment, or "—" if none. Actions Edit (pencil icon) and delete (trash icon) buttons for each payment. If no payments have been recorded, the message "No payments yet" is displayed. 2.3 Client Sidebar The right sidebar on the Invoice Detail View shows client information and communication tools (identical pattern to the Estimates sidebar). Client Information Client avatar (or initials) with company name Property type tags (Residential/Business) Phone number Email address Billing address Communication Tabs Tab Description Chat Real-time messaging with the client. Messages appear in the client's portal and your Inbox. Notes Internal notes visible only to your team. Attachments Upload and manage files associated with this invoice. 3. Editing an Invoice To update the basic details of an existing invoice: Step 1: Open the Invoice for Editing From the Invoice Detail View, click the three-dot menu in the header and select "Edit Invoice" . A drawer panel slides open from the right. Note: The "Edit Invoice" option is not available when the invoice is in Paid status. Step 2: Make Your Changes The edit panel shows the following fields: Field Required? Description Details Yes The invoice title/description. Placeholder: "Enter invoice title" . Issue Date No The invoice issue date. Due Date No The payment due date. Step 3: Save Your Changes Click "Apply Changes" to save. A loading indicator appears while saving. Click "Cancel" to close without saving. Editing Services, Products, and Billing Inline In addition to the Edit Invoice drawer, you can edit services, products, and billing details directly inline on the Job Details tab (see Section 2.1). This inline editing approach allows you to modify hours, rates, quantities, prices, taxes, discounts, and deposits without opening a separate form. 4. Deleting an Invoice Invoices can be permanently deleted from two places: From the Invoices List View Locate the invoice in the list. Click the delete icon (trash icon) in the Actions column. A confirmation dialog appears with the title "Delete Invoice" and the message: "Are you sure you want to delete this invoice?" Click "Delete" (red button) to confirm, or "Cancel" to go back. From the Invoice Detail View Open the Invoice Detail View. Click the three-dot menu in the header. Select "Delete" . Confirm in the dialog. After deletion, you are redirected to the Invoices List View. Note: Invoices in Paid status cannot be deleted. The delete option is hidden from the menu when the invoice is Paid. Warning: Permanent deletion cannot be undone. Once an invoice is deleted, all of its information, service items, products, payments, notes, and attachments are removed from the system permanently. 5. Changing Invoice Status You can change an invoice's status using the status dropdown in the Invoice Detail View header. Click the color-coded status tag to open the dropdown, then select the new status. Available Statuses Draft (purple) — Initial state, fully editable. Sent (orange) — Sent to client, still editable. Viewed (gray) — Client has viewed, still editable. Paid (green) — Fully paid, becomes read-only. While the status is being updated, a spinner appears with the message "Updating status to [new status]..." . On success, a toast appears: "Invoice successfully updated" . Paid Status Restrictions When an invoice is marked as Paid : The Job Details tab shows a read-only PDF-style preview instead of editable fields. The "Edit Invoice" and "Delete" options are hidden from the three-dot menu. The "Send Invoice" button is hidden. Services, products, and billing details cannot be modified. The Comments to Client field is disabled. Payments can still be added, edited, or deleted. Chat, Notes, and Attachments remain fully functional. 6. Sending an Invoice to a Client When your invoice is ready for the client, you can send it via email and/or SMS. Step 1: Open the Send Invoice Modal From the Invoice Detail View, click the "Send Invoice" button in the header. A full-screen drawer opens with the submission form. Note: The Send Invoice button is disabled if the invoice has no service items. Tooltip: "At least one service item required to send invoice" . Step 2: Configure Delivery Channels Choose how the invoice will be delivered: Email — Check to send via email. Enabled by default. SMS — Check to send via text message. Enabled by default. At least one delivery channel must be selected. Step 3: Customize the Message Field Default Value Description Subject "Your invoice is ready!" The email subject line. Required if sending via email. Email Message Pre-filled with a standard message including a link to view the invoice online. Fully customizable. A "Reset Text" link restores the default message. Send me the copy Checked Sends a copy of the email to your own address. SMS Message Same default as email message. Customizable independently from the email message. Using Template Variables Press the / key while typing in any text field to open a variable insertion menu: Category Variable Inserts General {{currentDate}} Today's date Client {{name}} Client's full name Client {{firstname}} Client's first name Client {{lastname}} Client's last name Client {{email}} Client's email address Client {{mobile}} Client's mobile number Step 4: Preview and Send A Message Preview section below the form shows how the message looks with all variables replaced. Toggle between Email and SMS previews. Click the "Preview Invoice" button (arrow icon) in the drawer header to see a full preview of the invoice as the client will see it. Click "Send Invoice" to send, or "Cancel" to close without sending. 7. Recording Payments Payments are recorded in the Payments tab of the Invoice Detail View. You can record payments even when the invoice is in Paid status. 7.1 Adding a Payment Navigate to the Payments tab. Click the "Add Payment" button. A drawer opens with the following fields: Field Required? Description Amount Yes The payment amount in dollars. Enter as a decimal (e.g., 250.00 ). Must be greater than 0. Transaction Date Yes The date the payment was received. Defaults to today. Payment Method Yes Select from: Check, Cash, Credit Card, Bank Transfer, or Other. Status Yes The payment status. Options: Completed (default), Pending, Failed, Refunded, Voided. Payment Reference No A reference number such as a check number, transaction ID, or confirmation code. Notes No A text area (4 rows) for any notes about the payment. Click "Add Payment" to save. On success: "Payment added to invoice" . 7.2 Editing a Payment In the Payments History table, click the edit icon (pencil) for the payment. The same drawer opens, pre-filled with the payment's current data. The title changes to "Edit Payment" . Make your changes and click "Update Payment" . On success: "Payment successfully updated" . 7.3 Deleting a Payment In the Payments History table, click the delete icon (trash) for the payment. A confirmation dialog appears: "Are you sure you want to delete this payment?" Click "Delete" to confirm. On success: "Payment successfully deleted" . 8. Downloading an Invoice as PDF To download a PDF copy of an invoice: Open the Invoice Detail View. Click the three-dot menu in the header. Select "Download PDF" . The system generates and downloads a PDF file named invoice_[InvoiceID].pdf . The PDF can be shared with clients via other channels (print, email attachments, etc.) or kept for your records. 9. Tax Management Tax rates are managed from within the Bill section of an invoice. Click the settings icon next to the tax rate dropdown to open the Tax Management Modal . From this modal you can: View all saved tax rates. Create a new tax rate by entering a name and percentage. Edit existing tax rates. Delete tax rates that are no longer needed. Select a tax rate to apply it to the current invoice. Tax rates are stored as basis points (e.g., 850 = 8.50%) and displayed as percentages. The tax is calculated only on taxable items — the backend determines the taxable total and applies the rate. 10. Searching and Filtering Invoices 10.1 Quick Search The search bar at the top of the Invoices List View allows you to search by keyword. Type your search term and the results update automatically. The search resets to page 1. 10.2 Status Filter Click the "Status" button to open the filter popover. Select one or more statuses: Filter Option What It Shows Draft Invoices being prepared, not yet sent. Sent Invoices sent to clients, awaiting payment. Viewed Invoices the client has opened and viewed. Paid Invoices that have been fully paid. A badge shows the number of active filters. Click "Reset filter" to clear all. Tip: Combine search and status filters for precise results. For example, filter by "Sent" to see all outstanding invoices awaiting payment. 11. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It Create a new invoice Click "+ New Invoice" on the List View, fill in the two-step wizard, and click "Create Invoice" . Edit invoice details Open the Detail View, click the three-dot menu > "Edit Invoice" , make changes, and click "Apply Changes" . Edit services inline On the Job Details tab, modify hours, rates, or remove items directly in the Services table. Click "Save Changes" . Edit products inline On the Job Details tab, modify quantities, prices, or remove items directly in the Products table. Click "Save Changes" . Add services or products On the Job Details tab, click "Add Services" or "Add Products" . Modify taxes/discounts In the Bill section, change the tax rate, discount, or deposit, then click "Apply Changes" . Change invoice status Click the status tag in the header and select the new status. Send to client Click "Send Invoice" , configure email/SMS, customize the message, and click "Send Invoice" . Record a payment Go to the Payments tab, click "Add Payment" , fill in the details, and click "Add Payment" . Download as PDF Click the three-dot menu > "Download PDF" . Delete an invoice Click the trash icon on the List View, or three-dot menu > "Delete" on the Detail View. Confirm in the dialog. Add comments for client In the Comments to Client section on the Job Details tab, type your message and click "Save" . Chat with client Use the Chat tab in the client sidebar. Add internal notes Use the Notes tab in the client sidebar. Upload attachments Use the Attachments tab in the client sidebar. Manage tax rates In the Bill section, click the settings icon next to the tax dropdown to open Tax Management.
timesheets-management: Timesheets Management Overview Timesheets are how you track working hours for your team. Each timesheet belongs to a specific user and a specific date, and contains one or more time entries that record what the person did and for how long. Time entries are categorized by type — whether the person was traveling to a job site (en route), performing work at a job site (job time), or working in the office (office time). Timesheets follow an approval workflow : they start as a Draft , get Submitted by the user for review, and are then either Approved or Rejected by an administrator or owner. This process ensures that hours are reviewed and verified before they are finalized. From this section you can: Create new timesheets for any user and date. Add time entries to track specific blocks of work, travel, or office time. Link and unlink time entries between timesheets. Submit timesheets for approval. Approve or reject timesheets (admins and owners only). Delete timesheets and time entries (with restrictions on approved timesheets). View analytics with summary cards showing total hours, en route time, office time, and job time. Search and filter by date range, user, and status. Important: Once a timesheet is approved , it becomes read-only. You cannot edit, delete, or unlink time entries from an approved timesheet. This protects finalized records from accidental modifications. Timesheets List View The Timesheets List View is the main view for managing all timesheets. To access it, navigate to Menu > Timesheets . Summary Cards At the top of the page, four summary cards show aggregated statistics for the current view. Each card shows a value and a percentage change compared to the previous week (with an up or down arrow indicator): Card Icon What It Shows Total Hours Timer The combined total of all hours across all displayed timesheets. Shows percentage change compared to the previous week. Total En Route Hours Truck The total travel time (en route hours) across all displayed timesheets. Total Office Time Building The total office hours across all displayed timesheets. Total Job Time Wrench The total on-site job hours across all displayed timesheets. Tip: The summary cards update based on your active filters. If you filter by a specific user or date range, the cards show the totals for that filtered view only. Controls and Filters Below the summary cards you will find: A New Timesheet button — click to open the create timesheet form. A Search bar — type a keyword to search across timesheets (with a slight delay for performance). A Date Range filter — select a predefined range or custom date range. Defaults to "All Time." A User filter (hidden for crew members) — a multi-select dropdown to filter timesheets by specific users. Shows up to 100 users. A Status filter — a popover with checkboxes for Draft, Pending, Approved, and Rejected. Shows a badge with the count of selected filters. Includes a "Reset filter" button to clear all selections. Note: Crew members can only see their own timesheets. The User filter is hidden for crew members since they cannot view other people's timesheets. Admins and Owners can see all timesheets across the account. Table Columns Column What It Shows User The user's avatar and full name. Timesheet Date The date the timesheet covers, formatted as short month, day, and year (for example, "Feb 19, 2026"). This column is sortable. Office Time Total office hours for this timesheet, displayed in HH:MM format (for example, "02:30"). Job Time Total on-site job hours for this timesheet, displayed in HH:MM format. En Route Time Total travel hours for this timesheet, displayed in HH:MM format. Status The timesheet's current status as a colored tag. This column is sortable. Actions Edit and Delete buttons (see Row Actions below). Status Tags Status Tag Color Meaning Draft Purple/Indigo background The timesheet is being worked on and has not been submitted for review. Pending Gray background The timesheet has been submitted and is waiting for an admin/owner to review it. Approved Green background The timesheet has been reviewed and approved. It is now read-only. Rejected Red background The timesheet has been reviewed and rejected. The user should make corrections and resubmit. Row Actions Action Icon What It Does Edit Pencil icon Navigates to the timesheet's detail page where you can view and manage all time entries. Delete Trash icon Deletes the timesheet. This button is disabled for approved timesheets — the tooltip reads: "This timesheet has been approved and cannot be deleted." Pagination The list uses server-side pagination with a default page size of 10 timesheets per page. Pagination controls at the bottom let you change pages and adjust the page size. Empty State If no timesheets match your search or filters, the table shows: "No Timesheets Found." Creating a New Timesheet To create a new timesheet: Step 1: Click the New Timesheet button at the top of the Timesheets List View. A modal dialog opens. Step 2: Fill in the timesheet details. New Timesheet Form Fields Field Required Description Date Yes The date this timesheet covers. Click the field to open a date picker. You cannot select a future date — the maximum date is today. Error if empty: "Date is required." User Yes The user this timesheet belongs to. Select from a dropdown of all users. Supports Quick Add to create new users inline. Error if empty: "User is required." Note: Crew members can only create timesheets for themselves. The User dropdown is disabled for crew members with the helper text: "Crew members can only create their own timesheets." Step 3: Click Add Timesheet to create the timesheet. The button shows "Saving..." while processing. On success: "Timesheet successfully created." On failure: "Failed to create a timesheet." After creation, you will be taken to the timesheet's detail page where you can start adding time entries. Timesheet Detail Page The detail page is where you manage a single timesheet and all its time entries. To access it, click the Edit button on any timesheet row, or navigate directly after creating a new timesheet. Page Header At the top of the detail page you will see: A Back button (chevron icon) — navigates back to the Timesheets List View. The timesheet description or the user's full name as the page title. The user's avatar and name with their crew affiliation. The status — displayed as a dropdown for admins/owners (who can change it directly) or as a read-only label for crew members. Overview Section A summary table shows key timesheet information: Field What It Shows Date Created The date the timesheet covers, formatted as short month, day, year. Crew The crew name with its color indicator. Shows "No Crew" if the user is not in any crew. User The user's avatar, name, and crew affiliation. Time Entries Section Below the overview, the time entries are organized into two tabs: Tab What It Shows Linked Entries Time entries that are linked to this specific timesheet. These are the entries that count toward this timesheet's totals. Unlinked Entries Time entries that exist but are not linked to any timesheet. You can link them to this timesheet or to a different one. This tab is hidden when the timesheet is Approved. Above the entries table you will find: A Search bar to filter entries. A Date Range filter to narrow entries by date. An Activity filter to show only specific entry types (All Activity, En Route, Work Time, Office Time). An Add Time button (plus icon) to create a new time entry. An Import Time button (download icon) to import entries from jobs. Time Entry Columns Column What It Shows Job The name of the job this entry is associated with, or "-" if no job (for example, office time entries). User The user's avatar and full name. Date The date of the entry in short format. Description A truncated description of what was done during this time period. Type A colored tag indicating the entry type: En Route (purple), Work Time (orange), or Office Time (gray). Time The duration of the entry in HH:MM format (for example, "02:30" for two and a half hours). Actions Action buttons that vary depending on the tab (see below). Linked Entry Actions Action Icon What It Does View Note icon Shows a popover with the full description and the time range (for example, "08:30 - 17:00"). Edit Pencil icon Opens the Add Time modal in edit mode to modify this entry. Disabled if the timesheet is approved. Unlink Unlink icon Removes this entry from the timesheet (it becomes an unlinked entry). Disabled if the timesheet is approved. A confirmation dialog appears first. Unlinked Entry Actions Action Icon What It Does Link Link icon Shows a popover with two options: Link (links directly to this timesheet) or Select Another (opens a modal to choose a different timesheet or create a new one). View Note icon Shows a popover with the full description and time range. Edit Pencil icon Opens the Add Time modal in edit mode. Delete Trash icon Permanently deletes the time entry. A confirmation dialog appears first. Adding a Time Entry Time entries are the individual blocks of work that make up a timesheet. To add a time entry: Step 1: On the timesheet detail page, click the Add Time button (plus icon). The Add Time modal opens. Step 2: Fill in the entry details. Add Time Entry Form Fields Field Required Description Date Yes The date this work was performed. Cannot be a future date. Error if empty: "Date is required." Job Conditional The job associated with this entry. Required for En Route and Work Time entries. Not required for Office Time entries (since office work is not tied to a specific job). Select from a dropdown of all jobs. Supports Quick Add to create a new job inline. Error: "Job is required for this time type." Time Type Yes Select the type of work being recorded. Three options displayed as tab-like buttons: En Route (traveling to a job), Work Time (working on a job site), or Office Time (working in the office). From Yes The start time of the work period. Uses a time selector. If the date is today, you cannot select a future time. Error if empty: "From time is required." Error if future: "Cannot select future time." To Yes The end time of the work period. Must be after the From time. Error if empty: "To time is required." Error if before From: "To time must be after from time." Error if future: "Cannot select future time." Description Yes A description of what was done during this time period. Multi-line text area (4 rows). Placeholder: "Describe what have been done." Always required. Step 3: Click Save to add the entry. The button shows "Saving..." while processing. On success: "Time entry successfully created." On failure: "Failed to save time entry." Editing a Time Entry Click the Edit (pencil icon) on any time entry to open the same Add Time modal pre-filled with the entry's current data. Modify any field and click Save to update it. On success: "Time entry successfully updated." Note: You cannot edit time entries on an approved timesheet. The edit button is disabled for approved entries. Linking and Unlinking Time Entries Time entries can exist independently (unlinked) or be associated with a specific timesheet (linked). This flexibility allows you to create time entries first and organize them into timesheets later. Linking an Unlinked Entry Go to the timesheet detail page and switch to the Unlinked Entries tab. Click the Link icon on the entry you want to add. A popover appears with two options: Link — Immediately links the entry to the current timesheet. Select Another — Opens a modal where you can choose an existing timesheet or create a new one to link the entry to. On success: "Time entry successfully linked." Link Entry Modal (Select Another) When you click Select Another , a modal opens with two options: Option What It Does Existing Timesheet Search for and select an existing timesheet from a dropdown. Shows timesheets as "User Name - Date" format. Create New Timesheet Create a new timesheet on the spot by selecting a date and user, then link the entry to it. Unlinking a Linked Entry On the Linked Entries tab, click the Unlink icon on the entry. A confirmation dialog appears with the title Unlink Time Entry and a confirmation message. Click Unlink to confirm, or Cancel to go back. On success: "Time entry successfully unlinked." The entry moves to the Unlinked Entries tab. Warning: You cannot unlink entries from an approved timesheet. The unlink button is disabled for approved timesheets. Timesheet Approval Workflow Timesheets follow a status-based workflow that ensures hours are reviewed before they are finalized. The flow works as follows: Status Flow Draft → (Submit) → Pending → (Approve) → Approved Pending → (Reject) → Rejected Pending → (Withdraw) → Draft Available Actions by Status and Role Status Who Available Actions Draft Timesheet owner Submit Timesheet — Submits for review (status changes to Pending). Pending Timesheet owner Change to Draft — Withdraws the submission (status changes back to Draft). Pending Admin or Owner Approve Timesheet (green button) — Approves the timesheet (status changes to Approved). Reject Timesheet (red button) — Rejects the timesheet (status changes to Rejected). Approved Everyone No actions available. The timesheet is finalized and read-only. Rejected Timesheet owner The user should correct the timesheet and resubmit. Note: Admins and Owners can also change the status directly using a status dropdown in the detail page header, without using the action buttons. Crew members see a read-only status label instead of the dropdown. Confirmation Dialogs All status changes require confirmation before they are applied: Action Dialog Title Confirm Button Style Submit "Submit Timesheet" Submit Info (blue) Approve "Approve Timesheet" Approve Info (blue) Reject "Reject Timesheet" Reject Danger (red) Withdraw "Change to Draft" Change to Draft Info (blue) Toast Messages for Status Changes Action Success Message Error Message Submit "Timesheet successfully submitted" "Failed to submit timesheet" Approve "Timesheet successfully approved" "Failed to approve timesheet" Reject "Timesheet successfully rejected" "Failed to reject timesheet" Withdraw "Timesheet successfully withdrawn" "Failed to withdraw timesheet" Deleting Timesheets and Time Entries Deleting a Timesheet To delete a timesheet, click the Delete (trash icon) on the timesheet row in the list view. A confirmation dialog appears. On success: "Timesheet successfully deleted." On failure: "Failed to delete timesheet." Warning: You cannot delete a timesheet that has been Approved . The delete button is disabled and the tooltip reads: "This timesheet has been approved and cannot be deleted." Deleting a Time Entry You can delete time entries from the Unlinked Entries tab by clicking the Delete (trash icon). The confirmation dialog title depends on the context: For unlinked entries: Title is "Permanently Delete Time Entry" with a message explaining the entry will be permanently removed. For linked entries: Title is "Delete Time Entry" with a message explaining the entry will be removed from the timesheet. On success: "Time entry successfully deleted." On failure: "Failed to delete time entry." Role-Based Permissions Access to timesheet features varies by user role: Capability Crew Member Admin Owner View timesheets Own only All All Create timesheets Own only For any user For any user Edit time entries Own (if Draft) All (if not Approved) All (if not Approved) Submit for approval Yes (own) Yes Yes Approve / Reject No Yes Yes Change status via dropdown No Yes Yes Delete timesheets Own (if not Approved) All (if not Approved) All (if not Approved) See User filter No Yes Yes See unlinked entries of others No (own only) Yes Yes Cross-Platform Usage Jobs Time entries are tied to specific jobs through the Job field. When you select a job for a time entry (En Route or Work Time type), the entry becomes associated with that job's record. Job names appear in the time entry table's Job column. Users and Crews Each timesheet belongs to a specific user. The timesheet detail page shows the user's crew affiliation. When viewing crew-related timesheets, crew names appear with their assigned color indicators. Reports Timesheet data feeds into the Reports section, where you can view daily and monthly views of hours worked. Reports offer additional breakdowns by employee, date range, job, and client, and support PDF generation for printing or sharing. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It View all timesheets Navigate to Menu > Timesheets Create a new timesheet Click New Timesheet > select date and user > click Save Add a time entry Open a timesheet > click Add Time (plus icon) > fill in details > click Save Edit a time entry Click the pencil icon on the entry > modify fields > click Save Link an unlinked entry Switch to Unlinked Entries tab > click the link icon > choose Link or Select Another Unlink an entry On Linked Entries tab > click the unlink icon > confirm Submit a timesheet for approval Open the timesheet > click Submit Timesheet > confirm Approve a timesheet Open the pending timesheet > click Approve Timesheet (green) > confirm Reject a timesheet Open the pending timesheet > click Reject Timesheet (red) > confirm Withdraw a submission Open your pending timesheet > click Change to Draft > confirm Delete a timesheet Click the trash icon on the row (only if not approved) > confirm Delete a time entry Click the trash icon on an unlinked entry > confirm Filter by date range Use the Date Range filter above the table Filter by user Use the User dropdown (admins/owners only) Filter by status Click the Status filter and check the statuses you want to see View a time entry's details Click the note icon on the entry to see the full description and time range
settings-configuration: Settings & Configuration Overview The Settings section is your central hub for configuring every aspect of your DHS account. It controls your company information, user profile, availability, notifications, business rules, payment processing, and more. To access Settings, navigate to Menu > Settings . The Settings page uses a sidebar + content layout: a navigation sidebar on the left lists all available sections, and the main content area on the right displays the selected section's form. The Settings sidebar includes the following sections: Section Description Account Company information, client contact details, and billing address. Profile Your personal information, password, language, and profile picture. Availability Service availability schedule by day-of-week, exception dates, and seasonal exceptions. Dashboard Dashboard display preferences such as list limits and upcoming jobs window. Notifications Notification channel preferences (Email, SMS, Push, Desktop) per category. Business Counter settings, payment disclaimers, Zelle payment options, crew settings, privacy, and tax rates. Timesheet Settings Automatic timesheet linking configuration (account-level and personal override). Accept Payments Stripe Connect integration for accepting credit card payments from clients. Note: Every settings section has a Save button and a Reset button. Changes are not saved until you click Save. The Reset button reverts all fields to their last saved values. 1. Account Settings The Account section manages your company's core information. This is the default section shown when you open Settings. 1.1 Company Information The top of the page shows a profile banner with your company's initial and name. Below it is the Company Information form: Field Required Description Company Name Yes Your business name as it appears throughout the system. Must be 2–100 characters. Email Yes The primary email address for your company account. Must be unique across the system (validated on blur). Maximum 254 characters. Website No Your company website URL. Accepts URLs with or without the https:// prefix. Maximum 255 characters. Timezone Yes Your business timezone. Select from over 100 timezone options. This affects how dates and times are displayed throughout the platform. 1.2 Client Information Optional contact information that can be displayed to your clients: Field Required Description Client Phone No A phone number your clients can use to reach you. Must be in valid phone format if provided. Client Email No An email address your clients can use to contact you. Validated for proper email format. 1.3 Billing Information Your company's billing address, used on invoices and financial documents. All billing fields are optional : Field Validation Billing Company Name Maximum 100 characters. Contact Name Maximum 50 characters. Address 1 5–100 characters. Address 2 Maximum 100 characters. City 2–50 characters. Letters, hyphens, and apostrophes only. State Select from the dropdown list of U.S. states. ZIP Code 5-digit or 5+4 format (e.g., 12345 or 12345-6789 ). Country Maximum 50 characters. 1.4 Sample Data If your account was created with sample data (demo clients, jobs, etc.), a Sample Data section appears at the bottom of the Account page. This is only visible when sample data exists. To delete all sample data: Click "Delete Sample Data" (red button). A confirmation modal appears with a warning about what will be deleted. Click "Delete Sample Data" in the modal to confirm. A progress bar tracks the deletion progress with a percentage indicator. Once complete, a success message appears and the section disappears. Warning: Deleting sample data is permanent and cannot be undone. All sample clients, jobs, estimates, invoices, and related records will be removed. Click "Save Account Info" to save your changes, or "Reset" to revert to the last saved values. 2. Profile Settings The Profile section manages your personal user information (not the company account). Click Profile in the Settings sidebar to access it. 2.1 Personal Information Field Required Description First Name Yes Your first name. 2–50 characters. Last Name Yes Your last name. 2–50 characters. Email Yes Your personal email address. Must be unique across the system. Phone Yes Your phone number. Supports international format. Country No Auto-detected from your phone number, or manually selectable. Timezone Yes Your personal timezone preference. Language Yes Choose between English and Spanish . This controls the language of the entire interface. Profile Picture No Upload a profile photo. Supports drag-and-drop. You can also remove an existing photo. If no photo is set, your initials are displayed instead. 2.2 Change Password To change your password: Enter your Current Password . Enter your New Password . Enter the new password again in Repeat Password to confirm. Click Save . Password Requirements: Minimum 10 characters . Must contain at least one lowercase letter. Must contain at least one uppercase letter. Must contain at least one digit . Must contain at least one special character ( !@#$%^&*(),.?":{}|<> ). Must be different from your current password. Each password field has a visibility toggle (eye icon) to show or hide the entered password. 2.3 Account Actions At the bottom of the Profile page, two important actions are available: Action Description Logout Signs you out of the platform. A confirmation dialog appears before logging out. Delete Account Permanently deletes your user account. A confirmation dialog with a warning appears before proceeding. This action cannot be undone. 3. Availability Settings The Availability section controls when your business is available for scheduling. This affects which dates and times are available when creating appointments, jobs, and estimates. Click Availability in the Settings sidebar to access it. 3.1 Weekly Schedule (Day/Time Slots) Set your availability for each day of the week: For each day (Monday through Sunday), you can define start time and end time . You can add multiple time slots per day (e.g., morning and afternoon shifts). Days without any time slots are considered unavailable. 3.2 Exception Dates Add specific date ranges when your business is not available (e.g., holidays, vacations): Click "Add Exception" to create a new exception. Set the Start Date and End Date for the unavailable period. Exception dates override your weekly schedule — even if a day is normally available, it will be blocked if it falls within an exception range. Each exception appears as a card that can be edited or deleted. 3.3 Seasonal / Monthly Exceptions For recurring unavailability patterns: Monthly Exceptions: Toggle individual months (January through December) as unavailable. Useful for businesses that don't operate during certain months. Seasonal Exceptions: Toggle entire seasons (Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall) as unavailable. Each exception appears as a card that can be removed. 4. Dashboard Settings The Dashboard section controls how information is displayed on your main dashboard. These are per-user settings (each user can have their own preferences). Click Dashboard in the Settings sidebar to access it. Dashboard Display Settings Setting Description Max List Length The maximum number of items shown in dashboard lists. Limits the number of records displayed for a cleaner view. Recommended: 10–20. Show Only New Toggle on to filter the dashboard to show only new/unread items. Toggle off to show all items. Upcoming Jobs Window How many days ahead to show upcoming jobs on the dashboard. For example, setting this to 7 shows jobs scheduled within the next 7 days. Recommended: 7–14 days. A Performance Settings info box shows your current settings alongside recommended values for optimal performance. Click "Save Dashboard Settings" to apply, or "Reset" to revert. 5. Notification Preferences The Notifications section controls how you receive notifications across different categories and channels. Click Notifications in the Settings sidebar to access it. The page displays a grid with notification categories as rows and delivery channels as columns. Each combination has a toggle switch. Delivery Channels Channel Description Email Notifications sent to your email address. SMS Text message notifications sent to your phone. Mobile (Push) Push notifications on your mobile device. Desktop In-app notifications visible in the platform's notification area. Notification Categories Category Description Authentication Login, password changes, and security-related notifications. Financial Payment received, payment failed, and other financial events. Jobs Job created, status changes, and job assignments. Estimates Estimate viewed, approved, or requiring attention. Appointments Appointment scheduled, confirmed, or status changes. Timesheets Timesheet submitted or approved. Inventory Low stock alerts and inventory changes. Team Team member changes and crew updates. Messages New client messages and chat notifications. Feedback Client feedback and review notifications. System Announcements Platform updates, maintenance, and system-wide announcements. Stripe Payment processing integration notifications. Billing Subscription and billing-related notifications. Note: The categories visible to you depend on your user role . Admins (SP_ADMIN) can see all categories. Some system-enforced categories cannot be customized and are always active. Click "Save Notification Settings" to apply, or "Reset" to revert to the last saved configuration. 6. Business Settings The Business section configures operational rules for your service provider account. Click Business in the Settings sidebar to access it. 6.1 Counter Settings Control the auto-numbering for estimates and invoices: Setting Description Estimate ID Counter The next number that will be assigned to a new estimate.
