Increase this if you want to start numbering from a specific point (e.g., set to 1001 to start estimates at #1001). Default: 1 . Invoice Number Counter The next number that will be assigned to a new invoice. Works the same as the estimate counter. Default: 1 . 6.2 Payment Disclaimer Settings Configure the payment disclaimer text shown on invoices and estimates sent to clients: Use Default Disclaimer (toggle): When ON : A standard default disclaimer is used. A preview of the default text is shown below. When OFF : A text area appears where you can write your own Custom Disclaimer Text . 6.3 Zelle Payment Details If you accept Zelle payments, you can display Zelle payment instructions to your clients: Show Zelle Payment Option (toggle): When ON : A text area appears where you enter your Zelle Payment Option Text (e.g., your Zelle email or phone number and instructions). Maximum 500 characters with a live character counter. When OFF : Zelle payment instructions are hidden from clients. 6.4 Crew Settings Visibility: This section is only visible to users with Admin , Owner , or Support roles. Allow Crew Time Log On Any Job (toggle): When ON : Crew members can log time on any job, regardless of whether they are assigned to it. When OFF : Crew members can only log time on jobs they are assigned to. An amber warning box appears explaining the restriction. 6.5 Privacy Settings Do Not Share Information (toggle): When ON : Your business information will not be shared with third parties. A blue info box confirms the privacy setting is active. When OFF : Standard information sharing is enabled. 6.6 Tax Rates (Revenue Section) Below the main business settings form, a separate Revenue section lets you manage tax rates. Tax rates are applied to services, products, estimates, invoices, and appointments. Click "Add Tax Rate" to create a new tax rate. A modal appears with fields for: Name — A label for the tax rate (e.g., "State Sales Tax", "County Tax"). Description — An optional description. Rate (%) — The percentage (e.g., 8.25 for 8.25%). Default — Mark one rate as the default that is automatically applied to new records. Existing tax rates are displayed in a list showing name, description, and rate percentage. Each tax rate can be edited or deleted . Group tax rates are supported (a tax rate that combines multiple individual rates). Click "Save Business Settings" to save the counter/disclaimer/Zelle/crew/privacy settings, or "Reset" to revert. Tax rates are saved independently through their own modals. 7. Timesheet Settings The Timesheet Settings section controls how timesheet entries are automatically linked to jobs and appointments. Click Timesheet Settings in the Settings sidebar to access it. 7.1 Account-Level Setting Visibility: This section is only visible to users with Admin or Owner roles. Enable Automatic Linking (toggle): When ON : When crew members start a timer on a job or appointment, the resulting timesheet entry is automatically linked to that record. When OFF : Timesheet entries are created without automatic linking. An amber warning box explains the implications. 7.2 Your Timesheet Preference (Per-User Override) Individual users can override the account-level setting for their own timesheet behavior: Disable Automatic Linking For Me (toggle): When ON : Automatic linking is disabled for your timesheet entries only, even if the account-level setting is enabled. When OFF (or not set): The account-level default applies. This toggle is disabled (grayed out) when the account-level automatic linking is already turned off, since there is nothing to override. 7.3 How Automatic Linking Works A "How It Works" section at the bottom explains the 3-step process: A crew member starts a timer on a job or appointment. When the timer stops, a timesheet entry is created. The entry is automatically linked to the source job/appointment. Click "Save Timesheet Settings" to apply changes. 8. Accept Payments (Stripe Connect) The Accept Payments section lets you set up credit card payment processing through Stripe Connect . This allows your clients to pay invoices online with a credit card. Click Accept Payments in the Settings sidebar to access it. [g] 8.1 Application Process Setting up Stripe Connect follows a multi-step process. The page displays different content depending on your current application status: Status What You See What to Do None An introduction card explaining the benefits of accepting payments. Click "Accept Payments" to express interest and start the process. Inquiry A pending review message. Your request has been submitted and is being processed. Wait for your request to be reviewed. The status will update automatically. Permission A form to provide your company details before applying to Stripe. Click "Apply" , then fill in the Company Information Form (see below) and click "Save Changes" . Applied Confirmation that your application has been submitted to Stripe. You can access the Stripe Dashboard to check your application status. Pending / Under Review Your application is being reviewed by Stripe. Wait for Stripe to complete their review. This typically takes 1–3 business days. Approved A success card confirming payment processing is active, with a list of features enabled. Click "Access Dashboard" to manage your Stripe account. You can now accept card payments, get automatic processing, real-time notifications, and detailed reports. Declined A message indicating additional verification is needed. Shows the decline reason and reference number if available. Review the decline reason. Contact Stripe support with your reference number for assistance. Click "Go to Dashboard" for more details. 8.2 Company Information Form When applying (Permission status), you must provide: Field Required Description Company Name Yes Your legal business name (pre-filled from your Account settings). Business Type Yes Select your entity type: Individual , Company , Non-Profit , or Government Entity . Phone Number Yes Your business phone number. Automatically formatted as +1 (XXX) XXX-XXXX . After submitting the form, you are redirected to Stripe's onboarding page to complete the verification process. 9. Navigating Between Settings Sections Use the sidebar navigation on the left to switch between sections. Each section has: An icon identifying the section type. A title (e.g., "Account", "Business"). A brief description of what the section manages. The currently active section is highlighted in the sidebar. Each section has its own URL (e.g., /settings-business , /settings-notifications ), so you can bookmark specific settings pages. The content header always shows: The main title "Settings" with the subtitle "Manage service provider settings". Below that, the current section title and its description. 10. Role-Based Access Not all settings are visible to all users. Access depends on your user role: Setting Who Can See / Edit Account settings (company info, billing) All users (typically managed by Admins/Owners). Profile settings (personal info, password) All users (each user manages their own profile). Availability settings All users. Dashboard settings All users (per-user preferences). Notification preferences All users (categories vary by role; Admins see all). Business settings — Crew Settings subsection Admin, Owner, Support roles only. Timesheet settings — Account-Level toggle Admin, Owner roles only. Timesheet settings — Personal override All users. Accept Payments (Stripe Connect) All users (typically managed by Admins/Owners). 11. Summary of Common Actions Action How to Do It Update company name or email Settings → Account → edit Company Information fields → click "Save Account Info" . Change billing address Settings → Account → scroll to Billing Information → edit fields → click "Save Account Info" . Delete sample data Settings → Account → scroll to Sample Data section → click "Delete Sample Data" → confirm. Update your name, email, or phone Settings → Profile → edit Personal Information fields → click Save . Change your password Settings → Profile → Change Password section → enter current + new + repeat → click Save . Upload a profile picture Settings → Profile → click the upload area or drag and drop an image. Change interface language Settings → Profile → select English or Spanish from the Language dropdown → click Save . Set weekly availability schedule Settings → Availability → configure start/end times for each day → click Save . Add a holiday / unavailable date Settings → Availability → click "Add Exception" → set start/end dates → click Save . Configure dashboard display Settings → Dashboard → adjust Max List Length, Show Only New, and Upcoming Jobs Window → click "Save Dashboard Settings" . Turn off SMS notifications for jobs Settings → Notifications → find the Jobs row → toggle off the SMS switch → click "Save Notification Settings" . Set estimate/invoice numbering Settings → Business → Counter Settings → set the desired counter values → click "Save Business Settings" . Write a custom payment disclaimer Settings → Business → toggle off "Use Default Disclaimer" → type your custom text → click "Save Business Settings" . Enable Zelle payment instructions Settings → Business → toggle on "Show Zelle Payment Option" → enter your Zelle details → click "Save Business Settings" . Add a new tax rate Settings → Business → scroll to Revenue section → click "Add Tax Rate" → fill in the modal → save. Restrict crew time logging Settings → Business → toggle off "Allow Crew Time Log On Any Job" → click "Save Business Settings" . (Admin/Owner/Support only.) Enable/disable automatic timesheet linking Settings → Timesheet Settings → toggle "Enable Automatic Linking" → click "Save Timesheet Settings" . (Admin/Owner only.) Override timesheet linking for yourself Settings → Timesheet Settings → toggle "Disable Automatic Linking For Me" → click "Save Timesheet Settings" . Set up credit card payments Settings → Accept Payments → follow the Stripe Connect application process (see section 8). Access your Stripe dashboard Settings → Accept Payments → click "Access Dashboard" (only available after approval). Log out of the platform Settings → Profile → click "Logout" → confirm.
checklists-management: Checklists Management [h] [i] Overview The Checklists Management section is where you create and manage reusable checklist templates for your business. Checklists help ensure that jobs are completed thoroughly by defining a set of items that must be reviewed or verified before a job can be marked as complete. Checklists work as templates — you create a checklist once, and then assign it to as many jobs or estimates as needed. Each job gets its own copy of the checklist, so changes made during a job do not affect the original template. From this section you can: Create new checklist templates with a title, description, and a set of items to verify. Edit existing checklists to add, remove, or reorder items. Duplicate an existing checklist to use it as a starting point for a new one. Archive checklists you no longer need (without losing historical data). Restore archived checklists if you need them again. Permanently delete archived checklists that are no longer needed. Search to quickly find a specific checklist. Important: A checklist can only be permanently deleted if it is not currently assigned to any job. If a checklist is in use, you must first remove it from all associated jobs before deletion is possible. Checklists List View The Checklists List View is the main view for managing all your checklist templates. To access it, navigate to Menu > Checklists . When you open the Checklists List View, you will see a table listing all your active checklists. Each row represents one checklist and displays the following information: Column What It Means Date Created The date the checklist was originally created (e.g., "Jan 1, 2026"). Title The name of the checklist, along with its description (if one was provided). The description is shown as smaller text below the name. Options Quick-action buttons to edit, duplicate, or archive/delete the checklist. Clicking anywhere on a row will open that checklist for viewing and editing its items. Active vs. Archived Checklists At the top of the list you will see two tabs: Active — Shows all checklists that are currently available for use. This is the default view. Active checklists can be assigned to new jobs and estimates. Archived — Shows checklists that have been archived (temporarily disabled). Archived checklists do not appear in the dropdown when creating or editing jobs, but any jobs that already have them assigned will continue to work normally. Sorting the List You can sort the checklists by clicking on the column headers for Date Created or Title . Click once to sort in ascending order, and click again to sort in descending order. Searching Use the search bar to the right of the tabs to find checklists by name or description. Type your search term and the results will update automatically. 1. Creating a New Checklist To create a new checklist template, follow these steps: Step 1: Open the New Checklist Form Click the "New Checklist" button (blue) located in the top-right corner of the Checklists List View. A panel will slide open from the right side of the screen. Tip: If the list is empty (no checklists have been created yet), you will see a "Create Your First Checklist" button in the center of the page. Step 2: Enter the Checklist Details Field Required? Description Checklist Title Yes Enter a clear, descriptive name for the checklist (e.g., "HVAC Installation Final Inspection" or "Plumbing Rough-In Checklist"). Maximum 512 characters. Description No Optionally, provide additional context about when or how this checklist should be used. Maximum 512 characters. Step 3: Add Checklist Items Below the title and description, you will see the Checklist Items section. This is where you define the individual tasks or verification points that make up the checklist. To add an item: Type the item title in the text field (e.g., "Verify all connections are sealed"). Press Enter or click the checkmark button to add the item. Repeat for each item you want to include. For each item, you can also check the "Description Required" checkbox. When this is checked, the person completing the job will be required to write a description for that item before the job can be marked as complete. This is useful for items that need documented evidence or notes (e.g., "Record the serial number of the installed unit"). After adding items, they will appear in a list below. You can: Edit an item by clicking the pencil icon next to it. Remove an item by clicking the trash icon next to it. Note: You must add at least one item before you can save the checklist. The "Add New Checklist" button will remain disabled until at least one item has been added. Step 4: Save the Checklist Once you have entered the title and added your items, click the "Add New Checklist" button at the bottom of the panel. You will see a brief "Creating Checklist..." overlay, and once the checklist is created successfully, a confirmation message will appear: "Checklist Successfully Created" . The new checklist will now appear in your Checklists List View and be available for assignment to jobs and estimates. 2. Viewing and Managing Checklist Items To view or edit the items in an existing checklist, click on any checklist row in the Checklists List View. This will open the Checklist Detail page. What You See The detail page shows: The checklist name at the top, with a pencil icon to edit the name and description. A status badge showing whether the checklist is "Active" or "Archived". A "New Item" button to add more items. A table of all checklist items with the following columns: Column What It Means Drag Handle Grip dots on the left side of each row. Click and drag to reorder items (see below). Title The name/description of the checklist item. Description Required A toggle indicator. Blue (filled) means a description is required when completing this item. Gray (hollow) means it is optional. You can click this toggle to change the setting directly from the table. Edit Pencil icon to open the item for editing. Delete Trash icon to remove the item from the checklist. Adding a New Item Click the "New Item" button at the top of the detail page. A dialog will appear with two fields: Title (required) — Enter the item name. Maximum 512 characters. Description Required (optional) — Check this box if a description must be provided when completing this item. Click "Add Item" to save, or "Cancel" to go back. A success message will appear: "Checklist Entry Successfully Created" . Editing an Item Click the pencil icon next to the item you want to edit. A dialog will appear pre-filled with the item's current title and description required setting. Make your changes and click "Apply Changes" , or click "Cancel" to discard. A success message will appear: "Checklist Entry Successfully Updated" . Reordering Items (Drag and Drop) You can change the order in which items appear by dragging and dropping them: Click and hold the drag handle (grip dots) on the left side of the item row. Drag the item up or down to the desired position. Release to drop it in place. The new order is saved automatically. The items will appear in this order wherever the checklist is used (in jobs and when completing jobs). Deleting an Item Click the trash icon next to the item you want to remove. A confirmation dialog will appear: "Are you sure you want to delete [Item Title]?" Click "Delete" to confirm, or "Cancel" to go back. A success message will appear: "Checklist Entry Successfully Deleted" . Note: You cannot delete the last item in a checklist. Every checklist must have at least one item. If you try to delete the last remaining item, the delete button will be disabled with a tooltip: "Cannot delete last item." 3. Editing a Checklist's Name and Description To change the name or description of a checklist (not its items): Open the checklist detail page by clicking on it in the Checklists List View. Click the pencil icon next to the checklist name at the top of the page. A panel will slide open with two editable fields: Checklist Title (required) — Maximum 512 characters. Description (optional) — Maximum 512 characters. Make your changes and click "Apply Changes" . A success message will appear: "Checklist Successfully Updated" . Tip: You can also access the edit panel by clicking the pencil icon in the Actions column on the Checklists List View. 4. Duplicating a Checklist If you want to create a new checklist that is similar to an existing one, you can duplicate it instead of starting from scratch: On the Checklists List View (Active tab), find the checklist you want to duplicate. Click the duplicate icon (copy icon) in the Options column. A confirmation dialog will appear: "Are you sure you want to duplicate [Checklist Name]?" Click "Duplicate" to confirm. A success message will appear: "Checklist Successfully Duplicated" . The duplicated checklist will appear in the list as a new, independent copy. You can then edit its name, description, and items without affecting the original. 5. Archiving Checklists Archiving is a safe way to disable a checklist without permanently deleting it. Archived checklists: Do not appear in the dropdown when creating or editing jobs and estimates. Are preserved in historical records — any existing jobs that have this checklist assigned will continue to work normally. Can be restored at any time if you need the checklist again. How to Archive a Checklist On the Checklists List View (Active tab), locate the checklist you want to archive. Click the archive icon in the Options column. A confirmation dialog will appear: "Are you sure you want to archive [Checklist Name]?" Click "Archive" to confirm, or "Cancel" to go back. You will see an "Updating Checklist..." overlay, followed by a success message: "Checklist Successfully Archived" . The checklist will be removed from the Active tab and moved to the Archived tab. 5.1 Restoring an Archived Checklist If you need to bring an archived checklist back into active use: Switch to the Archived tab on the Checklists List View. Find the checklist you want to restore. Click the restore icon (undo icon) in the Options column. A confirmation popover will appear: "Do you want to restore this checklist?" Click "Restore" to confirm. A success message will appear: "Checklist Successfully Restored" . The checklist will be moved back to the Active tab and will once again appear in the dropdown when creating or editing jobs. 5.2 Permanently Deleting a Checklist Permanent deletion is only available for archived checklists and only when the checklist is not in use (not assigned to any job). Switch to the Archived tab on the Checklists List View. Find the checklist you want to permanently delete. Click the delete icon (trash icon) in the Options column. If the delete icon is grayed out (disabled), it means the checklist is still assigned to at least one job. You will see a tooltip: "Checklist cannot be deleted while in use." A confirmation dialog will appear: "Are you sure you want to delete [Checklist Name]?" Click "Delete" to confirm, or "Cancel" to go back. You will see a "Deleting Checklist..." overlay, followed by a success message: "Checklist Successfully Deleted" . Warning: Permanent deletion cannot be undone. Once a checklist is deleted, it is removed from the system permanently. If you are unsure, keep the checklist archived instead. 6. How Checklists Are Used Across the Platform Checklists are primarily used in Jobs and Estimates . When you assign a checklist to a job, the job gets its own independent copy of the checklist — so any changes made to items during the job (adding entries, filling in descriptions) do not affect the original template. Assigning a Checklist to a Job When creating or editing a job, you will see a "Checklist (Optional)" dropdown in the Job Details step. This dropdown lists all your active checklists (archived checklists are not shown). Select a checklist to assign it to the job. Once a checklist is assigned to a job: A "Checklist" tab appears on the job detail page. The Checklist tab shows all the items from the template, displayed in order. You can expand each item by clicking the chevron to enter a description or notes for that item. If an item has "Description Required" enabled, it will be marked with an asterisk (*), and a description must be provided before the job can be completed. You can also add new items directly to the job's checklist — these additions only affect the job, not the original template. You can delete items from the job's checklist — again, this only affects the job copy. Completing a Job with a Checklist When you attempt to mark a job as complete, and the job has a checklist assigned: A completion modal will appear showing all checklist items with checkboxes. You must check every item to confirm it has been completed or verified. For items marked as "Description Required", a description text field will appear when you check the item. You must fill in the description before proceeding. The "Complete Job" button will remain disabled until all items are checked and all required descriptions are filled in. This ensures that no job is marked as complete without first verifying all items on the checklist. Note: If a job does not have a checklist assigned, the completion modal will simply ask: "Are you sure you want to complete this job?" without any checklist review. Assigning a Checklist to an Estimate Similar to jobs, you can assign a checklist to an estimate using the "Checklist (Optional)" dropdown on the Estimate Details step. When the estimate is later converted to a job, the checklist assignment carries over. Understanding Checklist Items Each item in a checklist is simple by design. An item has just two properties: Property What It Means Title A short description of what needs to be checked or verified (e.g., "Test water pressure at all fixtures"). Maximum 512 characters. Description Required When enabled, the person completing the job must write a description or notes for this item (e.g., "Record the pressure reading in PSI"). When disabled, the item only needs to be checked off. This simplicity makes checklists easy to create and fast to complete in the field, while the "Description Required" option provides accountability for items that need documented verification. Summary of Common Actions What You Want to Do How to Do It Create a new checklist Click "New Checklist" on the Checklists List View View/edit checklist items Click on any checklist row to open its detail page Edit checklist name/description Click the pencil icon next to the checklist name Add a new item Click "New Item" on the checklist detail page Reorder items Drag and drop using the grip dots on the left side of each item Toggle description required Click the toggle indicator in the Description Required column Duplicate a checklist Click the duplicate icon on the Active tab Archive a checklist Click the archive icon on the Active tab Restore a checklist Click the restore icon on the Archived tab Permanently delete a checklist Click the delete icon on the Archived tab (only if not in use) Search for a checklist Type in the search bar at the top Assign a checklist to a job Select it from the "Checklist (Optional)" dropdown when creating/editing a job [a] Unable to find option or image for this section [b] Please skip this section, this is actually in progress in the sp-board [c] Not Visible [d] Not found this option [e] Work in progress in a local branch [f] This implementation is now available on develop branch [g] Showing Pending review @jhosselin@stwitechnologies.com [h] Not found in setting @jhosselin@stwitechnologies.com [i] This is a standalone feature, not part of the settings.
whatsapp-ai: WhatsApp AI Assistant Overview The WhatsApp AI Assistant is a conversational AI built right into WhatsApp that lets you manage your entire DHS business from your phone — using simple text messages or voice memos. Instead of switching between apps, you can search for clients, create appointments, send invoices, check your schedule, and much more, all from a WhatsApp chat. With the WhatsApp AI Assistant you can: Search and look up clients, jobs, estimates, invoices, crews, and more Create new records like appointments, clients, jobs, estimates, and invoices Update and manage existing records — change statuses, reschedule, edit fields Track time — clock in, clock out, and review timesheets Communicate — send messages and notifications to your team or clients Get reports — daily briefings, schedule summaries, and business reports Use utilities — check the weather, get directions, run quick calculations The assistant supports over 103 tools and works in both English and Spanish . You can type your requests in natural, everyday language — no special commands to memorize. Getting Started Linking Your WhatsApp Number Before you can use the WhatsApp AI Assistant, you need to link your WhatsApp phone number to your DHS account. Here is how: Open the DHS app on your phone or desktop. Go to Settings and select WhatsApp Integration . Enter the phone number associated with your WhatsApp account. Tap Link Number . You will receive a verification code via WhatsApp. Enter the verification code in the DHS app to confirm the link. Once verified, you can start messaging the DHS AI Assistant directly on WhatsApp. Authentication and Security Your WhatsApp AI session is protected by multiple layers of security: Twilio Verification — Your phone number is verified through Twilio to confirm your identity. Session Lock — After a period of inactivity, your session is automatically locked. To unlock it, you will receive a verification email with a secure link. Email Verification — When your session locks, simply check your email, click the verification link, and your WhatsApp session will unlock automatically. If someone else tries to message the assistant from an unlinked number, they will not be able to access any account data. What You Can Do The WhatsApp AI Assistant gives you access to over 103 tools organized into the categories below. You do not need to memorize tool names — just describe what you need in plain language and the assistant will figure out the right action. The AI Assistant is available on both the web app and the mobile app (iOS and Android). It uses the same powerful AI engine as the DHS WhatsApp integration, but is accessed through a dedicated chat interface within the app itself. Where to Find It Web App — Look for the AI Assistant icon in the main navigation or sidebar. Mobile App — Tap the AI Assistant button on the bottom navigation bar or home screen. appointment-line: DHS IVR Appointment Line Overview The DHS Appointment Line is an AI-powered phone system that allows callers to find service providers, schedule appointments, manage existing bookings, and request callbacks — all by voice or keypad input. The system is fully bilingual, supporting both English and Spanish. Phone Number: +1 (855) 244-4981 With the Appointment Line, callers can: Search for service providers by name, extension code, or service type and location Schedule new appointments with available providers Check, change, or cancel upcoming appointments Request a callback from a service provider Get a company's direct phone number How to Call Step 1: Dial the Number Call +1 (855) 244-4981 from any phone. The system will greet you and offer a language selection. Step 2: Select Your Language English: Press 1 or simply wait — the system defaults to English. Spanish: Press 2 to continue in Spanish. Step 3: Connect to a Provider You can reach a service provider in several ways: Automatic recognition — If you are a returning client, the system recognizes your phone number. Extension code — Enter the 5-digit code your provider gave you. Say the company name — Speak the name of the business you want to reach. Press 9 — Search for a provider by service type and location. Connecting to a Service Provider Returning Clients If you have called before or have an account with a service provider, the system may recognize your phone number automatically: The system checks your caller ID against its records. If you have an upcoming appointment , it will ask if you are calling about that appointment. If your number is linked to multiple providers , the system will list them so you can choose which one you want to reach. Extension Code Your service provider may give you a 5-digit extension code (a number between 10000 and 99999). To use it: When prompted, enter the 5-digit code on your keypad. Press # (pound) to submit. The system will connect you directly to that provider's appointment line. Extension codes are the fastest way to reach a specific provider on the shared line. Say the Company Name If you know the name of the company you want to reach, simply speak it when prompted. The system uses AI to match your spoken words to registered business names and connect you. Search by Service and Location (Press 9) If you do not have a specific provider in mind, press 9 to search by the type of service you need: Describe the service: Say what you need, such as "plumbing," "house cleaning," or "HVAC repair." Location detection: The system automatically detects your general location from your phone number's area code. Adjust location (optional): Say a different city and state, or a zip code, to search in another area. Press 2 to change location during a search. Browse results: Providers are listed sorted by distance, and include customer ratings when available. Press 0 to hear the next page of results. Select a provider: Press the number corresponding to the provider you want (1 through 8). Tip: Say "any" to search across all service types in your area. Over 30 service categories are supported in both English and Spanish. What You Can Do Once connected to a service provider, the AI assistant can help you with any of the following: Action Description Schedule a new appointment Book a service visit by choosing an available date and time. The system will walk you through selecting a service, date, and time slot. Check upcoming appointments Hear the details of your next scheduled appointment, including date, time, and service type. Change an appointment Reschedule an existing appointment to a different date or time. Cancel an appointment Cancel a scheduled appointment. The system will ask you to confirm before finalizing the cancellation. Request a callback Ask the provider to call you back at a convenient time. Get the company phone number Press 8 at any time to hear the provider's direct phone number. Keypad Commands You can use the following keypad shortcuts at any time during your call: Key Action When to Use # (pound) Submit input After entering an extension code or other numeric input 0 Start over / Next page Return to the beginning of the call, or see the next page of search results 1 Select English / Select first item Language selection, or choosing the first option from a list 2 Select Spanish / Change location Language selection, or changing your search location during provider search 1–8 Select from a list Choose a provider or option from the current list of results 8 Hear company phone number Once connected to a provider, hear their direct phone number 9 Search for a provider Start a service and location search to find available providers Bilingual Support The Appointment Line provides full bilingual support in English and Spanish. This includes: All voice prompts are available in both languages. AI conversation understands and responds in your selected language. Service categories are recognized in both English and Spanish
